ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("10s UI-render watchdog can fire spuriously if the JCEF bridge takes longer than 10s to render (rare but possible on slow machines / cold start) ŌĆö the LLM gets [UI_RENDER_FAILED] and is told to ask the question as plain text, even though the user might still be looking at a wizard that was about to render.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("5-minute suspension grace; if the user goes idle the loop sits idle for 5 minutes before timing out. No interim progress signal to the user; the chat just shows the unanswered question.")
ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("A clear, specific question to ask the user. This is the simple mode ŌĆö the question appears in the chat and the user types their answer.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Asks a trivial question whose answer is already in the conversation context, project files, or current editor ŌĆö wastes a 5-minute-suspendable round-trip when read_file / search_code would have answered it. The 'use judiciously' line in the description targets this but the LLM still over-asks, especially after compaction trims the relevant context.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Both modes use a shared `pendingQuestions: CompletableDeferred<String>` companion-object field as the suspension primitive. Concurrent callers would clobber each other (last-writer-wins) ŌĆö fine in practice because the agent loop is single-threaded, but worth flagging if the loop ever parallelizes user-input tools.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Calls ask_followup_question after the user has already volunteered the information (e.g. they wrote 'use Postgres' in the prompt) ŌĆö re-asking burns the user's patience and the suspension grace period. Trust user-stated facts; don't re-ask.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Cancellation produces a different result shape than skip: cancelled returns isError=false but a 'User dismissed' content; skip returns a `<skipped>` block instructing the LLM to use best judgement. The LLM has to handle three response shapes (answered, skipped, cancelled) plus two error shapes (timeout, UI render failed) ŌĆö five terminal states for one tool.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Clickable choice chips for the simple-mode question ŌĆö turns 'type your answer' into 'click one of these'. Best for closed-set decisions (Yes/No, A/B/C) where free-text would be slower.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Error: Duplicate question ID '${q.id}'", "Error: duplicate ID", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Forgets that simple mode's `options` is a JSON-encoded string array, not a JSON array literal ŌĆö passes `[Option A, Option B]` (unquoted) and the parse fails, falling back to plain-text question. Must be `[\"Option A\", \"Option B\"]`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Header text for the wizard panel ŌĆö only renders in wizard mode. Like the title bar on a setup-wizard dialog.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("JSON array of structured questions for the wizard mode. Use when you have 2+ related decisions to gather at once, OR a single question where the user may pick more than one option (type:\"multiple\" renders checkboxes; type:\"single\" renders radios). Each question may carry an optional short `header` (Ōēż12 chars) shown as a scannable tag. Example: [{\"id\":\"db\",\"header\":\"Database\",\"question\":\"Which database?\",\"type\":\"single\",\"options\":[{\"id\":\"pg\",\"label\":\"Postgres\"},{\"id\":\"my\",\"label\":\"MySQL\"}]},{\"id\":\"feat\",\"header\":\"Features\",\"question\":\"Which features to enable?\",\"type\":\"multiple\",\"options\":[{\"id\":\"auth\",\"label\":\"Auth\"},{\"id\":\"cache\",\"label\":\"Caching\"}]}]")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Like a chatbot pausing to ask 'before I proceed, can you clarify X?' ŌĆö the agent puts a question in the chat (or a multi-step wizard in the side panel), then waits at the door until you answer, skip, or close it. After 5 minutes of silence the agent gives up and reports a timeout instead of hanging forever.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Optional JSON array of 2-5 string options for the user to choose from, e.g. [\"Option 1\", \"Option 2\"]. Saves the user from typing. Only used with the 'question' parameter (simple mode).")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Parsed via kotlinx.serialization as List<String>. On parse failure, options are dropped and a parseWarning is prepended to the eventual answer content (the question still renders, just without chips). Re-serialized via JsEscape.toJsonString before being passed to the JCEF bridge.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Phrases the question open-endedly ('What should I do next?') instead of presenting concrete options ŌĆö the user has to type a free-form answer when chips would have closed the loop in one click. When you can enumerate the choices, do.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Provides neither `question` nor `questions` ŌĆö both are validated as required-one-of in execute(); returns an error ToolResult and the iteration is wasted.")
ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Required-one-of with `question`. If neither is provided, execute() returns an error ToolResult.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Required-one-of with `questions`. If neither is provided, execute() returns an error ToolResult; if `questions` is provided instead, wizard mode runs.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Routes to executeSimple(): question is shown via showSimpleQuestionCallback (chat-based) or wrapped as a single-question wizard fallback. The agent loop suspends on a CompletableDeferred until the user answers, skips, dismisses, or the 5-min timeout fires. Returned content wraps both the question and the answer in `<question>` / `<answer>` tags so context survives compaction.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Routes to executeWizard(): JSON is validated (max 20 questions, max 10 options each, unique IDs, type Ōłł {single, multiple}). On validation failure returns an error ToolResult with a format example. On success, fires showQuestionsCallback to render the wizard; the agent loop suspends on a CompletableDeferred until the user submits, cancels, or the 5-min timeout fires.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Simple and wizard modes share the same scaffolding (CompletableDeferred + uiRenderConfirmed + 10s watchdog Timer + 5-min withTimeoutOrNull + cancelled/[UI_RENDER_FAILED]/[SKIPPED] sentinel handling). The two execute*() methods differ only in (a) which callback fires (showSimpleQuestionCallback vs showQuestionsCallback) and (b) the result-content shape. A unified executeBlocking(payload, callback, summaryShape) helper would cut ~60 lines of duplication and pin the watchdog/timeout invariants in one place.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Simple mode already falls back to wizard mode (wraps the question as a single-question wizard) when showSimpleQuestionCallback is null. This means wizard mode's UI surface is the universal renderer; simple mode is a UI-affinity hint, not a separate code path at the dashboard layer. Could collapse to a single mode with an `is_wizard` boolean flag and let the UI choose the render strategy.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Simple-mode `options` parsing failures are silent at the LLM-facing level ŌĆö the question still renders, but without chips, and the LLM has no way to know the parse failed until the answer comes back with the parseWarning prepended. The warning surfaces in content, not in the tool response shape.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Stuffs multiple unrelated questions into a single `question` string ('What database, what auth scheme, and what deployment target?') ŌĆö the user has to type a multi-part answer in plain text instead of getting clickable options for each. Either ask one question at a time (simple mode) or use wizard mode with structured questions.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("The 10s UI-render watchdog uses `java.util.Timer` (daemon thread) rather than a coroutine, with the comment explaining structured-concurrency reasons (a child coroutine outliving withTimeoutOrNull would leak). Pattern is correct but uncommon in the codebase; worth keeping the rationale in the source.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("The simple-mode question ŌĆö one sentence, shown inline in the chat. Pair with `options` to give the user clickable choices instead of a typing prompt.")
ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("The user chose to skip this question. Proceed using your best judgement, or ask a different question if you still need clarification.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Two-mode user-input primitive: simple mode shows a chat question with optional clickable choice chips; wizard mode renders a multi-step JCEF dashboard wizard with single/multi-select options, back/skip/next nav, and a summary page. Both modes complete a CompletableDeferred that suspends the agent loop until the user answers, dismisses, or the 5-minute timeout fires; a 10s UI-render watchdog auto-resolves with [UI_RENDER_FAILED] when the JCEF bridge never confirms the render.")
ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("User sees the question with a typing prompt only ŌĆö no clickable chips. Fine for open-ended questions.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Uses wizard mode (`questions` array) for a single yes/no decision ŌĆö the heavyweight multi-step UI for one click is overkill; should use simple mode with `options=[\"Yes\", \"No\"]`. Wizard mode is for genuine multi-step decision trees (pick database ŌåÆ pick schema ŌåÆ pick migration strategy).")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Wizard mode validation rejects on first error with a JSON format example ŌĆö useful for the LLM to retry, but a malformed wizard means a wasted iteration. Simple mode is more forgiving (silently drops bad options).")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("Wizard-mode payload ŌĆö a JSON array of structured questions, each with its own options list and an optional short `header` tag. Renders as a multi-step wizard in the dashboard with back/skip/next navigation. Use for 2+ related decisions or a single multi-select question; for one single-choice question, simple mode is lighter.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("`pendingQuestions` is a single-slot @Volatile field. If the LLM emits two ask_followup_question calls back-to-back (e.g. recovering from a malformed first call), the second overwrites the first deferred and the first user-input round-trip is lost. Defensible because the agent loop serializes tool calls; brittle if that invariant changes.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("`uiRenderConfirmed` is set by an out-of-band JCEF bridge round-trip; tests can't easily exercise the watchdog branch without a live JCEF instance. The watchdog branch is therefore lightly tested in integration.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("ask_user_input", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Deferred-tier sibling ŌĆö exists in the same problem space (gather input from user). Worth comparing when auditing input-gathering tool surface area.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("attempt_completion", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Contrast: attempt_completion ends the session with a finished result; ask_followup_question pauses mid-task to gather more input. Calling attempt_completion when you should be asking a question loses the user's chance to course-correct before the wrong work lands.")
ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("description suggests 2-5 options, but no enforcement ŌĆö the LLM can pass 1 or 50; UI ergonomics suffer outside the 2-5 band")
ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("each question's `type` must be `single` or `multiple` ŌĆö `multiple` lets the user select more than one option")
ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("must be a JSON-encoded string array (not a JSON array literal); malformed JSON is silently dropped with a warning prepended to the answer")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("mutually exclusive with `questions` in practice ŌĆö if both are sent, simple mode wins (the early check on `simpleQuestion != null` runs first)")

ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("plan_mode_respond", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Contrast: plan_mode_respond proposes a multi-step plan and waits for user feedback or approval; ask_followup_question gathers a single piece of clarifying info needed to proceed. Both suspend the loop on a CompletableDeferred but differ in payload shape (plan vs question) and rendered surface (plan card vs chat question / wizard).")
ArgumentListWrapping:AskQuestionsTool.kt$AskQuestionsTool$("question IDs must be unique within the array; option IDs must be unique within their question")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("A copy-pasteable command, URL, or one-line instruction telling the user how to confirm the work landed correctly. The 'how do I check this?' answer pre-baked.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("A guess at what the user will type next ŌĆö shown as faded autocomplete in the chat input box, accepted with Right Arrow. ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Batch-stripped: if the LLM emits attempt_completion alongside other tool calls in the same turn, AgentLoop's batch guard removes it and nudges the model to re-issue after observing the other results. This is intentional but can confuse the LLM if the prompt didn't warn it.")
ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Becomes the card's primary text and is truncated to the first 200 chars in the cross-session sessions index summary.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Calls attempt_completion in the same turn as other tool calls (e.g. read_file + attempt_completion). The summary would be a guess based on tools whose results haven't been observed yet ŌĆö AgentLoop's batch guard strips the completion from the multi-tool batch and nudges the LLM to re-issue it on its own next turn after seeing the results.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Calls attempt_completion mid-task before the user's actual request is satisfied ŌĆö typically after partially exploring code or finishing a sub-step. There is no automatic guard for this; only LoopDetector's repeat-call detection catches it indirectly.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Card renders without a verification CTA. Acceptable for purely-internal refactors where the LLM has already proven correctness via tests.")
ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Classification of this completion. Must be 'done', 'review', or 'heads_up'. See tool description for criteria.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Despite the name, this tool does not 'attempt' anything ŌĆö it terminates the loop unconditionally. The legacy name is a Cline carry-over; renaming would break every persona prompt that mentions it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("How the agent says 'I'm done' to you. Like the chef tapping the bell at a restaurant ŌĆö the meal stops cooking, the plate goes out, and a card lands in chat saying what was made and whether you should taste-test it before paying.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Maps to the CompletionKind enum (DONE / REVIEW / HEADS_UP) and tags the rendered CompletionCard. The card UI changes accent colour and CTA wording per kind.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Optional. A short, plausible next message the user is likely to send (e.g. \"run the tests\", \"open the failing log\", \"commit this\"). Rendered as faded ghost-text in the chat input; the user accepts it with Right Arrow. Keep it under ~12 words and phrase it as the user would (imperative, first-person). Omit if no next step is obvious.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Optional: a CLI command, URL, or instruction the user can follow to verify the result. Valid on all kinds. When kind=review, this is the primary CTA and should clearly describe what to check.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Orchestrator-only completion signal. Returns a Completion-typed ToolResult that drives loop termination via ToolResultType.Completion and renders a CompletionCard in the chat with a kind-tagged result, optional verify-how CTA, optional surprising discovery, and optional ghost-text next-step suggestion.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Orchestrator-only ŌĆö `allowedWorkers = {ORCHESTRATOR}` means sub-agents calling this get a worker-type rejection at schema time. Sub-agents must use task_report. Easy to forget when porting persona prompts.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Picks `kind=done` when the work needs human verification (browser check, deploy validation, schema review). The user gets a 'no action needed' card and only later realises something's wrong. Correct choice: `kind=review` with a concrete verify_how.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Picks `kind=heads_up` and forgets to set `discovery`, or sets a blank string ŌĆö execute() rejects with 'heads_up requires a non-empty discovery' and the LLM has to retry. Mitigation lives in the description but the omission is a recurring pattern.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Pre-fills the chat input with faded ghost-text on the next prompt. User taps Right Arrow to accept; any other typing dismisses it. Blank values are stripped so an empty `next_step: \"\"` does not pollute the input.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Rendered as a CTA / verify-button on the CompletionCard. For kind=review it is the primary action; for kind=done/heads_up it appears as secondary detail.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Required for kind=heads_up (otherwise the call fails with 'heads_up requires a non-empty discovery'). Optional and typically omitted for kind=done and kind=review.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Required when kind=heads_up ŌĆö execute() rejects a heads_up call without it. Rendered as a distinct discovery callout on the card so it doesn't get lost in the result line.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Required when kind=heads_up: the surprising finding, hidden risk, or scope gap the user needs to know about. Must be omitted or null for done and review.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Short summary card shown to the user. Must be concise ŌĆö your detailed explanation should be in the streamed text BEFORE this tool call, not here.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("Stuffs the full detailed explanation into `result` instead of streaming the explanation as text BEFORE the tool call. The CompletionCard truncates the summary at 200 chars in the cross-session log, so verbose results get clipped where the user reads them later.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("The 'oh and by the wayŌĆ”' note ŌĆö something the user didn't ask about but ought to know before moving on. Hidden risk, scope gap, dead code stumbled on, etc.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("The one-or-two-sentence headline shown on the completion card. Anything longer belongs in the streaming chat above the card ŌĆö the long explanation the model just wrote, not duplicated here.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("What flavour of completion this is ŌĆö like the difference between 'I shipped it' (done), 'please double-check before I ship it' (review), and 'shipped, but watch out for this' (heads_up). Drives the icon, colour, and call-to-action on the completion card the user sees.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("`kind=heads_up` enforces non-empty discovery only at execute() time ŌĆö schema-level enforcement is impossible because JSON Schema can't express 'discovery required iff kind==heads_up'. The LLM occasionally trips on this and burns a retry.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("`next_step` is unique among completion params in that it leaks state INTO the next session (pre-fills the chat input). All other fields are read-only artefacts of the just-finished task.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("`result` is shown verbatim as the card headline AND truncated to 200 chars for the global sessions index ŌĆö verbose multi-paragraph results get clipped where the user reads them between sessions. Streaming the long-form text BEFORE the tool call is the workaround, but the LLM doesn't always do it.")
ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("aim for under ~12 words; phrase as imperative first-person ('run the tests'), not third-person ('the user could run the tests')")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("ask_followup_question", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use instead when blocked and user input can unblock the work. attempt_completion ends the task; ask_followup_question pauses it pending an answer.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("must be exactly one of: done, review, heads_up ŌĆö case-sensitive; any other value returns 'Invalid value for kind' before any persistence")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("plan_mode_respond", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Plan-mode equivalent: presents a plan and suspends the loop pending Approve. Different UI, different exit semantics ŌĆö plan_mode_respond doesn't terminate the session, just gates act-mode entry.")

ArgumentListWrapping:AttemptCompletionTool.kt$AttemptCompletionTool$("task_report", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Sub-agent counterpart. Sub-agents are gated away from attempt_completion (allowedWorkers=ORCHESTRATOR only) and must call task_report instead ŌĆö its fields flow directly into the parent LLM's tool result, where attempt_completion targets the user UI.")
ArgumentListWrapping:AutoWakeGuardStateTest.kt$AutoWakeGuardStateTest$(0, guard.attemptCount("sess-off"), "DISABLED must not advance the counter")
ArgumentListWrapping:AutoWakeGuardStateTest.kt$AutoWakeGuardStateTest$(1, guard.attemptCount("sess-cool"), "COOLDOWN must not advance the counter")
ArgumentListWrapping:AutoWakeGuardStateTest.kt$AutoWakeGuardStateTest$(2, guard.attemptCount("sess-cap"), "CAP_REACHED must not advance the counter past the cap")
ArgumentListWrapping:AutoWakeGuardStateTest.kt$AutoWakeGuardStateTest$(AutoWakeGuardState.Decision.CAP_REACHED, capA, "session A has hit its cap")
ArgumentListWrapping:AutoWakeGuardStateTest.kt$AutoWakeGuardStateTest$(AutoWakeGuardState.Decision.CAP_REACHED, third, "third decide call must be blocked by cap")
ArgumentListWrapping:AutoWakeGuardStateTest.kt$AutoWakeGuardStateTest$(AutoWakeGuardState.Decision.COOLDOWN, secondTooSoon, "second call within cooldown must be skipped")
ArgumentListWrapping:AutoWakeGuardStateTest.kt$AutoWakeGuardStateTest$(AutoWakeGuardState.Decision.PROCEED, after, "after cooldown window elapses, decide() must proceed")
ArgumentListWrapping:AutoWakeGuardStateTest.kt$AutoWakeGuardStateTest$(AutoWakeGuardState.Decision.PROCEED, afterReset, "after resetSession, the cap starts over from zero")
ArgumentListWrapping:AutoWakeGuardStateTest.kt$AutoWakeGuardStateTest$(AutoWakeGuardState.Decision.PROCEED, okB, "session B's counter is independent from A's")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("Approval prompt fires; on approval, process is SIGTERM'd, waited briefly, then SIGKILL'd if needed; pool entry removed.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("Byte cursor for incremental polling ŌĆö pass back the previous call's `offset=` header value to get only new bytes.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("Cap the response to just the last N lines of available output ŌĆö like `tail -n N`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("Either returns Exit + output (build done) or ATTACH_TIMEOUT (still building after 5min ŌĆö let the LLM decide whether to keep waiting or kill).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("Exact bytes (after escape processing) to write. The LLM controls newlines explicitly ŌĆö `y` does not press Enter, `y\\n` does.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("Full output written to {sessionDir}/tool-output/<file>; tool returns a preview + path. LLM can follow up with read_file/search_code on the path.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("How long to wait before returning AttachTimeout ŌĆö capped at 10 minutes to keep the agent loop responsive.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("Java regex applied per line after offset/tail slicing ŌĆö only matching lines are returned.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("Process receives `y\\n` on stdin. Follow up with `attach` or `output(since_offset=ŌĆ”)` to see the reaction.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("Returns only the new bytes printed since the previous call. Updated `offset=` header threads forward to the next poll.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("The handle id returned when the background process was spawned (or surfaced by `list`).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("UNSUPPORTED_ACTION: '$action' ŌĆö expected one of: status, output, attach, send_stdin, kill")

ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("When true, the full content is spilled to disk under the session's tool-output dir and the response carries a head/tail preview plus the file path.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("action", "When action is omitted and id is omitted, list is the implicit default; passing action=list explicitly is also valid.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("tail_lines", "Status hard-codes the last-5-lines preview; use `output` for arbitrary tails.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("the handle must still exist in the session pool ŌĆö Exited/Killed handles return NO_SUCH_ID_IN_SESSION")
ArgumentListWrapping:BackgroundProcessTool.kt$BackgroundProcessTool$("timeout_seconds", "Kill uses BackgroundHandle's internal timeouts; no per-call override exposed.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("401 Unauthorized", "PasswordSafe token is wrong or expired. Stop and ask the user to update the token in Settings.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("401 Unauthorized", "PasswordSafe token wrong or expired. Stop and ask user to update the token.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("401 Unauthorized", "Token wrong or expired. Stop and ask the user to update the token in Settings.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("403 Forbidden", "User has BUILD_READ but not BUILD permission, or the plan is disabled. Surface to user.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("403 from missing X-Atlassian-Token", "This is handled transparently by the service ŌĆö if it surfaces it indicates a server misconfiguration, not an auth issue.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("404 Not Found", "build_key doesn't exist. Often a typo in the build number ŌĆö confirm with the user.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Also loads the list of commits that went into this build (SHA, message, author). Useful for tracing which commit broke CI.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Bamboo build result key e.g. PROJ-PLAN-123 ŌĆö used across all actions that operate on a single build")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Bamboo plan key e.g. PROJ-PLAN ŌĆö for build_status, trigger_build, recent_builds, get_running_builds")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Brief description shown in approval dialog ŌĆö for write actions: trigger_build, stop_build, cancel_build")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Build must be in a completed state (Successful, Failed, or Stopped). In-progress builds return an empty list.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Build must have completed (or at least started running tests) ŌĆö results are empty for queued builds.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("ChainKeyResolver maps PROJ-BACKEND + feature/PROJ-999-auth-refactor ŌåÆ PROJ-BACKEND42. Returns in-progress builds for that branch chain.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("ChainKeyResolver maps plan_key + branch to the Bamboo branch-chain key. Fails with an error if the branch has never been built in Bamboo.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("ChainKeyResolver maps to branch chain key. Branch must have been built at least once.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Completes the artifact-fetch workflow. Without it, the LLM must fall back to curl ŌĆö with the same credential-leak risk as other Bamboo curl fallbacks.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Critical pre-step for targeted log/test fetching ŌĆö provides job resultKeys that keep downstream calls small.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Custom Bamboo build variables to inject. Passed as JSON ŌĆö keys and values must both be strings.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Efficient trend analysis without per-build network calls. Used whenever the user wants build history.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Either the plan-level build key (PROJ-PLAN-138) or a job-level resultKey (PROJ-PLAN-UNIT-138) from get_build.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("How many recent builds to return. Default is 10; increase for longer trend analysis.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("If branch is supplied, it must have been built at least once in Bamboo (chain key must exist).")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("If true, also fetch the per-build commit list (SHA, message, author) via Bamboo's expand=changes.change. Default false to keep token cost low.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Irreplaceable for CI triage ŌĆö the agent cannot diagnose a build failure without log access.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Lists all artifacts produced by a completed build. Delegates to service.getArtifacts(buildKey). Returns artifact names and download URLs for use with download_artifact.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Much more efficient than log parsing for test-failure triage ŌĆö structured output, minimal tokens.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Necessary for the 'requeue after fixing the issue' workflow. Without it the user must manually stop the build in Bamboo.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Necessary pre-flight for stop_build and cancel_build ŌĆö prevents 400/no-op errors from operating on builds in the wrong state.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("No chain key for branch", "Branch has never been built in this plan. The user must trigger at least one build on that branch first.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Optional branch name ŌĆö for build_status, recent_builds. Use project_context tool to discover current branch.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Optional list of stage names to run ŌĆö for trigger_build. Omit to run all stages (Bamboo default). When provided, Bamboo runs from the first stage in the list forward (REST API limitation: only the first stage is passed). Example: [\"Build\", \"Unit Tests\"] triggers from 'Build' stage onward. Rejected with an error if the list is empty.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Optional local path to save artifact ŌĆö for download_artifact (defaults to temp file)")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Pairs with stop_build to give full queue management. Without cancel_build the user must go to the Bamboo UI to pull a queued build.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Parallel async call to service.getBuildChanges(buildKey). Result is appended as 'Commits (showing N of M)' block. Capped at 50 commits displayed.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Parsed by BambooToolUtils.parseVariables; invalid JSON returns a parse error before the network call.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Parsed to a Set<String>; first element passed as stage=<name> with executeAllStages=false to the Bamboo REST queue endpoint.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Queries the master plan key directly (trunk builds only). Feature-branch builds will NOT appear.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Repository name for multi-repo projects ŌĆö for build_status, recent_builds, get_running_builds")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Required precondition for download_artifact ŌĆö there is no other way to discover artifact URLs.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Restrict the build to specific stages. Bamboo runs from the first stage in the list forward. Empty list is rejected.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns 'Artifact downloaded to: <absolutePath>\nSize: <bytes> bytes'. LLM can then use read_file or run_command on the downloaded path.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns a list of in-progress builds (build key, state, start time, elapsed). Empty list if nothing is running.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns a list of recent builds with build number, state, start time, duration, and build key.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns a summary line with state (Successful / Failed / In Progress), build number, start time, duration, and key.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns artifact list with names and download URLs. LLM picks the relevant URL and calls download_artifact.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns build details plus a commit list (SHA, author, message) ŌĆö LLM can identify the offending commit.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns build header (state, number, key, duration, start time) plus stage/job breakdown with per-job resultKeys and test counts. With include_commits=true, appends a commit list block.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns confirmation that the stop signal was sent. The build transitions to Stopped state asynchronously.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns failing test list with class names and error messages. LLM can link failures to source files.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns last 5 builds with status. LLM can spot intermittent failures or a broken CI trend.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns stages and jobs with resultKeys. LLM picks the failing job's resultKey for the next get_build_log call.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns test summary (total, passed, failed, skipped) plus a list of failing tests with class names, method names, and error messages.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns the log text (potentially large ŌĆö 29KB+ for multi-job plans). ToolOutputSpiller may page the output if it exceeds 30K chars.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Returns the queued build key (e.g. PROJ-BACKEND-143) and a link to view the build in Bamboo.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Scope the status check to a specific branch. If omitted, returns the default (trunk) plan's latest build.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Shows what files a build produced ŌĆö like the Artifacts tab in Bamboo. Returns names and URLs; use download_artifact to actually fetch a file.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("The Bamboo plan identifier ŌĆö project prefix, dash, plan shortkey (e.g. MYPROJECT-BACKEND).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("The URL from get_artifacts. Must be a Bamboo artifact download URL ŌĆö not an arbitrary external URL.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("The cheapest CI health check ŌĆö one API call, minimal tokens. High usage frequency.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("The only write path into Bamboo CI ŌĆö trigger_build is the only way the agent can start a build without the user pressing a button. The XSRF and form-encoding handling is what makes it safe.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("The specific build run identifier ŌĆö plan key plus build number (e.g. PROJ-BACKEND-142).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Validated by ToolValidation.validateBambooBuildKey. Bamboo routes automatically based on key format.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Validated by ToolValidation.validateBambooBuildKey. The legacy 'result_key' alias also resolves here.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("Where to save the file on disk. Use an absolute path so you can reliably reference it later.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("bamboo_plans", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use bamboo_plans to discover plan keys and job structure before calling bamboo_builds actions.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("bitbucket_pr", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Show CI build status on a PR: get_build_log result can be annotated on the PR via bitbucket_pr(add_pr_comment).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("build must be completed ŌĆö artifacts are not available for queued or in-progress builds")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("build must currently be in Running state; stopping a queued or finished build is a no-op or error")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("build not running", "Bamboo returns an error or no-op. Use get_running_builds to verify state before stopping.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("build_status and recent_builds overlap for the 'is CI green?' query ŌĆö build_status is the fast path; recent_builds adds history. The distinction is clear but may be confusing to the LLM; a single action with a history_count=0 default could unify them.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("empty list on in-progress build", "Build hasn't produced artifacts yet. Wait for completion and retry.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("empty results on in-progress build", "Test results may be incomplete while the build is still running. Wait for build completion or poll via get_running_builds.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("get_artifacts and download_artifact form a mandatory two-step pattern ŌĆö consider whether they could be combined into a single action that lists and optionally downloads in one call.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("java.io.File.createTempFile('bamboo-artifact-', '.tmp') is used ŌĆö path is returned but lost across sessions if not recorded.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("jira", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Link a build result to a Jira ticket: get the commit SHAs from get_build(include_commits=true) then call jira(get_dev_branches) to complete the BambooŌåÆBitbucketŌåÆJira triangle.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("must be a URL from a prior get_artifacts call; arbitrary external URLs may be rejected by the Bamboo auth layer")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("must match Bamboo plan key format: uppercase letters/digits/hyphens, at least one dash")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("parent directory missing", "If target_path parent dir doesn't exist, the file write fails. Use /tmp or omit target_path.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("parent directory must exist; relative paths are resolved from the JVM working directory")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("plan_key must exist in Bamboo. If branch is supplied, the branch must have been built at least once.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("stop_build and cancel_build both accept the legacy 'result_key' alias alongside 'build_key'. The alias is undocumented in the LLM description and could be removed to simplify the schema.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsTool.kt$BambooBuildsTool$("variables parse error", "Invalid JSON in variables param ŌĆö BambooToolUtils.parseVariables returns a ToolResult error before the network call. LLM must fix the JSON.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooBuildsToolTest.kt$BambooBuildsToolTest$(result.content.contains("Fix the critical login bug"), "content must reference the commit message first line")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("401 Unauthorized", "Bamboo token is expired or invalid. Stop and ask the user to re-enter the token in Settings.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("5-tier detection runs: may return a branch plan key (MYAPP-BUILD-PAYMENT) or master plan key (MYAPP-BUILD) depending on what exists.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Always pass repo_root, branch_name, preferred_master when available ŌĆö the 5-tier algorithm is substantially more accurate for multi-module repos.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Bamboo plan key e.g. PROJ-PLAN ŌĆö for get_plan_branches, get_plan_variables, rerun_failed_jobs, trigger_stage")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Bamboo queues a rerun of only the failed jobs. LLM can poll via `bamboo_builds(build_status)` to track progress.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Brief description shown in approval dialog ŌĆö for write actions: rerun_failed_jobs, trigger_stage")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Clicks 'Rerun failed jobs' in the Bamboo UI for a specific build ŌĆö runs only the jobs that failed, not the whole plan from scratch.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Distinct from `trigger_build` ŌĆö retries without the cost of running passing stages again. Frequent use case for flaky tests.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Essential pre-flight before trigger_stage(variables=...) ŌĆö validates variable names and shows editability flags.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("For a fresh plan run with no variable overrides, bamboo_builds(trigger_build) is equally valid.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Git remote URL (e.g. git@github.com:org/repo.git or https://github.com/org/repo) ŌĆö for auto_detect_plan")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("High cost on large Bamboo instances. If `search_plans` supported wildcard/empty query the LLM could always use search instead.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Large response truncated", "On very large Bamboo instances the response is spilled to disk via ToolOutputSpiller. Use `search_plans` instead.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Like clicking 'Run' on a specific stage of a Bamboo plan (not just 'run everything'). You can override build variables at trigger time ŌĆö e.g. point the deploy stage at a different environment.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Lists branches configured for a plan. Optionally filters by repository name for multi-repo plans.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Lists every CI build plan on the server ŌĆö like pulling up the 'All Plans' view in Bamboo. Use sparingly on large servers.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Low standalone value ŌĆö most users know their project key. Could be removed if `get_project_plans` validated the key and returned a helpful error message listing valid keys.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("More efficient than `get_plans` when the project is known ŌĆö saves returning the entire plan catalog.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Name of the trunk/main branch in your team's convention (e.g. 'main', 'master', 'trunk').")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Narrows the plan list to a single Bamboo project ŌĆö like clicking on a project folder in the Bamboo sidebar to see only its plans.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Needed before triggering a branch build to confirm the branch plan exists. Without this, the LLM might trigger on a non-existent branch key.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Needed by `auto_detect_plan` legacy path and for user requests to enumerate everything.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Niche ŌĆö most users care about plan-level defaults (get_plan_variables), not per-build snapshots. Could merge into bamboo_builds since it operates on a build result key.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Optional. Branch to detect. Triggers the 5-tier auto_detect_plan overload when present.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Optional. Local repo root path. When provided alongside git_remote_url (or with branch_name/preferred_master), routes auto_detect_plan to the richer 5-tier detection (better for multi-module repos).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Optional. Preferred master/main branch name. Triggers the 5-tier auto_detect_plan overload when present.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Override plan-level variable defaults for this run. Pass as a JSON object string.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Parsed as Int; invalid non-numeric values return an explicit error before any network call.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Parsed by BambooToolUtils.parseVariables; invalid JSON returns an error before the network call.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Prefer `search_plans` if any part of the plan name is known ŌĆö avoids fetching the full list.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Required precondition for `get_project_plans`. Low cost; no alternative.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Retries only the failed jobs from an existing build run. Requires plan_key and build_number. build_number is validated as an integer. Write action ŌĆö goes through approval gate.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Returns all build plans across all projects. Expensive on large Bamboo instances ŌĆö can return hundreds of plans.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Returns confirmation that the build/stage was queued. LLM can poll via `bamboo_builds(build_status)` to track.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Returns list of variable definitions: name, default value, whether the variable is editable (can be overridden at trigger time).")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Returns the default variable definitions for a plan (name, value, editable flag). These are the values used in builds unless overridden at trigger time.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Returns the detected plan key (e.g. MYAPP-BUILD). LLM can immediately use this as plan_key in other actions.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Returns the variable values that were used in a specific build result (identified by result_key, e.g. PROJ-PLAN-123).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Routes to the 5-tier `BambooService.autoDetectPlan(repoRoot, remoteUrl, ...)` overload. More accurate for multi-module repos.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Routes to the 5-tier overload. Helps the algorithm disambiguate the master plan from branch plans.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Server-side plan search by name substring. More efficient than `get_plans` on large Bamboo instances.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Shows the plan's 'environment variables' ŌĆö the default values that every build of this plan inherits unless explicitly overridden when triggering.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Shows what variable values were injected into a specific past build run ŌĆö like checking 'what flags were set when build #123 ran'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Shows which Git branches have their own Bamboo plan branch (branch plan = a parallel build for that branch). Useful before triggering a build on a feature branch.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Shows you the top-level project folders in Bamboo ŌĆö like viewing the project list in the Bamboo sidebar.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("The build number to retry (the trailing number in the result key, e.g. 42 from PROJ-PLAN-42).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("The canonical 'find me the plan key' action. Saves token cost vs `get_plans` on large Bamboo instances.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("The current Git branch. When provided, the 5-tier algorithm may prefer branch plans over the master plan.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("The exact stage name as configured in Bamboo. Omit to trigger the entire plan from the beginning.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("The git remote URL of the repository whose plan you want to detect. Usually `origin`. Can be SSH or HTTPS format.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("The local directory where the git repo lives. Triggers the richer 5-tier detection algorithm.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("The only action that combines per-stage targeting + variable injection in one call. High value for deploy workflows.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("The short project identifier in Bamboo (e.g. PROJ, INFRA). Discover valid keys via `get_projects`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("The three-segment build result key: project-plan-buildNumber. Note: this is NOT the same format as plan_key.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Triggers a specific stage of a plan with optional variable overrides. `stage` param is optional ŌĆö omitting it triggers the entire plan. Variables are parsed from a JSON string. Write action ŌĆö goes through approval gate.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Type-ahead search for Bamboo plans ŌĆö like the search box in Bamboo that filters the plan list as you type.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Uses the legacy 1-arg overload (plan-list scan) when no other 5-tier triggers are present.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("Valuable for debugging 'why did that build behave differently'. Without this, the LLM has to parse raw build logs.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("When the plan uses multiple linked repositories, filter branches to just this repo.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("bamboo_builds", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "bamboo_builds covers build results, logs, test outcomes, and artifact retrieval ŌĆö the execution side of the same CI system. Always use bamboo_builds to track a build you triggered via bamboo_plans.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("build_number", "trigger_stage starts a new run; it does not operate on an existing build number")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("jira", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Bamboo builds can be linked to Jira tickets via the Atlassian integration; jira(get_ticket, include_dev_status=true) surfaces build status without requiring a separate bamboo_plans call.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("multiple matches", "Several plans match the URL. The 5-tier algorithm uses branch_name and preferred_master to break ties; the legacy path returns all matches for the LLM to disambiguate.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("no failed jobs", "Bamboo may return an error or no-op. Confirm via `bamboo_builds(build_status)` first.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("no match found", "No plan matched the remote URL. The plan may not have a repository link configured in Bamboo, or the URL format differs from what Bamboo stores. Fall back to `search_plans` by repo name.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("no matches", "Empty list returned (not an error). LLM should broaden the query or fall back to `get_plans`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("non-editable variable override", "Bamboo may silently ignore or reject the override. Check editability flag via `get_plan_variables`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("plan_key", "get_build_variables requires result_key (3 segments) not plan_key (2 segments) ŌĆö use get_plan_variables for plan-level defaults")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("plan_key", "search_plans does not filter by a specific plan key ŌĆö use get_plan_branches or get_plan_variables for that")

ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("result not found", "Build may have been purged per Bamboo retention policy. Try `bamboo_builds(get_build)` first to confirm it still exists.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("result_key", "get_plan_variables returns plan defaults, not per-build snapshots ŌĆö use get_build_variables for build-level values")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("result_key", "rerun_failed_jobs takes plan_key + build_number separately, not a combined result_key")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("stage name not found", "Bamboo returns an error. Stage names are case-sensitive; verify via `bamboo_builds(get_build)` on a recent result.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("variables JSON invalid", "BambooToolUtils.parseVariables returns a parse error before any network call.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansTool.kt$BambooPlansTool$("variables", "rerun_failed_jobs retries with the same variables as the original build ŌĆö use trigger_stage for variable overrides")
ArgumentListWrapping:BambooPlansToolTest.kt$BambooPlansToolTest$(repoRoot = any(), remoteUrl = any(), branchName = any(), preferredMaster = any())

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("## Summary
Fixes UTCŌåÆlocal timezone bug in OrderService#toLocalTime.

## Testing
Unit tests added for DST edge cases.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("401 Unauthorized", "Token invalid or expired. Stop and ask the user to re-enter the Bitbucket token in Settings.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("404 / empty list", "No PRs found for this branch. May mean the branch hasn't been pushed yet or no one opened a PR.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("404 branch not found", "The from_branch or to_branch doesn't exist in the repo. LLM should confirm branch name with the user.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("409 Conflict", "A PR for this branch already exists. Surface to user; use get_prs_for_branch to find the existing PR.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("409 Conflict", "Author attempted self-approval. LLM should check PR author in get_pr_detail before calling approve.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("409 Conflict", "Merge vetoes are in place (unapproved, conflicts, CI failure). Run check_merge_status to identify blockers.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Application Links not configured", "Returns empty list ŌĆö not an error. The tool cannot populate links if the Jira integration isn't set up server-side.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Approval dialog description for write actions: create_pr, approve_pr, merge_pr, decline_pr")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Bitbucket DC only (tested on 9.4.16 / DC 10.x). Bitbucket Cloud uses different API paths and a different auth scheme. This tool does NOT work with Bitbucket Cloud.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Bitbucket-Jira Application Link must be configured server-side for linked issues to appear")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Branch name (e.g. 'feature/PROJ-1') to look up PRs for. Required for get_prs_for_branch.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Changes the description/body of an existing PR ŌĆö like editing the main text box in the Bitbucket PR view.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Changes the title of an existing PR ŌĆö like clicking the pencil icon next to the title in Bitbucket.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Cheap, targeted readout that avoids loading the full activity feed for a yes/no blocker question.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Closes a PR without merging ŌĆö the equivalent of clicking 'Decline' in Bitbucket. The code stays in the branch but the PR is closed.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Conditional registration only: this tool is absent from the schema if the user hasn't configured a Bitbucket URL in Settings ŌåÆ Tools ŌåÆ Workflow Orchestrator ŌåÆ Connections. The LLM must call tool_search('bitbucket') to find it in a fresh session.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Core write action ŌĆö the agent's ability to open a PR is the end-goal of every coding task. Without it the agent must ask the user to open the PR manually.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Could be partially replaced by check_merge_status (which also surfaces blockers) or get_pr_activities (which lists them). " + "If check_merge_status is called first anyway, this action's info is redundant. Drop candidate if usage is low.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Dashboard-level discovery action; useful at session start to orient the LLM to the user's PR state.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Declines (closes without merging) a PR. Irreversible ŌĆö declined PRs can be reopened only if the source branch still exists.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Diff is the primary content surface for AI code review ŌĆö without this the LLM can only see file names, not code.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Enables the agent to explain CI merge gates to the user and set expectations about what must pass before merging.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Enables the key cross-tool workflow: merge PR ŌåÆ get linked Jira keys ŌåÆ transition tickets to Done. Without this the agent has to extract keys from PR title/branch heuristically.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Essential for understanding reviewer feedback. Without it the LLM can't tell what changes are requested before attempting a re-push or merge.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Fetches full PR metadata: title, description, author, reviewers, status, source/target refs, version, links.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("File-change listing is necessary for meaningful code review and impact analysis ŌĆö a core PR inspection action.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Fills a critical gap: get_my_prs returns author-scoped PRs only; this action answers 'is there a PR for this branch' regardless of who opened it.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Frequently needed after scope changes ŌĆö PR descriptions get stale when implementation evolves.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("High-value pre-merge check that avoids merge_pr 409s from unapproved state.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("How to merge. no-ff = merge commit; squash = one commit; rebase-no-ff = rebase then merge commit.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Lists PRs authored by the currently authenticated user. Filterable by state (OPEN/MERGED/DECLINED; default OPEN).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Lists PRs you've been asked to review ŌĆö like the 'Reviewing' section in Bitbucket's PR dashboard.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Lists files changed in the PR (changed paths, change types: add/modify/delete/copy/move).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Low-cost housekeeping action. Part of the PR editing pair with update_pr_description.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Low-frequency relative to other actions ŌĆö most titles are set correctly at create time. Could be dropped if token budget is tight; the user can edit the title manually in Bitbucket.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Low-frequency standalone use. Most PR workflows use get_pr_changes or get_pr_diff for content. This adds commit list only ŌĆö could be folded into get_pr_detail as an optional flag.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Merge and decline are irreversible without manual Bitbucket admin action. Always require user approval gate confirmation before executing.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Necessary counterpart to merge_pr. A PR management workflow without decline is incomplete.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Not recommended ŌĆö always include description for irreversible write actions so the user knows what they're approving.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("One-line description for the agent approval dialog. Recommended for write actions.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("One-line description for the approval gate. Strongly recommended for an irreversible action.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("One-line description for the approval gate. Strongly recommended for an irreversible-feeling action.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Rarely queried in practice ŌĆö most users know which builds run. Could be a drop candidate if usage telemetry shows near-zero calls.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Reads everything about a PR ŌĆö title, description, who's reviewing, current state, and whether it can merge.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Reads the PR's history and discussion ŌĆö all comments, approvals, and state changes chronologically, like the Bitbucket activity tab.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Required gate before merge_pr. Skipping it leads to 409 from Bitbucket when blockers exist. Saves the user from attempting an impossible merge.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Returns 'canMerge: false, vetoes: [Not all required tasks are complete, Merge checks: build not green]'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Returns 3 reviewers: 2 APPROVED, 1 UNAPPROVED. LLM can decide whether required approvals are met.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Returns PR #42 in OPEN state targeting 'main'; agent can link to it or proceed to check_merge_status.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Returns PR header (id, title, description, state, author, reviewers with approval status, version, links).")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Returns a list of PR summaries (id, title, state, target branch) whose source branch matches branch_name.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Returns all review comments and approvals; LLM can identify which comments need addressing.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Returns participants with role (REVIEWER/AUTHOR), status (APPROVED/NEEDS_WORK/UNAPPROVED), and lastReviewedCommit.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Returns raw unified diff text. Large PRs may be truncated by ToolOutputSpiller (>30K chars) with a spill path for read_file.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Returns required-build conditions grouped by branch pattern (e.g. 'main: must pass CI-Pipeline, Security-Scan').")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Reviewer-role complement to get_my_prs; enables the agent to manage a user's full PR workload, not just authored PRs.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Shows the actual code changes ŌĆö line by line, additions and deletions ŌĆö the same as `git diff` for the PR.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Slightly overlaps with update_pr_title as a housekeeping pair. If one is dropped, this one is more useful (descriptions get stale more than titles).", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Submits an approval for the PR. Delegates to BitbucketService.approvePullRequest. Author cannot approve their own PR on Bitbucket DC.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("The body of the PR ŌĆö what changed, why, how to test. Markdown is rendered by Bitbucket.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("The foundational read action for all PR workflows. Nearly every multi-step PR workflow starts here.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("The numeric PR ID from Bitbucket ŌĆö the number after '/pull-requests/' in the URL.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("The terminal write action in the PR lifecycle. Without it the agent cannot complete PR-based workflows ŌĆö the user must go to Bitbucket UI to finish.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Useful for scope-check before merge and for code-review context-gathering.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Validated non-blank. If equals to_branch, the tool returns an error before any network call.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Version-race risk on all mutations: merge_pr, approve_pr, and decline_pr send the Bitbucket `version` field (from get_pr_detail). ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Whether to delete the feature branch after a successful merge. 'true' or 'false' string.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Which Bitbucket repository. Omit for single-repo projects; required when the project has multiple repos.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("Write action with real workflow value ŌĆö enables the agent to close the PR review loop as a designated reviewer.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("bitbucket_repo", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use bitbucket_repo for branch management (create_branch, get_branches, get_file_content, build statuses) before or after PR operations.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketPrTool.kt$BitbucketPrTool$("bitbucket_review", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use bitbucket_review(add_pr_comment, add_inline_comment, reply_to_comment) to leave inline code review feedback on the PR.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketRepoTool.kt$BitbucketRepoTool$("Prefer this over get_build_statuses when the user just wants 'did it pass?' ŌĆö far fewer tokens.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketRepoTool.kt$BitbucketRepoTool$("Repo context; the build-status API is commit-global in Bitbucket DC, but this may affect service routing for multi-repo projects.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketRepoTool.kt$BitbucketRepoTool$("Returns PR #42 (MERGED): 'Add login rate-limiting' ŌĆö LLM can then use bitbucket_pr.get_pr_detail for the full review context.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketRepoTool.kt$BitbucketRepoTool$("Returns list of build statuses; LLM can report which jobs passed and link to failed build logs.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketRepoTool.kt$BitbucketRepoTool$("The commit SHA, branch name, or tag to branch from ŌĆö the 'base' of the new branch.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketRepoTool.kt$BitbucketRepoTool$("The commit must be reachable from a branch that has or had an open/merged PR in the target repo.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketRepoTool.kt$BitbucketRepoTool$("Token-efficient CI pass/fail gate. The compact output makes it the right first call in any 'is this commit ready?' workflow before escalating to the full get_build_statuses if needed.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Approval/LGTM is the most common reviewer action. Without this, the agent can't close the review loop.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Bitbucket project key (e.g. PROJ) ŌĆö for list_comments, get_comment, edit_comment, delete_comment, resolve_comment, reopen_comment")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Completes the resolve/reopen cycle ŌĆö needed for review workflows where comments are closed prematurely.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Deletion is permanent and not recoverable via the API. Prefer resolve_comment for soft-hiding.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Fixed in the latest commit ŌĆö moved the connection pool initialization to the constructor.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("High-value close-the-loop action ŌĆö lets the agent mark review feedback as addressed without deleting the audit trail.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("If version was stale (e.g. another reviewer edited the comment), Bitbucket returns 409. LLM re-fetches with get_comment and retries.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Needed for cleanup; rare in practice ŌĆö resolve_comment is usually preferred.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("PR not found", "Bitbucket returns 404 ŌåÆ surfaced as error result. LLM should confirm pr_id with user.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Removes someone from the reviewers list ŌĆö like clicking the Ō£Ģ next to a reviewer's name in the Bitbucket sidebar.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Repository slug ŌĆö for list_comments, get_comment, edit_comment, delete_comment, resolve_comment, reopen_comment")

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Required for structured review conversations ŌĆö without it the agent always starts new threads, fragmenting the review.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Required gateway for edit_comment and delete_comment ŌĆö the version field is the only safe way to prevent optimistic-lock conflicts.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:BitbucketReviewTool.kt$BitbucketReviewTool$("Required precondition for edit/delete/resolve/reopen ŌĆö without a comment ID+version the write actions can't proceed.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
