ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("A cycle-detection result is appended: either 'No cycles detected' or the cycle path.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Actually run the pytest tests ŌĆö with the same filtering options as the `pytest` command line. Unlike python_runtime_exec, this uses a shell subprocess and works in any IDE.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Architecturally critical ŌĆö the only way to verify inter-module dependency contracts (e.g., `:agent` must only depend on `:core`) without parsing build files manually.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Avoids the LLM reading all conftest.py files manually. Useful when test suite has many fixtures.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Case-insensitive substring filter applied to dependency groupId:artifactId strings.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Check whether `poetry.lock` is consistent with `pyproject.toml` ŌĆö reports lock file age, package count, and whether `poetry lock --check` passes.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Check whether `uv.lock` is consistent with `pyproject.toml` ŌĆö reports lock file age, package count, and lock check result.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Convenient but thin ŌĆö wraps a single pip command. Merge candidate with poetry_list and uv_list.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Could be `run_command pip list | grep <term>` at the cost of one extra tool call. Low drop priority unless action count becomes a concern.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Cycle detection runs by default. Pass false only if the graph is very large and you don't need it.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Essential for Gradle-based projects. Parsing the build file is much faster than running the daemon.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Filter by Gradle configuration (implementation, api, testImplementation, etc.) ŌĆö for gradle_dependencies")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Filter by dependency scope (compile, test, runtime, provided) ŌĆö for maven_dependencies")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Filter by name/value substring ŌĆö narrows the listing output for the maven/gradle/pip/poetry/uv listing actions.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Filter dependency tree to paths containing this artifact ŌĆö for maven_dependency_tree")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Gradle module name, typically the subproject name (e.g., 'core', 'jira', ':agent').")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Include library dependencies in output ŌĆö for module_dependency_graph. Default: false.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Include transitive (indirect) dependencies ŌĆö for module_dependency_graph. Default: false.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Irreplaceable when debugging POM inheritance chains. The alternative is opening a chain of parent POMs by hand.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Keyword expression passed to pytest's `-k` flag ŌĆö matches test names by substring or boolean expression.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Like `mvn dependency:tree` ŌĆö shows not just direct dependencies but everything they pull in transitively, in a tree format.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Like `mvn help:effective-pom` ŌĆö shows the POM after all parent inheritance is merged, so you see the final resolved configuration.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List all the tests pytest can find ŌĆö like `pytest --collect-only` but returning a structured list of test IDs the agent can reference in pytest_run.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List every Gradle task available ŌĆö like `./gradlew tasks` but without starting the Gradle daemon.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List every Maven plugin the project uses to build itself ŌĆö like the compiler plugin, Surefire, Shade, etc.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List every Python package in the Poetry-managed virtualenv ŌĆö like `poetry show` in the terminal.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List every Python package in the uv-managed environment ŌĆö like `uv pip list` in the terminal.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List every Python package installed in the current pip environment ŌĆö like `pip list` in the terminal.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List every library the Maven project depends on ŌĆö like running `mvn dependency:list` but structured and instant.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List every module IntelliJ knows about ŌĆö like looking at the Project Structure dialog's Modules tab, but as text the agent can read.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List every pytest fixture available ŌĆö like `pytest --fixtures` ŌĆö so the LLM knows what setup helpers are available before writing a new test.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List the command-line scripts the package installs ŌĆö like looking at `[tool.poetry.scripts]` in pyproject.toml, but without opening the file.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("List the libraries declared in a Gradle build file ŌĆö like `./gradlew dependencies` but parsed from the file, not executed.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Marker expression passed to pytest's `-m` flag ŌĆö selects tests by their pytest marks.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Show everything pip knows about one installed package ŌĆö dependencies it needs, who needs it, where it's installed.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Show the key=value properties from `gradle.properties` ŌĆö like plugin versions, JVM args, and custom project-wide settings.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Transitive dependency debugging is a common Java task. This is cleaner and faster than spawning mvn.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM is asked to check for dependency updates or security patches in a pip-managed project.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM is working in a Poetry project and needs to check installed packages ŌĆö analogous to pip_list for Poetry environments.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM is working in a uv-managed Python project and needs to check installed packages.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM is writing a new pytest test and needs to know which fixtures are already defined ŌĆö e.g., to find a `db_session` fixture or check the scope of a `client` fixture.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs detailed information about a single package in a Poetry project ŌĆö analogous to pip_show.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to check which Python packages are installed ŌĆö e.g., to verify `requests` is available before importing it, or to find the installed version of `django`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to know Gradle project-wide configuration values ŌĆö e.g., the `pluginVersion` used in `gradle.properties`, JVM memory flags, or `kotlin.code.style` settings.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to know what CLI entry points a Poetry package exposes ŌĆö e.g., to find the correct command name for a Django management command wrapper or a custom CLI tool.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to know what tasks a Gradle project exposes ŌĆö e.g., to find the correct task name for running integration tests, or to discover if a custom `dockerBuild` task exists.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to know what version variables or build flags are set in the POM, e.g. `java.version`, `project.build.sourceEncoding`, or custom plugin configuration properties.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to know which libraries a Gradle module depends on ŌĆö e.g., to verify `implementation(\"org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter\")` is present, or to find the Kotlin stdlib version.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to know which libraries a Maven module depends on ŌĆö e.g., to check if spring-boot-starter-web is present, or to find the version of jackson-databind.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to know which tests exist before running them ŌĆö to get exact test IDs for pytest_run's `path` or `pattern` parameter.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to run a subset of Python tests to verify a fix ŌĆö after using pytest_discover to confirm test IDs, or when the LLM knows the test path directly.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to understand WHY a transitive dependency is on the classpath ŌĆö e.g., tracing who pulled in `log4j-core`, or finding all paths to `guava`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to understand a specific package's dependency surface ŌĆö e.g., to check what `celery` requires before upgrading it, or to find where `numpy` is installed.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to understand environment-specific build configuration ŌĆö e.g., checking if a 'docker' profile toggles a dependency, or diagnosing why a build behaves differently in CI.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to understand how the project builds, packages, or tests its code ŌĆö e.g., to check if the Failsafe plugin is configured for integration tests, or to find the Shade plugin version.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to understand inherited POM settings that aren't visible in the local pom.xml ŌĆö e.g., what compiler source/target the parent BOM sets, or what version of the Surefire plugin is inherited.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to understand inter-module wiring ŌĆö e.g., to verify that `:agent` only depends on `:core` (per architecture rules), or to detect a circular dependency between modules.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to understand the project's module structure before scoping a dependency query, or when it needs to find the right module name to pass to another `build` action.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM needs to understand what a Python project explicitly declares as its dependencies ŌĆö distinct from pip_list which shows what's currently installed.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM suspects the lockfile is stale after a `pyproject.toml` edit, or wants to confirm lockfile health before running tests.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When the LLM wants to confirm uv lockfile health before running tests or installing packages.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When true, follows dependency edges recursively to show the full transitive closure.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When true, includes external JAR/library nodes alongside module nodes in the graph.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("When true, runs a DFS cycle check and reports any detected circular dependency paths.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("Which Maven module (by artifactId) to inspect. In a single-module project this is usually omitted.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("java_runtime_exec", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Use `java_runtime_exec` to actually compile a module or run JUnit tests after " + "using `build` to understand the module layout and dependencies.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("pip not on PATH or network unavailable", "Returns error. The outdated check requires a live PyPI connection.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("pip not on PATH", "Returns an error message. The LLM should fall back to run_command pip list.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("python_runtime_exec", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Use `python_runtime_exec` to run pytest natively with IDE integration after " + "`build(action=pytest_discover)` confirms the test IDs.")

ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("run_command", Relationship.FALLBACK, "Last-resort fallback for build-tool queries that `build` doesn't cover (e.g., " + "`./gradlew dependencyUpdates` for version-update reports). Slower and noisier.")
ArgumentListWrapping:BuildTool.kt$BuildTool$("scope", "Tree shows all scopes; use maven_dependencies with scope filter for scoped lists.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Bidirectional call-graph walk for a method: callers (recursive `ReferencesSearch` up to `depth Ōłł [1,3]` levels, IdentityHashMap-based visited set for cycle safety) and callees (one-level outbound calls via `PsiTreeUtil.findChildrenOfType(PsiMethodCallExpression)` for Java + Kotlin call expressions via reflection in `JavaKotlinProvider`). Both directions delegate to the per-language `LanguageIntelligenceProvider`; output is capped at 30 callers + 30 callees.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Callees only resolve through method-call expressions the provider knows about ŌĆö `JavaKotlinProvider` walks `PsiMethodCallExpression` (Java) plus Kotlin `KtCallExpression` via reflection. Lambda invocations, method references (`::foo`), reflective calls, and dynamic dispatch through interfaces resolve only when `resolveMethod()` succeeds; unresolved calls are silently dropped. Python callees are best-effort against the `PythonProvider` reflection helper.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Caller list is hard-capped at 30 entries per recursion level (`callers.take(30)`); callee list is hard-capped at 30 entries (`callees.take(30)`). For high-fanin methods (loggers, utility helpers, equals/hashCode), the displayed callers are an arbitrary first-30 subset of `ReferencesSearch` results ŌĆö order is determined by the searcher, not by the user's intent. The LLM has no signal whether 30 is 'all of them' or 'a tiny biased sample'; only the trailing `... (N more)` line distinguishes.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Calls call_hierarchy during indexing ŌĆö gets a dumb-mode error and retries immediately without backoff. The `inSmartMode(project)` deferral applies only after entry; the `PsiToolUtils.isDumb(project)` guard at the top short-circuits with a hard fail.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Calls call_hierarchy on a hot, ubiquitously-called method (`logger.info`, `equals`, `toString`) at `depth=3` ŌĆö explodes into thousands of edges, all silently truncated to the first 30 callers per level. The LLM treats the truncated output as canonical and reasons about a tiny biased sample. Use `class_name` to scope or pin `depth=1` for high-fanin methods.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Calls call_hierarchy on a non-method element ŌĆö a class, field, or constructor that isn't a `PsiMethod`. The provider's `findCallers`/`findCallees` cast `element as? PsiMethod` and silently return an empty list, producing `(no callers found)` + `(no callees found)`. The LLM then concludes the method is unused, when really it's a misuse.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Coerced into `[1, 3]`. The provider's `findCallers` does a DFS up the caller chain, marking each `PsiElement` in an `IdentityHashMap`-based visited set so mutual recursion (AŌåÆBŌåÆA) terminates instead of looping.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Confuses 'callers' with 'called by' / 'callees' with 'calls' when summarising the output back to the user. The labels in the output (`Callers (who calls this method):` / `Callees (what this method calls):`) are unambiguous, but the LLM occasionally swaps them in prose. Bias check: callers point UP (who depends on me), callees point DOWN (what I depend on).")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Cycle detection uses `PsiElement` identity (IdentityHashMap), not `.equals()`, because `PsiElement.equals()` is not a stable contract. This is correct, but it means the visited set is keyed on the LIVE PSI object ŌĆö if PSI is invalidated mid-walk (rare, but possible during a write action) the visited set becomes meaningless. Read-only execution under `ReadAction.nonBlocking` makes this very unlikely in practice.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("How far up the caller chain to walk. `1` = direct callers only. `2` = callers and their callers. `3` = three levels up. Higher depths can explode for ubiquitously-called methods. Note: only callers are recursed; callees are always one level out.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("How many levels deep to trace callers (default: 1, max: 3). depth=2 shows callers of callers.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("If `class_name` is set, the lookup composes `class_name#method` first; otherwise the bare name is fed to each provider's `findSymbol`. The first provider in `registry.allProviders()` that returns a `PsiMethod` wins, and that same provider drives both `findCallers` and `findCallees`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("If multiple classes have a method with this name (e.g. several `save()` methods), name the class to scope the lookup. Internally builds `class_name#method` and feeds that to `findSymbol`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Like the IDE's 'Show Call Hierarchy' tree, but for the agent ŌĆö it picks one method and tells the LLM both who calls it (callers, traced upward through the codebase) and what it calls (callees, listed one level out). Same machinery as Cmd-Alt-H in IntelliJ, just driven by an LLM.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("No disambiguation note for overloaded methods (unlike `find_definition`). When `executeTask` is overloaded, `findSymbol` returns the first match and the tool reports the call hierarchy of THAT overload only, with no `(N other method(s) with same name)` warning. The LLM has no signal that `class_name` would help.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Output format mixes the two directions in a single string with section headers (`Callers (who calls this method):` / `Callees (what this method calls):`). LLMs occasionally lose the section boundary mid-summary and merge entries from both lists when reporting back to the user.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Skips `class_name` for an overloaded method like `execute` or `build` ŌĆö `findSymbol` returns the first match from `PsiShortNamesCache`, which may be a different overload than the one the user is asking about. Unlike `find_definition`, this tool does NOT append a 'N other methods with same name' disambiguation note; the LLM has no signal that disambiguation is needed.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Tool runs the bare-name path. The first provider that finds ANY method with this name wins ŌĆö there's no disambiguation note, so an overloaded name silently picks one overload.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("Which method's call graph to walk. Bare name (`executeTask`) or `Class#method` syntax (`AgentService#executeTask`); when ambiguous, use `class_name` instead of inlining the qualifier.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("`depth` only recurses on callers, not callees. Callees are always exactly one level out ŌĆö the listing is direct calls inside the method body via `PsiTreeUtil.findChildrenOfType(PsiMethodCallExpression)`, with no recursion into the called methods. Asking `depth=3` does NOT give a 3-deep callee tree.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("`findSymbol` is called with the composed `<class_name>#<method>` string. The first provider that resolves it wins.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("coerced to `[1, 3]` ŌĆö values outside this range are clamped, not rejected; `0` becomes `1`, `99` becomes `3`")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("find_definition", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Run first to locate the method, then call_hierarchy to walk its call graph. find_definition ŌåÆ call_hierarchy is the canonical 'how is this method wired into the codebase' pipeline ŌĆö same `findSymbol` resolution, but find_definition jumps to the source while call_hierarchy fans out from it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("find_implementations", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "For interface or abstract methods, call_hierarchy on the abstract method finds CALLERS but no CALLEES (abstract methods have no body). Pair with find_implementations to enumerate concrete overrides, then call_hierarchy on each override for full coverage.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("find_references", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use instead when you only need direct callers of a single method (no recursion, no callees) ŌĆö find_references is cheaper and returns full reference-site context lines, while call_hierarchy is summarized and capped at 30 entries per direction.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("get_method_body", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use after call_hierarchy when a caller looks suspicious ŌĆö get_method_body fetches the source of a single caller without re-reading the whole file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("must resolve to a `PsiMethod` ŌĆö classes, fields, and Python top-level functions that aren't methods will silently return empty caller/callee lists (the cast `element as? PsiMethod` fails)")

ArgumentListWrapping:CallHierarchyTool.kt$CallHierarchyTool$("type_hierarchy", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Class-relation analogue ŌĆö type_hierarchy walks supertypes/subtypes (inheritance), call_hierarchy walks callers/callees (invocation). Use type_hierarchy for 'who extends this' / 'what does this extend'; use call_hierarchy for 'who calls this' / 'what does this call'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("A new empty changelist is created with this name. Duplicate names are silently allowed by IntelliJ (it appends a counter or returns the existing list ŌĆö implementation-dependent).")

ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("After `list_shelves` to bring a parked WIP back into the working tree ŌĆö usually because the user is ready to continue that line of work or merge it.")
ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Always before `unshelve` (so the LLM can pick the right index), and as a quick audit before deciding whether to `shelve` more.")

ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Before shelving, to see what's currently uncommitted; or to confirm a `create` action took effect; or as a quick 'do I have uncommitted work?' probe.")

ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Cheap, read-only, useful precondition for any other action. Bundles cleanly with the other shelve operations.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Collects `ChangeListManager.allChanges` and calls `ShelveChangesManager.shelveChanges(changes, comment, rollback=true)`. ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Creates a new empty named bucket for grouping uncommitted changes ŌĆö like creating a new playlist before adding songs. Useful for organising work-in-progress before a partial commit.")
ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Defaults to the literal string `Shelved changes` ŌĆö generic, harder to disambiguate later.")

ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Dispatches across 5 actions on the IntelliJ ChangeListManager + ShelveChangesManager: list changelists, list shelves, create a named changelist, shelve all current working-tree changes (reverting them), and unshelve a previously-shelved entry by 0-based index. ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Forgets `list_shelves` is required before `unshelve` ŌĆö guesses an index and unshelves the wrong entry, or passes a stale index after a previous unshelve has shifted the list.")

ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("IDE-shelf vs git-stash divergence: the two stores are physically separate. ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("If `revert_file` ever grows a 'preserve before reverting' option, that option could absorb `shelve` ŌĆö the unified narrative would be 'revert-with-snapshot'. ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Indices in `unshelve` are unstable: each successful unshelve removes its entry, so the next entry's index drops by 1. The LLM has to re-run `list_shelves` between unshelves or it'll pick the wrong target.")

ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Like `git stash`, but the changes go into IntelliJ's own 'Shelf' drawer instead of the git stash pile. ArgumentListWrapping:ChangelistShelveTool.kt$ChangelistShelveTool$("Shows what's currently parked in the IntelliJ Shelf ŌĆö the 'I'll come back to this' drawer. Each entry has an index you'll need to restore it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("'Run this test (or this whole class) and tell me what got covered.' The agent gets back both the test result (passed/failed) and a coverage report keyed by class ŌĆö line %, branch %, plus a list of every uncovered or partially-covered line with method names. Multi-method runs are merged into one snapshot ŌĆö useful for 'cover this class with these three tests'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("'ask' will block the agent loop on the IDE modal ŌĆö only use when a human is actively watching")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("After a `run_with_coverage` call, when the LLM wants to drill into a specific class to find uncovered branches and methods to write follow-up tests for.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("After writing or modifying tests, when the LLM wants to verify it actually exercises the new code paths ŌĆö or when asked 'what's the coverage of class X?'")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("All three methods run in one coverage session via JUnit 5 PATTERNS; one merged snapshot returned. Useful when the LLM wants 'these three tests, together, cover what?'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Auto-derived from pom.xml <groupId>, build.gradle/kts group, or src/main/java source-tree walk (in that order). If none is derivable, all classes are returned with a warning.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Builds a transient JUnit/TestNG run config (class mode for 0 methods, method mode for 1, JUnit 5 PATTERNS mode for 2+), launches it via `ProgramRunnerUtil.executeConfigurationAsync` with the Coverage executor, suspends until `TestResultsViewer.onTestingFinished` (or `processTerminated` fallback), then extracts a `CoverageSnapshot` via reflection over `CoverageDataManager` ŌåÆ suite bundle ŌåÆ `ProjectData.getClasses`. Per-line hits, FULL/PARTIAL/NONE status, jump branches, switch cases, and per-method rollups all returned. Suppresses the replace-or-append modal via `CoverageOptionsProvider.setOptionsToReplace(int)` and restores the user's saved policy in `finally`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Calls `get_file_coverage` before `run_with_coverage` and gets 'No coverage data available' ŌĆö the snapshot is in-memory only, populated by the most recent run. There is no persistence across plugin restarts.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Calls `run_with_coverage` on a TestNG class with `method='testA,testB'` ŌĆö returns a hard 'config creation failed' error because TestNG has no JUnit 5 PATTERNS equivalent and the tool refuses to split (splitting would lose the merged snapshot). LLM must either pick a single method or switch to JUnit.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Cheap follow-up to `run_with_coverage` ŌĆö no process spawn, no compile, just a Map lookup + format. Required to avoid expensive reruns when the LLM iterates on test gaps.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Defaults to 'replace' ŌĆö the safest default for an LLM-driven loop. NOT 'ask', because that re-introduces the modal freeze.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Error: could not build execution environment", "Error", testIsError = true)

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Heavy output ŌĆö full coverage detail for a large class can run hundreds of lines (uncovered lines + branch detail). Mitigated by `MAX_UNCOVERED_ENTRIES=50` cap and `ToolOutputConfig.COMMAND` (100K) spill, but still costly to context.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("How long to wait for the test process before giving up. Coverage runs are slower than plain test runs.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("IntelliJ-IDEA-only ŌĆö the Coverage executor is a JetBrains platform plugin, and the reflective `CoverageDataManager` / `ProjectData` extraction path is JVM-coverage specific. ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Like clicking IntelliJ's 'Run with Coverage' button and then reading the green/red gutter ŌĆö but as data the agent can reason about. ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Maps to REPLACE_SUITE=0 / ADD_SUITE=1 / IGNORE_SUITE=2 ŌĆö applied via `CoverageOptionsProvider.setOptionsToReplace(int)` reflection. Prior value snapshotted and restored in `finally` (success / exception / timeout / cancel ŌĆö all paths).")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Methods are validated against `METHOD_NAME_REGEX`; 1 method ŌåÆ method-mode config, 2+ methods ŌåÆ JUnit 5 `bePatternConfiguration` setPatterns reflection (TestNG rejected with 'config creation failed').")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("New run merges into the prior suite ŌĆö useful for building a cumulative picture across many test classes. The IDE's coverage tab now shows the union.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Overall: ${String.format("%.1f", overallLinePct)}% line coverage, ${String.format("%.1f", overallBranchPct)}% branch coverage")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Passes a file path with extension (`MyService.java`) to `get_file_coverage` and assumes the tool re-reads disk; in fact it matches against fully-qualified class names harvested from the snapshot ŌĆö falls back to suffix match on simple class name, but the LLM sometimes tries paths like `src/main/java/...` that don't resolve.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Re-reads the in-memory `lastSnapshot` (or one-shot `extractCoverageSnapshot` retry) for a single class. Matches input by exact FQ class name, then PSI-resolved class name (resolves a relative source path ŌåÆ FQ name via `LocalFileSystem` + `PsiJavaFile`), then suffix match on simple class name. Returns the same `formatFileCoverageDetail` block as `run_with_coverage` but for one class only ŌĆö with FULL/PARTIAL/NONE per-line lists, jump/switch branch detail, and per-method rollups. No process spawn.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Reflection into `CoverageOptionsProvider.setOptionsToReplace(int)` is fragile to platform-version changes. If the API renames/disappears, the modal-suppression silently no-ops and the agent loop will hang on the dialog. Source-contract test pins the call site shape but cannot validate the platform's contract.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Reflection into `com.intellij.coverage.CoverageOptionsProvider.setOptionsToReplace(int)` is fragile ŌĆö IntelliJ Platform refactors the Coverage plugin every few releases. The apply-then-restore pattern is pinned by `apply and restore calls are balanced and restore lives in finally` source-contract test, but a method rename or signature change will silently make the modal-suppression a no-op (the rest of the run still works ŌĆö the loop just hangs on the first dialog). Re-validate on every Platform bump.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Resolved via three-step lookup: exact match on snapshot keys ŌåÆ PSI-resolved FQ name from path ŌåÆ suffix match on simple class name.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Restricts the coverage snapshot to your project's own packages, hiding framework internals like ByteBuddy proxies, Mockito-generated classes, CGLIB proxies, and transitive dependencies.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Returns `=== ClassName ===` block with line %, branch %, uncovered/partial methods (sorted by lowest coverage first), and individual line entries for every PARTIAL or NONE line. Consecutive NONE lines with no branches and the same method are range-collapsed (`lines 43ŌĆō45 [NONE]`). Capped at 50 line entries with overflow note.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Returns a 2-section text result: first the test outcome (PASSED/FAILED/NO_TESTS_FOUND with stack traces if any ŌĆö same classifier as `java_runtime_exec`), then a coverage block listing every covered file with line % and branch %, sorted by line coverage ascending. The summary line includes file count: `5 tests passed | 3 files covered`. The full snapshot is also cached in `lastSnapshot` for subsequent `get_file_coverage` calls.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Returns full per-line detail for `OrderService` ŌĆö every uncovered line with its method, every partial branch (`if[0] ŌåÆ true=3, false=0`), every uncovered method. No new test process spawned.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Run only specific test method(s) instead of the whole class ŌĆö comma-separated for batch. Whitespace around commas is fine.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Runs `run_with_coverage` with `method=testFoo` (a single method) and concludes 'low coverage' from the sparse snapshot ŌĆö single-method runs only cover the lines reachable from that one test, not the whole class. Mitigation: omit `method` to cover the class, or list multiple methods comma-separated.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Seconds before test process is killed (default: 300, max: 900) ŌĆö for run_with_coverage")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Single-call replacement for: build run config + launch under Coverage + parse JaCoCo XML + diff against source. Nothing else exposes branch coverage and per-method rollups in a single tool result.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Skips `on_existing_suite` and the IDE pops the 'replace or append coverage suite?' modal mid-run ŌĆö the agent loop hangs waiting for a click that will never come. The default policy is now `replace`, but LLMs that explicitly pass `on_existing_suite='ask'` re-introduce the freeze.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Snapshot aggregation only works within a single `run_with_coverage` call ŌĆö multi-method runs aggregate, but two separate runs do not (unless `on_existing_suite=append` is used, which relies on IntelliJ's internal merge logic).")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Split on commas, trimmed; each entry is a prefix matched via `className.startsWith(prefix + \".\")` (so `com.example` includes `com.example.app.UserService` but excludes `com.example2`). Empty string disables filtering entirely.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Test method name(s) ŌĆö for run_with_coverage. Single: 'testFoo'. Multiple methods from the same class in one coverage run: 'testFoo,testBar,testBaz' (comma-separated, whitespace around commas trimmed). Requires JUnit 5 ŌĆö TestNG multi-method returns a hard error (coverage aggregates only within one run; splitting would lose the merged snapshot).")
ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("TestNG + 2+ methods is rejected (no shell fallback ŌĆö splitting loses snapshot aggregation)")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("TestNG with 2+ methods is a hard error with no shell fallback. The LLM occasionally hits this and has to either pick a single method or migrate the test class to JUnit 5.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("TestNG with 2+ methods", "createJUnitRunSettings returns null ŌåÆ tool returns 'config creation failed' with a hint that multi-method coverage requires JUnit 5. NO shell fallback (would lose snapshot aggregation).")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Two actions consolidate launch+harvest and re-read of the IDE's coverage data into a single tool. The LLM gets structured per-line/per-branch/per-method coverage that no shell command can replicate (mvn jacoco:report ŌåÆ HTML ŌåÆ manual parse) in one round trip. Multi-method JUnit 5 PATTERNS support is a real productivity win for 'cover this class with these tests' workflows.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Two-action wrapper around IntelliJ's Coverage executor: `run_with_coverage` launches a JUnit/TestNG test (via reflective JUnit 5 PATTERNS for multi-method runs) under the Coverage runner and harvests the resulting ProjectData via `CoverageDataManager` reflection ŌĆö line hits, jump branches, switch cases, and per-method rollups; `get_file_coverage` re-reads that cached snapshot for one class without rerunning. All run paths route through `RunInvocation` for leak-free disposal and reflectively flip `CoverageOptionsProvider.setOptionsToReplace(int)` to suppress the IDE's replace-or-append dialog before launch (restored in `finally`). REGISTRATION: gated by `ToolRegistrationFilter.shouldRegisterCoverageTool(ideContext)` which requires `edition == ULTIMATE || edition == PROFESSIONAL` ŌĆö NOT just Java plugin presence. On IntelliJ IDEA Community or PyCharm Community, this tool is NOT registered even with the Java plugin installed; the coverage runner ships only with Ultimate/Professional editions.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("What to do when a previous coverage suite is already active ŌĆö for run_with_coverage. 'replace' (default): silently swap in the new run's data ŌĆö fresh measurement. 'append' / 'add' / 'merge': merge into existing suite ŌĆö use for incremental coverage across many classes. 'ignore': silently discard the new run's data. 'ask': surface IntelliJ's native merge dialog (will block the agent until the user clicks). Default 'replace' prevents the merge dialog from freezing the agent loop.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Which IntelliJ policy to use when there's already coverage data from a prior run. The IDE normally pops a 'replace or append' modal ŌĆö this param suppresses it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("Whole class runs under coverage; tool returns test outcome + line/branch % for every class touched. `lastSnapshot` is populated for follow-up `get_file_coverage` calls.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("`lastSnapshot` is in-memory only ŌĆö restarting the plugin/IDE wipes it. `get_file_coverage` will retry one extraction from the live `CoverageDataManager` but cannot recover snapshots from killed sessions.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("`run_with_coverage` and `get_file_coverage` are bound by a single-tool cache (`lastSnapshot`, `@Volatile`). Tool instances are session-scoped; cross-session coverage is invisible to `get_file_coverage` unless the IDE's `CoverageDataManager` still holds a current suite. Worth flagging in the description.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("`run_with_coverage` returns the test outcome AND the coverage block in one `ToolResult`. There is no way to suppress the test stdout ŌĆö high-output tests will exercise the spill path (`ToolOutputConfig.COMMAND` cap=100K) and the LLM will read the spill file via `read_file`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("`timeoutSeconds` is coerced to [1, 900]; the suspendable launch is wrapped in `withTimeoutOrNull(timeoutSeconds * 1000)`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CoverageTool.kt$CoverageTool$("a `run_with_coverage` call must have populated `lastSnapshot`, OR the IDE's CoverageDataManager must still hold a current suites bundle from a prior IDE-side run")
