ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Approval is required (`ApprovalPolicy.SESSION_APPROVABLE`) ŌĆö the user can grant 'allow for session', but the first call always pauses the loop for human confirmation. In headless / CI-style usage this is a friction point.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Auto-creates parent directories without prompting. Convenient when intentional, but a typo in `path` (`src/maim/...` instead of `src/main/...`) will silently produce a stray directory tree that the user has to clean up manually.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Bug fixed ŌĆö two correctness issues surfaced by Phase 5 tool-docs swarm (Batch 3): (1) the I/O fallback now uses EncodingProjectManager.defaultCharset instead of hardcoded UTF-8, eliminating the charset asymmetry on non-UTF-8 projects; (2) overwrite=true now reads existing content and computes a real unified diff (existing ŌåÆ new) for the approval modal instead of always showing \"\" ŌåÆ new content.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Bytes are written verbatim via VirtualFile.setBinaryContent (using the file's detected charset) or, on the fallback path, `File.writeText(EncodingProjectManager.getInstance(project).defaultCharset)`. Both paths use the same project-level charset so bytes are consistent regardless of which write path triggers.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Create a new file (or overwrite-on-flag) at a project-relative or absolute path. Routes through PathValidator (project root + `{agentDir}/memory/` only), auto-creates missing parent directories via VfsUtil/mkdirs, writes via WriteCommandAction so the change is undoable from the IDE's Edit menu, and falls back to direct java.io.File + LocalFileSystem.refresh if the VFS path is unavailable. Refuses to clobber existing files unless `overwrite=true`. Both write paths (VFS and I/O fallback) use the project's configured charset via EncodingProjectManager.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Defaults to false ŌĆö the call errors if the target file already exists, protecting the user from accidental clobber.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Empty content produces a 0-byte file rather than erroring. Sometimes useful (placeholder files, .gitkeep), occasionally surprising.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Forgets that a file with that path already exists, calls without `overwrite=true`, gets the `file exists` error, then panics and retries with overwrite ŌĆö when the right move was edit_file all along.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("If true, an existing file at `path` is replaced. The approval gate shows a real unified diff between the prior content and the new content so reviewers can see exactly what changes. If false (default), an existing file causes the call to error out with a `file exists` message that nudges toward edit_file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Like hitting File ŌåÆ New ŌåÆ Save in your editor: type a path, type the contents, and the file appears on disk and in IntelliJ's project view. Won't quietly stomp a file that already exists ŌĆö you have to explicitly say 'yes, replace it'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("MUST be the entire intended file body ŌĆö placeholders like `// ... unchanged ...` end up literally on disk")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("One-line human-readable rationale shown in the user's approval dialog. Not written to the file. Required so the user has context when granting approval.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Path is canonicalised, validated against project + memory roots, and the file is created at that location. Missing parent directories are created automatically (mkdirs / VfsUtil.createDirectoryIfMissing).")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Plan mode blocks this tool entirely (it's in `WRITE_TOOLS`). Even an LLM that hallucinates a create_file call in plan mode will be rejected at the AgentLoop execution guard.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Reaches for create_file with `overwrite=true` to make a small change to an existing file ŌĆö re-writing all 800 lines instead of using edit_file. Wastes output tokens, defeats the per-edit diff that ships to the approval dialog, and makes the change impossible to review at a glance.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Safety latch. False (default) means 'I expect this file to be brand new ŌĆö fail if it isn't'. True means 'I really do want to replace whatever is on disk with the content I'm about to send'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("The content to write to the file. ALWAYS provide the COMPLETE intended content of the file, without any truncation or omissions. You MUST include ALL parts of the file, even if they haven't been modified.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("The full text of the file. Whatever you put here is exactly what hits disk ŌĆö no template, no merge, no comment-stripping. Line endings and trailing newlines are preserved verbatim; the tool does not normalise CRLF to LF.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Tries to `create_file` outside the project ŌĆö most often into `/tmp/`, the user home, or a sibling repo to 'stage' something. PathValidator rejects with 'path outside project'; the only out-of-project root that works is `{agentDir}/memory/` for agent memory files.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Truncates content with `// ... rest of file unchanged` placeholders. The description spells out 'COMPLETE intended content' and 'NO truncation', but the LLM still does it; the result is a file with literal `ŌĆ”` ellipses on disk that breaks the next compile.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Where the new file should land. Relative paths are resolved against the project root; absolute paths are accepted but must canonicalise back into the project (or `{agentDir}/memory/`).")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("Writes through the `description` parameter into the approval dialog ŌĆö but the parameter shares its name with the tool's own `description` LLM-facing field, which is a recurring source of authoring confusion when reading the source.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("`overwrite=true` could plausibly be split into a separate `overwrite_file` tool ŌĆö keeping `create_file` strictly for new-file creation would tighten the description and eliminate the 'reach for overwrite instead of edit_file' failure mode.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("edit_file", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use this instead when you're modifying an EXISTING file ŌĆö edit_file produces a targeted diff, preserves unrelated content, and is the right tool for nearly every change to an already-tracked file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("glob_files", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Run before create_file when generating multiple files in a directory ŌĆö confirms the directory layout and catches name collisions in advance.")
ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("must resolve inside the project root or `{agentDir}/memory/` ŌĆö anywhere else is rejected before any I/O")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("read_file", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use after a create_file to confirm what landed on disk, especially before chaining further edits ŌĆö read_file sees unsaved Document state, so it confirms the IDE picked up the change.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileTool.kt$CreateFileTool$("revert_file", Relationship.FALLBACK, "Use to undo a botched create_file before any further work touches the file. Pairs with the LocalHistory checkpoint create_file leaves behind.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileToolTest.kt$CreateFileToolTest$(text.contains("MemoryIndex.onMemoryFileCreated"), "CreateFileTool must call MemoryIndex.onMemoryFileCreated after a successful memory-dir write")
ArgumentListWrapping:CreateFileToolTest.kt$CreateFileToolTest$(text.contains("memoryAutoIndexEnabled"), "CreateFileTool must respect the memoryAutoIndexEnabled kill switch")

ArgumentListWrapping:CurrentTimeTool.kt$CurrentTimeTool$("Calls current_time as a no-op stall while thinking, instead of using `think`. Tool-call audit shows clusters of current_time invocations preceding actual work.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CurrentTimeTool.kt$CurrentTimeTool$("Like glancing at the clock on the wall ŌĆö the agent gets back today's date, the current time in two zones, and what day of the week it is. That's it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CurrentTimeTool.kt$CurrentTimeTool$("No monotonic-clock variant. If the LLM tried to use this for elapsed-time measurements across two calls, NTP adjustments or DST transitions could yield negative deltas. Not what the tool is for, but the description doesn't warn against it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CurrentTimeTool.kt$CurrentTimeTool$("Re-calls current_time multiple times in the same session for ~~freshness~~ ŌĆö wasted iterations, since wall-clock drift across an agent turn is meaningless for sprint/deadline reasoning.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CurrentTimeTool.kt$CurrentTimeTool$("Registered as a deferred tool under 'Utilities' ŌĆö invisible until the LLM `tool_search`es for it. For a tool this cheap, the discovery friction probably costs more in extra `tool_search` calls than the schema would cost in the core set.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CurrentTimeTool.kt$CurrentTimeTool$("Returns the JVM-local timezone (whatever the IDE inherits from the OS), not the user's preferred reporting zone ŌĆö there's no `tz` param. A user in PST sees `-08:00`; the same code running on a CI box in UTC sees `+00:00`. The UTC field is always present as an unambiguous anchor.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CurrentTimeTool.kt$CurrentTimeTool$("Returns the current wall-clock time as four lines: ISO-8601 offset-date-time in the JVM's local zone (`Local:`), the same instant in UTC (`UTC:`), the day-of-week name (`Day:`), and the IANA timezone id (`Timezone:`, e.g. `Europe/London`). No parameters; reads `ZonedDateTime.now()` directly with no caching.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CurrentTimeTool.kt$CurrentTimeTool$("Schema cost is ~0 (zero parameters) yet the tool sits in the deferred tier. Promoting to core would save the `tool_search` round-trip whenever the LLM needs a timestamp ŌĆö worth measuring.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CurrentTimeTool.kt$CurrentTimeTool$("Uses the local `Local:` field for log/sprint comparisons against UTC timestamps from Jira/Bamboo, producing off-by-N-hours errors. The `UTC:` field is right there in the same output but the LLM picks the more familiar local one.")

ArgumentListWrapping:CurrentTimeTool.kt$CurrentTimeTool$("run_command", Relationship.FALLBACK, "If current_time were unavailable, `run_command date -u +%FT%TZ` is the closest UTC-only substitute on Unix; on Windows the format differs.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$( "Error: at least one of 'offset' or 'line' must be provided", "Error: missing position", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true )

ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("Added to `document.getLineStartOffset(line - 1)` to derive the character offset. Clamped to the line's end offset if it overshoots.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("Converted to a 0-based offset via `document.getLineStartOffset(line - 1) + (column - 1)`. Clamped to the line's end offset if the column overshoots.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("Defaults to column 1 ŌĆö the start of the line ŌĆö which may land on whitespace or the wrong token if the expression starts mid-line. Always provide column when you know it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("File is located via `LocalFileSystem`, parsed into a `PsiFile`, and dispatched to `JavaKotlinProvider.analyzeDataflow` once the offset resolves to a leaf PSI element.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("If `line` is also absent, the tool returns an error: 'at least one of offset or line must be provided'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("If `offset` is also absent, the tool returns an error. If `offset` IS present, `line` is ignored entirely.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("Used directly as the PSI leaf-element lookup offset. Bounds-checked against `psiFile.textLength`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("diagnostics", Relationship.FALLBACK, "When dataflow_analysis is unavailable (Kotlin file, Python file, dumb mode), `diagnostics` surfaces the IDE's already-computed nullability warnings and constant-condition warnings as diagnostic messages. Less precise (no range or constant value), but covers Kotlin and Python.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("find_definition", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "find_definition anchors the declaration of the variable or method whose return type you want to analyse; dataflow_analysis then runs at usage sites to answer 'what does it actually hold at runtime' (which may differ from the declared type, e.g., NOT_NULL despite a nullable return type).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("find_references", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Run find_references first to locate every assignment site for a field or parameter, then run dataflow_analysis at each site to determine what value is being assigned. Together they answer 'who writes this value and what range of values does it take'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("must be a `.java` file ŌĆö `.kt` and `.kts` are rejected with an explicit error message before provider dispatch")

ArgumentListWrapping:DataFlowAnalysisTool.kt$DataFlowAnalysisTool$("type_inference", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "type_inference tells you the DECLARED type of an expression (e.g., 'String?'); dataflow_analysis tells you the NARROWED type in context (e.g., NOT_NULL because the branch already checked for null). Use both when the declared type is nullable but you suspect the IDE knows it's always non-null here.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Auto-injected by AgentLoop because `db_explain` is in `OUTPUT_FILTERABLE_TOOLS`. A second-stage line filter applied after the plan is rendered ŌĆö useful for grep-ing 'Seq Scan', 'Index Scan', 'Hash Join', etc., out of a multi-page plan.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Auto-injected by AgentLoop. When `true`, the full plan is written to `{sessionDir}/tool-output/db_explain-<epoch>-output.txt` and the LLM gets a head-20 + tail-10 preview plus the file path. Useful for huge nested-loop plans you want to grep through later.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Connects to `DatabaseProfile.resolvedDefaultDatabase` (the database configured in the profile dialog).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Cost estimates use the engine's cached statistics (`pg_stats`, `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.STATISTICS`, etc.) which can be wildly stale on a database that hasn't been ANALYZED/OPTIMIZED recently. A plan claiming `rows=10` may actually scan 10M ŌĆö only `analyze=true` (Postgres) reveals the truth.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Defaults to `false` ŌĆö runs plain `EXPLAIN <sql>`, which on every supported engine returns cost estimates from cached statistics WITHOUT executing the SELECT. Fast and cheap; data may be stale if `ANALYZE`/`OPTIMIZE TABLE` hasn't run recently.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Doesn't realize that `database` parameter is silently ignored for SQLite/Generic profiles ŌĆö passes `database=\"analytics\"` against a SQLite profile thinking the EXPLAIN runs on a different file; in reality it runs against whatever file the profile's URL points at.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Each call opens a fresh JDBC connection (no pool reuse) ŌĆö fine for one-off calls, but pays connect overhead per invocation.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Forgets that on PostgreSQL `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` actually executes the statement to gather runtime stats ŌĆö believes it's a 'dry run'. Safety-wise it's fine here (the connection rolls back), but the LLM sometimes uses `analyze=true` on a query the user said 'don't run yet' and is surprised when expensive computation happens. The plain `analyze=false` path is a true cost-only estimate and doesn't execute.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Forwarded to `DatabaseProfile.jdbcUrlFor(database)`, which substitutes the database segment of the JDBC URL for PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQL Server profiles. The new URL is opened for this single call only ŌĆö the saved profile is not mutated.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Hard `queryTimeout=30s` ŌĆö for `analyze=true` on a slow query this can fire mid-execution and return a JDBC timeout instead of a partial plan. The LLM should pre-narrow with WHERE before asking for ANALYZE.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("If true, save full output to a file and return a preview with the file path. Use for large outputs you may need to search later. Read the file with read_file or search_code.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Lets one server-engine profile reach any database on that server. Useful when the profile defaults to `postgres` but the query targets `myapp`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Like running `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` in psql or `EXPLAIN FORMAT=JSON` in MySQL ŌĆö shows the agent how the database intends to (or did) run the query: which indexes it picked, which scans it used, and how many rows it estimated vs. actually saw. Same read-only guards as db_query, so even when ANALYZE actually executes the SELECT to gather runtime stats, nothing gets written back.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Lines of the rendered plan that don't match the regex are dropped before the LLM sees them; filtering happens before spill, so it also shrinks any spilled file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("On PostgreSQL: switches the wrap to `EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS, FORMAT TEXT) <sql>`, which actually runs the SELECT to gather `actual time`, `rows`, and buffer-hit stats. The result includes `Planning Time` and `Execution Time` lines. The `autoCommit=false` + always-rollback pattern guarantees no writes survive even though the query was 'really' executed. On non-Postgres engines: the flag is silently ignored and a plain `EXPLAIN` runs.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Optional database name on the profile's server. Defaults to the profile's default database. Use db_list_databases to see what's available. Ignored for SQLite/Generic profiles.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Pastes the EXPLAIN keyword into the `sql` parameter ŌĆö `db_explain(sql=\"EXPLAIN SELECT ...\")` ŌĆö producing `EXPLAIN EXPLAIN SELECT ...` on the wire, which is a JDBC syntax error. The contract is to pass the bare SELECT and let the tool prepend EXPLAIN.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Plan output is engine-specific text ŌĆö Postgres returns a TEXT tree, MySQL returns a tabular EXPLAIN, SQL Server returns a flat estimate, SQLite returns `id|parent|notused|detail` rows. The LLM has to know each format to interpret the output; there's no normalization layer.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Reads a Postgres plan with `Seq Scan on big_table (cost=0.00..1234.00 rows=1000)` and concludes 'this is fine' without checking that the planner-estimated `rows=1000` matches reality ŌĆö only `analyze=true` shows the `actual rows=N` count. Cost-only plans are estimates from cached statistics that may be stale (run `ANALYZE` on the table first to refresh).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Regex pattern to filter output lines. Only lines matching this pattern are returned. Use when you only need specific information from a potentially large output.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Resolved through `lookupDbProfile` ŌĆö manual profile ŌåÆ `DbProfileLookup.Found`; IDE-managed data source ŌåÆ `DbProfileLookup.IdeManaged` (returns an explanatory error because IDE-stored credentials are not reachable from the agent); unknown id ŌåÆ `DbProfileLookup.NotFound` with a hint to call `db_list_profiles`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Returns the engine's query plan for a single SELECT statement. Reuses `DatabaseConnectionManager.validateReadOnly` (rejects DML/DDL by prefix), opens a short-lived `autoCommit=false`+`isReadOnly=true` connection, and runs `EXPLAIN <sql>` (or `EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS, FORMAT TEXT) <sql>` on PostgreSQL when `analyze=true`). Plan rows are joined with newlines and rendered as a fenced code block; the formatted output is always passed through `spillOrFormat` so multi-page plans are auto-spilled to `{sessionDir}/tool-output/`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Runs db_explain on a non-SELECT ŌĆö INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE ŌĆö assuming EXPLAIN is itself a 'safe wrapper' that prevents execution. The tool blocks this at `validateReadOnly` because the prefix is INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE; never even reaches the EXPLAIN keyword. The LLM occasionally retries with `EXPLAIN UPDATE ...` as the SQL value (which would technically be a SELECT-ish prefix on some engines), but that still fails because the prefix check only inspects the first keyword and `EXPLAIN` itself is not in the allow-list ŌĆö meaning even `EXPLAIN SELECT ...` would be blocked if passed verbatim. The contract is: pass the bare SELECT, the tool wraps it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Sets `analyze=true` on a non-PostgreSQL profile expecting MySQL-style `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` (8.0.18+) ŌĆö but the tool only injects the `(ANALYZE, BUFFERS, FORMAT TEXT)` flags when `dbType == POSTGRESQL`. For MySQL/SQL Server/SQLite the `analyze` flag is silently ignored and a plain `EXPLAIN` runs, producing only cost estimates. The LLM then misreads cost-only output as actual runtime stats.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("The bare SELECT ŌĆö do NOT prefix it with `EXPLAIN`; the tool wraps it. Engine dialect is the LLM's responsibility (Postgres `LIMIT/OFFSET`, SQL Server `TOP`/`OFFSET FETCH`, etc.).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Toggles between cost-only estimation (false, fast, no execution) and actual runtime stats (true, slow, executes the query). On PostgreSQL only ŌĆö silently ignored on MySQL/SQL Server/SQLite.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("When true, runs EXPLAIN ANALYZE (PostgreSQL only: includes BUFFERS). The statement is actually executed but immediately rolled back ŌĆö data is safe. Defaults to false.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("Which configured database connection to use ŌĆö same picker semantics as db_query. The id comes from `db_list_profiles`; manual profiles live in `Settings ŌåÆ Tools ŌåÆ Workflow Orchestrator ŌåÆ Agent ŌåÆ Database Profiles`, and IDE-managed data sources are surfaced read-only with their displayName.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("`analyze=true` actually executes the SELECT (Postgres 'EXPLAIN ANALYZE' runs the query to collect runtime stats). The connection's `autoCommit=false` + always-rollback contract makes this safe for SELECT (no writes survive), but a side-effecting function call inside the SELECT (e.g. `SELECT my_logging_proc()`) WOULD execute. The blocked-prefix gate doesn't inspect function calls ŌĆö only the first keyword.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("`analyze=true` is PostgreSQL-only ŌĆö silently ignored on MySQL/SQL Server/SQLite. The LLM gets a plain EXPLAIN back and may not realize the runtime stats it asked for are missing. No warning, no error.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("`spillOrFormat` is still called (every db_explain result goes through it) but only auto-spills when content exceeds 30K chars. Smaller plans are inlined verbatim.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("`spillOrFormat` writes the full content to disk and returns a preview; `ToolResult.spillPath` carries the path so the LLM can re-read it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("`validateReadOnly(sql)` strips leading whitespace, uppercases, and rejects any statement starting with one of the 14 blocked prefixes (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DROP/CREATE/ALTER/TRUNCATE/REPLACE/MERGE/GRANT/REVOKE/EXEC/EXECUTE/CALL/DO). If accepted, the tool prepends `EXPLAIN` (or `EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS, FORMAT TEXT)` on Postgres + `analyze=true`) and runs it with `queryTimeout=30s`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("db_list_databases", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Call before db_explain when the EXPLAIN target lives in a non-default database on a server-engine profile.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("db_list_profiles", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Required first call when the LLM doesn't yet know which profile id to pass.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("db_query", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Identify a slow query via db_query, then run db_explain on the same SQL to see why ŌĆö Seq Scan vs Index Scan, missing where clauses, bad row estimates.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("db_schema", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Read the table's indexes via db_schema before db_explain so you know which indexes the planner could have picked but didn't.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("db_stats", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Use for table-level row counts and storage size ŌĆö sometimes the cheaper answer to 'why is this slow?' is 'the table is 50GB'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("filters the rendered Markdown output (which includes the SQL fence and plan fence), not the raw plan rows ŌĆö pattern must tolerate the fenced-code-block markers")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("first non-whitespace keyword must NOT be one of: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, CREATE, ALTER, TRUNCATE, REPLACE, MERGE, GRANT, REVOKE, EXEC, EXECUTE, CALL, DO")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("ignored for SQLite (the URL points at a file) and Generic (the user supplies the URL verbatim) ŌĆö no error, no warning, just silently uses the profile's URL as-is")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("must NOT be pre-wrapped with `EXPLAIN` ŌĆö the tool prepends it; doing so produces `EXPLAIN EXPLAIN <sql>` which is a syntax error")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("must match an existing manual profile ŌĆö IDE-managed data sources are surfaced but not queryable from the agent (their credentials live in IntelliJ's encrypted store, not PasswordSafe)")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("queryTimeout=30s applies ŌĆö for long-running queries, ANALYZE may exceed this; reduce the WHERE clause first")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbExplainTool.kt$DbExplainTool$("when true, the SELECT really executes ŌĆö same wall-clock cost as running it via db_query, but with rollback")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("Connection cost: opens a JDBC connection just to run one discovery query. Fine for occasional use but expensive if the LLM re-calls every turn (see llmMistake about caching).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("Depends on connection grants ŌĆö many DB users in production lack permission to list all databases (e.g. a least-privilege Postgres role). The tool surfaces driver errors but cannot recover.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("Hard error on SQLite/Generic profiles instead of gracefully returning the single profile-bound database. Forces the LLM to encode a 'check dbType before calling' rule that other db_* tools don't impose.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("Redundant when the profile pins a single database (the common case for app-scoped profiles). The `*(default)*` marker is the only useful info, and `db_list_profiles` already shows the same field.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("System-database filtering (`pg_toast`, `information_schema`, `mysql.*`, etc.) is hand-rolled in `DatabaseDiscovery.filterSystemDatabases` ŌĆö a new engine or a new system DB convention will silently leak through until the allowlist is updated.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("db_list_profiles", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Call first to discover which servers exist; this tool then lists databases on the chosen server. Different concerns: profiles = config, databases = runtime discovery.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("db_query", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Once a database is chosen, run actual queries against it with db_query(profile, database=\"<name>\", sql=\"...\").")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("db_schema", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Drill into a discovered database with db_schema(profile, database, schema?, table?) to see schemas ŌåÆ tables ŌåÆ columns.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("db_stats", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Same conceptual layer ŌĆö discovery/inspection of an existing database; both are read-only and depend on a resolved profile.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("must point at a server-type engine (Postgres / MySQL / SQL Server) ŌĆö SQLite and Generic JDBC profiles return an error directing the LLM to db_schema instead")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListDatabasesTool.kt$DbListDatabasesTool$("must resolve to a manually-configured profile ŌĆö IDE-managed Database tool window data sources error out because their credentials aren't accessible to the agent")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbListProfilesTool.kt$DbListProfilesTool$("\n\n_IDE profiles are discovered from IntelliJ's Database tool window. Credentials are managed there ŌĆö not here._")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListProfilesTool.kt$DbListProfilesTool$("db_explain", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Also takes a profile id as its first parameter; use db_list_profiles to resolve the id before calling db_explain on a slow query.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListProfilesTool.kt$DbListProfilesTool$("db_list_databases", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "For server-type profiles (Postgres/MySQL/SQL Server), follow up with db_list_databases(profile=...) to discover all databases on that server. Distinct concern: profiles = config, databases = runtime discovery.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListProfilesTool.kt$DbListProfilesTool$("db_query", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Call db_list_profiles first to get a valid profile id, then pass it to db_query(profile=...) to run SELECT queries.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbListProfilesTool.kt$DbListProfilesTool$("db_schema", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use the profile id from db_list_profiles with db_schema to inspect table/column structure before writing queries.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbListProfilesTool.kt$DbListProfilesTool$("db_stats", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Also takes a profile id; db_list_profiles is the prerequisite for any db_stats call too.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("30s queryTimeout is non-configurable ŌĆö long analytical queries get killed mid-flight with a JDBC timeout error. Pre-aggregate or narrow before calling.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("A one-liner shown to the user in the approval dialog so they understand WHY the agent wants to run this query ŌĆö like a commit message for a single SELECT. Skipping it leaves the user staring at raw SQL with no context, which makes denial more likely.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Auto-injected by AgentLoop because `db_query` is in `OUTPUT_FILTERABLE_TOOLS`. A second-stage line filter applied AFTER the Markdown table is built ŌĆö like piping `db_query | grep <grep_pattern>`. Useful for narrowing a wide multi-column result to just the rows mentioning a specific value, without rewriting the SQL.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Auto-injected by AgentLoop. When `true`, the full Markdown table is written to `{sessionDir}/tool-output/db_query-<epoch>-output.txt` and the LLM gets a head-20 + tail-10 preview plus the file path. Pattern: query once broad, then `read_file`/`search_code` against the spilled file instead of re-running the SELECT.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Cell values truncated at 500 chars with a `ŌĆ”` suffix ŌĆö large JSON/TEXT/BLOB columns appear clipped, and the LLM may parse the truncated value as if it were complete.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Connects to `DatabaseProfile.resolvedDefaultDatabase` (the database configured in the profile dialog).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Each call opens a fresh JDBC connection (no pool reuse) ŌĆö fine for one-off agent queries, but bursting many db_query calls in a row pays connect overhead each time and can hit per-DB connection limits on shared dev databases.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Executes a single read-only SQL SELECT against a configured `DatabaseProfile` over a short-lived JDBC connection. Defence in depth: prefix-based statement allow-list (rejects INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL/DCL/EXEC/CALL before any I/O), `Connection.isReadOnly=true`, `autoCommit=false` with rollback on close, and `Statement.queryTimeout=30s`. Results are rendered as a Markdown table truncated at 200 rows ├Ś 500 chars/cell; the formatted output is always passed through `spillOrFormat` so large result sets are auto-spilled to `{sessionDir}/tool-output/`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Forgets to fill the `description` param ŌĆö the user's approval dialog then shows an empty 'why is the agent running this?' line, increasing the chance of the user denying the call. Description is optional in the schema but high-leverage in practice.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Forwarded to `DatabaseProfile.jdbcUrlFor(database)`, which substitutes the database segment of the JDBC URL for PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQL Server profiles. The new URL is opened for this single call only ŌĆö the saved profile is not mutated.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Hard 200-row cap with no `limit` parameter ŌĆö the LLM can't ask for more without rewriting the SQL with explicit `LIMIT` / `OFFSET` pagination. Large analytical results are silently truncated.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("IDE-managed data sources (the ones that show up in IntelliJ's Database tool window) are listed but not queryable ŌĆö their credentials live in IntelliJ's encrypted store and are not exposed via reflection. Users have to re-enter them as a manual profile.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("If true, save full output to a file and return a preview with the file path. Use for large outputs you may need to search later. Read the file with read_file or search_code.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Lets one server-engine profile reach any database on that server ŌĆö the same way DBeaver lets you switch databases under a single connection. Useful when your Postgres profile defaults to `postgres` but the app's data is in `myapp`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Like opening your IDE's database tool window, typing a SELECT query, and hitting Run ŌĆö except the agent is the one driving. The tool refuses anything that would change the data, only opens a read-only connection, and caps results at 200 rows so a runaway `SELECT *` can't drown the chat.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Lines of the rendered Markdown table that don't match the regex are dropped before the LLM sees them; filtering happens before spill, so it also shrinks any spilled file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Mixes engine dialects ŌĆö uses `LIMIT 10 OFFSET 20` against SQL Server (which wants `OFFSET ... FETCH NEXT`), or `TOP 10` against PostgreSQL. The blocked-prefix validator does not catch dialect errors, so the call returns a JDBC syntax error and the LLM has to retry. Should call `db_list_profiles` first to learn `dbType`, or `db_schema` to confirm the engine.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("No second-stage filter ŌĆö the full (200-row-capped) Markdown table is returned subject to ToolOutputSpiller.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("No support for prepared statements / bind parameters ŌĆö the LLM concatenates values into the SQL string, so a maliciously crafted user-supplied value in the prompt could in principle be SQL-injected into the query the agent runs. Mitigated by read-only enforcement (worst case: data exfiltration via crafted SELECT, not data corruption).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Optional database name on the profile's server. Defaults to the profile's default database. Use db_list_databases to see what's available. Ignored for SQLite/Generic profiles.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Pastes the database name into `sql` (e.g. `SELECT * FROM analytics.events`) AND also passes `database='analytics'` ŌĆö works on MySQL/Postgres, but on SQL Server the cross-database reference may fail because the JDBC connection is bound to a specific catalog after `database=` is applied.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Prefix-based read-only check is conservative ŌĆö `WITH ŌĆ” SELECT` (CTE) works because it starts with `WITH` (not blocked), but `WITH ŌĆ” INSERT` and `WITH ŌĆ” UPDATE` would also slip past the prefix gate. Defence in depth (`isReadOnly=true` + `autoCommit=false` + final rollback) is what actually stops writes; a misbehaving driver that ignores `isReadOnly` would be the failure mode.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Regex pattern to filter output lines. Only lines matching this pattern are returned. Use when you only need specific information from a potentially large output.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Resolved through `lookupDbProfile` ŌĆö manual profile (Settings UI) ŌåÆ `DbProfileLookup.Found`; IDE-managed data source ŌåÆ `DbProfileLookup.IdeManaged` (returns an explanatory error because IDE-stored credentials are not reachable from the agent); unknown id ŌåÆ `DbProfileLookup.NotFound` with a hint to call `db_list_profiles`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Skips `db_list_databases` and queries against the profile's default DB when the data lives elsewhere on the same server (e.g. a Postgres profile defaulting to `postgres` when the app DB is `myapp`). Symptom is an empty result or a 'relation does not exist' error.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("The actual SQL ŌĆö must start with SELECT (or WITH ŌĆ” SELECT). Anything else (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DROP/CREATE/ALTER/TRUNCATE/REPLACE/MERGE/GRANT/REVOKE/EXEC/EXECUTE/CALL/DO) is rejected before the connection is even opened. Engine dialect (PG vs MySQL vs SQL Server vs SQLite) is the LLM's responsibility ŌĆö there's no portability shim.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Tries DML hoping it'll slip through ŌĆö `UPDATE orders SET status='X' WHERE id=1`. Blocked at the prefix check with 'Blocked: \\'UPDATE\\' statements are not permitted.' The LLM occasionally re-tries by quoting the statement or wrapping in a CTE; both still fail because the validator only inspects the first keyword.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("WITH recent AS (SELECT * FROM logs WHERE ts > now() - interval '1 hour') SELECT level, count(*) FROM recent GROUP BY level")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Which configured database connection to use ŌĆö like picking a saved connection in DBeaver. The ID comes from `db_list_profiles`; manual profiles live in `Settings ŌåÆ Tools ŌåÆ Workflow Orchestrator ŌåÆ Agent ŌåÆ Database Profiles` (XML-backed via `DatabaseSettings`), and IDE-managed data sources are surfaced read-only with their displayName.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("Writes `SELECT * FROM <big_table>` with no WHERE/LIMIT ŌĆö the table has 5M rows but the result is silently truncated at 200; the LLM treats those 200 as representative and reasons from a biased sample. Should always pair broad SELECTs with `LIMIT`, `WHERE`, or an aggregate.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("`database` parameter is silently ignored for SQLite and Generic profiles ŌĆö no error, no warning. The LLM may believe it's switching databases when it isn't.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("`spillOrFormat` is still called (every db_query result goes through it) but only auto-spills when content exceeds 30K chars. Smaller results are inlined verbatim.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("`spillOrFormat` writes the full content to disk and returns a preview; `ToolResult.spillPath` carries the path so the LLM can re-read it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("`validateReadOnly(sql)` strips leading whitespace, uppercases, and rejects any statement starting with one of the 14 blocked prefixes. If accepted, runs against the profile's connection with `queryTimeout=30s` and the result is converted to a Markdown table via `resultSetToMarkdown` (200 rows max, 500 chars per cell, ellipsis-truncated).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("at most 200 rows are materialized; rows beyond that are silently dropped with a `_Results truncated at 200 rows._` footer")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("db_explain", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "After a slow db_query, use db_explain on the same SQL to see the engine's query plan and find the missing index.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("db_list_databases", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Call before db_query when the profile is a server engine and you need to discover databases beyond the default.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("db_list_profiles", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Call first when you don't know which profiles exist ŌĆö db_query needs a valid profile id to do anything.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("db_schema", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Call before db_query to learn table/column names ŌĆö saves a round-trip of guess-then-fix on column names.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("db_stats", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Use for table-level row count / size summaries; cheaper than `SELECT count(*) FROM ŌĆ”` on large tables.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("each cell is truncated to 500 chars with a `ŌĆ”` suffix ŌĆö large TEXT/BLOB columns will appear clipped")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("filters Markdown output, not SQL ŌĆö so it sees the table border lines (`| --- |`), the header row, and the trailing `_N row(s) returned._`. Pattern must tolerate or skip those")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("first non-whitespace keyword must NOT be one of: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, CREATE, ALTER, TRUNCATE, REPLACE, MERGE, GRANT, REVOKE, EXEC, EXECUTE, CALL, DO")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("ignored for SQLite (the URL points at a file) and Generic (the user supplies the URL verbatim)")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("must be a database that exists on the server ŌĆö typos return a JDBC connection error, not a friendly 'use db_list_databases' hint")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("must match an existing manual profile ŌĆö IDE-managed data sources are surfaced but not queryable from the agent (their credentials live in IntelliJ's encrypted store, not PasswordSafe)")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTool.kt$DbQueryTool$("queryTimeout=30s ŌĆö long-running analytical queries WILL be killed; pre-aggregate or narrow the WHERE clause")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTruncationMarkerTest.kt$DbQueryTruncationMarkerTest$(table.contains("3 cell value(s) truncated"), "Expected count of 3 in footer, got:\n$table")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTruncationMarkerTest.kt$DbQueryTruncationMarkerTest$(table.contains("line one line two") || table.contains("line one line two"), "Expected newline to be space-replaced, got:\n$table")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbQueryTruncationMarkerTest.kt$DbQueryTruncationMarkerTest$(table.lines().any { it == "line two |" }, "Expected newline to be replaced with space; got:\n$table")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Calls `db_schema(profile=\"qa\", table=\"orders\")` without `schema` ŌĆö the tool silently treats it as Level 1 (list schemas) because the table-describe path requires schema != null. The LLM then re-invokes with the right combo, wasting a turn.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Confuses `database` with `schema`. On Postgres, `database` selects the catalog (`mydb` vs `analytics` on the same cluster) while `schema` is the namespace within it (`public`, `reporting`). LLMs often pass the schema name as `database` and get back an empty schema list.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Constructs a `db_query(\"SELECT col1, col2 FROM orders\")` from memory without first running `db_schema` ŌĆö column names drift from the real schema and the query fails. The fix is to read schema first; the recurring miss suggests the system prompt needs a stronger 'always inspect before querying' nudge.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("DatabaseConnectionManager.withConnection routes the JDBC URL to the named database, overriding the profile's default.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Drills straight to Level 3 (table describe) on a fresh profile without verifying the table exists in that schema ŌĆö gets back a result with empty column rows (no error, just an empty markdown table) which the LLM then misreads as 'table has no columns'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Foreign-key and index queries are wrapped in `runCatching {}` ŌĆö drivers that throw on these calls (some Generic JDBC profiles) silently produce a partial result rather than reporting the failure.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Hierarchically inspect a JDBC profile's structure: with profile only it lists schemas, with schema it lists tables/views, and with schema+table it returns columns, PKs, FKs, and grouped indexes via DatabaseMetaData.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Level 1 system-schema filter is a hand-rolled allowlist (pg_catalog, information_schema, pg_toast, sys, dbo, guest, plus prefix matches for pg_temp_, pg_toast_temp_, db_). New Postgres extensions or custom audit schemas starting with 'db_' will be filtered out unintentionally.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Level 3 (describe table) silently returns an empty columns table when (schema, table) doesn't exist. No 'table not found' error ŌĆö the LLM may mistake an empty result for 'this table has no columns'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Level 3: calls getColumns / getPrimaryKeys / getImportedKeys / getIndexInfo for the (schema, table) pair and renders markdown.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Like running `\\d` in psql or `DESCRIBE` in MySQL ŌĆö drills from 'what databases live here?' down to 'what columns does this table have?' without forcing the agent to know each engine's catalog dialect.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Limited to whatever the JDBC driver exposes via DatabaseMetaData. Engine-specific features ŌĆö generated columns, computed columns, check constraints, partitioning, table comments, GIN/GiST/BRIN index types, exclusion constraints, materialized views ŌĆö are not surfaced.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("On MySQL, calls Level 1 expecting a schema list and gets back a redirection message ('MySQL uses databases as schemas ŌĆö use db_list_databases'). MySQL has no separate schema concept, so the tool short-circuits.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("On a multi-database server (e.g. one Postgres cluster hosting `mydb` AND `analytics`), this picks which one to inspect. SQLite has only one database per file, so it's irrelevant there.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Optional database name on the profile's server. Defaults to the profile's default database. Use db_list_databases to see what's available. Ignored for SQLite/Generic profiles.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Output for a wide table can be large ŌĆö gets routed through `spillOrFormat` so the LLM sees a head/tail preview with the full markdown on disk. The LLM must follow the spillPath to read the full schema if it spilled.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Schema (namespace) within the database. If omitted, lists all schemas in the database. If provided without a table, lists all tables in this schema. Examples: 'public', 'reporting', 'billing'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Switches behaviour from Level 1 (list schemas) to Level 2 (list tables in this schema) when `table` is null, or Level 3 (describe table) when `table` is also set.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Table to describe in detail. Requires schema to also be provided. Returns columns, data types, primary key, foreign keys, and indexes.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("The namespace inside the database ŌĆö like a folder grouping related tables. On Postgres `public` is the default; SQL Server uses `dbo`. MySQL has no schemas (use database= instead).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("The profile is resolved through DbProfileResolver. Manual profiles connect; IDE-managed profiles return a friendly error pointing the user at Settings.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("The specific table you want a full breakdown of. Returns a markdown columns table plus a primary-key line, foreign-key list, and grouped indexes (composite indexes are rolled up under one entry).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Tool runs Level 1 ŌĆö lists user schemas via DatabaseMetaData.getSchemas(), filtering out system schemas (pg_catalog, information_schema, pg_toast, sys, dbo, etc.).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("Which configured database connection to use ŌĆö like picking a saved connection in DataGrip's 'Database' tool window. The agent gets these IDs by calling db_list_profiles first.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("`NULLABLE` parsing reads the metadata column as a string and compares to \"1\" ŌĆö the JDBC spec returns it as an int (DatabaseMetaData.columnNullable=1, columnNoNulls=0, columnNullableUnknown=2). Most drivers happen to stringify '1'/'0' the same way, but a non-conformant driver could mislabel every column as NOT NULL.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("db_explain", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Once you know the schema and have a query, db_explain shows whether the indexes db_schema lists are actually being used.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("db_list_databases", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use to discover which `database=` values are valid on a multi-database server before drilling into schemas.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("db_list_profiles", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Call first to discover which profile IDs exist ŌĆö db_schema's `profile` param is opaque without it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("db_query", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Call db_schema first to learn column names and types, then craft db_query SELECTs that don't fail on typos.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("db_stats", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Schema tells you the structure; db_stats tells you the row counts and storage sizes for the same tables.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("ignored for SQLite (single-file) and Generic JDBC profiles where the URL itself names the database")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("must match an id in PluginSettings.databaseProfiles or be the discoverable id of an IDE data source")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbSchemaTool.kt$DbSchemaTool$("requires `schema` to also be set ŌĆö the tool does not guess the namespace; without schema, table is silently ignored")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbStatsTool.kt$DbStatsTool$("Connects to DatabaseProfile.resolvedDefaultDatabase ŌĆö whatever the profile's URL points at by default.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbStatsTool.kt$DbStatsTool$("Reverts to schema mode (all tables in schema) when schema is set, or profile-only mode when schema is also absent.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbStatsTool.kt$DbStatsTool$("Runs profile-only mode: all user tables, ordered by total size descending, capped at 50 rows.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbStatsTool.kt$DbStatsTool$("Runs single-table mode: applies both schema and table filter to the engine-specific query. Returns at most one row.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbStatsTool.kt$DbStatsTool$("SQLite is unsupported ŌĆö no row count or storage size API is available via standard JDBC for SQLite. The tool returns an error immediately without opening a connection.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbStatsTool.kt$DbStatsTool$("Switches from profile-only mode (top 50 across all schemas) to schema mode (all tables in this schema). If table is also provided, switches to single-table mode.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbStatsTool.kt$DbStatsTool$("db_explain", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "After db_stats reveals a large table, use db_explain on a slow query against that table to see whether its indexes are being used.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbStatsTool.kt$DbStatsTool$("db_query", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use db_query with engine-specific catalog SQL when you need exact row counts on MySQL/SQL Server or need to filter by a custom criterion ŌĆö at the cost of writing the right dialect yourself.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DbStatsTool.kt$DbStatsTool$("db_schema", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "db_schema shows the structure (columns, PKs, FKs, indexes); db_stats shows the volume (row counts, storage sizes). Run both when investigating an unfamiliar table.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DbStatsTool.kt$DbStatsTool$("requires `schema` to also be set ŌĆö silently ignored without it, making the call run as profile-only")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$(" NOTE: Method is overloaded (${psiResult.overloadCount} variants). Breakpoint set on first match.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$(" PERFORMANCE WARNING: Method breakpoints are 5-10x slower than line breakpoints. Use sparingly.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("(Use 'id: bp-N' with remove_breakpoint(breakpoint_id=...) to remove any type including exception/method breakpoints.)")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("A Java expression evaluated at the throw site. Only pause when the condition is true.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("A Java/Kotlin expression evaluated in the breakpoint's scope. The debugger only pauses when it evaluates to true.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Applied to the breakpoint's SuspendPolicy. 'none' combined with a log_expression creates a tracepoint.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Applied via JavaBreakpointProperties reflection (COUNT_FILTER / COUNT_FILTER_ENABLED). Silently ignored if the Java debugger plugin is unavailable.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Break only on every Nth hit (e.g., pass_count=100 breaks on the 100th hit). Useful for loops and high-traffic code ŌĆö for add_breakpoint")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Breakpoint id from list_breakpoints output (e.g. 'bp-0') ŌĆö for remove_breakpoint. Removes any breakpoint type including exception, method, and field watchpoints that have no file:line anchor.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Breakpoint set at BatchProcessor.java:234, fires on every 100th hit ŌĆö skips the first 99 iterations of the loop.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Breakpoint set at UserService.kt:87 with condition 'userId == 42'. Fires only when that user's request hits the line.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Controls whether the whole JVM pauses or just the thread that hit the breakpoint. Use 'thread' when debugging concurrent code so other threads keep running.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Core breakpoint primitive. Without it the LLM cannot programmatically set any stopping condition ŌĆö all debugging would require manual user intervention.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Crucial before calling remove_breakpoint ŌĆö verifies the exact file:line of each breakpoint and whether it's a line type (removable by file:line) or an exception type (not removable by file:line).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Debugger attached to JVM at localhost:5005. Returns session ID that can be used with debug_step and debug_inspect. Breakpoints set before attaching will activate once the session is connected.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Defaults to 'all' (suspend all threads) unless log_expression is provided, in which case defaults to 'none'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Error attaching to process at $host:$port: ${e.message}", "Error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Error setting exception breakpoint: ${e.message}", "Error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Error setting field watchpoint: ${e.message}", "Error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Error setting method breakpoint: ${e.message}", "Error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Essential for breakpoint awareness. The LLM has no other way to discover what breakpoints exist in the IDE before deciding whether to add or remove one.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Essential for breakpoint cleanup. The breakpoint_id path closes the long-standing gap where exception/method breakpoints could not be removed programmatically.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Exception breakpoint set for java.lang.NullPointerException, fires only on uncaught throws. Debugger will pause at the throw site the next time an uncaught NPE escapes.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("File path relative to project or absolute ŌĆö for add_breakpoint, remove_breakpoint, list_breakpoints, field_watchpoint")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Fully qualified class name ŌĆö for method_breakpoint, exception_breakpoint, field_watchpoint")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("IntelliJ rejects the run config", "processNotStarted callback fires; cont.resume(\"\") causes the 30 s window to return an empty sessionId, which the tool treats as failure.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("JVM not listening on port", "Times out after 30 s; returns 'Failed to attach to {host}:{port} within 30 seconds. Verify the target JVM is running with JDWP agent enabled on port {port}.'")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Java debugger plugin must be available (JavaExceptionBreakpointType class must be loadable)")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Method breakpoint on PaymentService.processPayment, fires on entry and exit. Performance warning included in response.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("No alternative path exists to programmatically attach the debugger to a remote JVM. This is the only way to debug containerised or externally-launched processes without asking the user to manually use Run > Attach to Process.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("No equivalent mechanism exists ŌĆö line breakpoints cannot intercept exceptions before they're caught. This is the only way to trace a swallowed exception back to its origin.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("No other tool can watch field access at the JVM level without modifying the source. Invaluable for tracking down cache corruption and unexpected state mutation.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Non-suspending log breakpoint at CacheService.kt:55 ŌĆö prints 'cache hit: <key>' to the Debug console on each pass without pausing execution.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("One-shot breakpoint. Useful for 'pause here once' scenarios like initialization code that only runs at startup.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Optional expression to log when hit without stopping (log breakpoint) ŌĆö for add_breakpoint")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Pause when the exception propagates up without being caught ŌĆö typically causing the thread to die.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Pause whenever the field is assigned. Default on ŌĆö the most common use case is catching unexpected writes.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Pause whenever the field is read. High-traffic fields make this noisy ŌĆö prefer watch_write unless you specifically need reads.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Performance overhead (5-10├Ś slowdown) makes it unsuitable for hot code. ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Port must be between 1 and 65535, got $port", "Invalid port", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Pre-flight checks whether this line is breakpointable; if not, returns a structured error before the WriteAction.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Removes the breakpoint at index 1 from list_breakpoints output ŌĆö works even if it's an exception or method breakpoint with no file:line anchor.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Resolved to a VirtualFile URL; only XLineBreakpoints with matching fileUrl are included. Exception breakpoints are excluded when a file filter is active.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Resolved to the first matching method in the class via psiClass.methods.filter { it.name == methodName }.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Resolved via PathValidator; a VirtualFile is located and used to create the breakpoint.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Restrict output to breakpoints in a specific file. Exception breakpoints are always excluded from file-filtered results because they have no file anchor.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Returns 'Attached to remote JVM: {host}:{port}\\nSession: {sessionId}\\nConfiguration: {name}\\nStatus: connected'. The sessionId can be passed to debug_step and debug_inspect for subsequent operations.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Returns 'Breakpoint added at {file}:{line}' with a summary of applied traits (conditional, log, temporary, pass_count, suspend type).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Returns 'Exception breakpoint set for {exceptionClass}' with caught/uncaught flags. Appends a warning line only if PSI lookup fails to find the FQN on the project classpath.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Returns a formatted list: 'Breakpoints (N):' followed by one entry per breakpoint. Line breakpoints show 'file:line [enabled, conditional: expr, ...]'. Method breakpoints show 'Method: class.method [entry, exit, enabled]'. Field watchpoints show 'Field: class.field [access, modification, enabled]'. Exception breakpoints show 'Exception: {class} [caught, uncaught, enabled]'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Returns all breakpoints: line breakpoints with conditions/traits, method breakpoints with entry/exit flags, exception breakpoints with caught/uncaught flags, field watchpoints with access/modification flags.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Returns confirmation with class.method, watch_entry, watch_exit values. Appends an overload note if the method is overloaded. Always appends a PERFORMANCE WARNING about the 5-10├Ś overhead.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Returns only the line, method, and watchpoint breakpoints anchored in PaymentService.kt. Exception breakpoints are excluded from this view even if they would fire in this file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Set as an XExpressionImpl on the breakpoint. Evaluated by the JVM at each hit; invalid expressions cause the breakpoint to disable itself silently.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Sets logExpressionObject and forces suspend_policy=NONE ŌĆö the thread keeps running. If suspend_policy is also provided explicitly, the explicit param overrides this default.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Setting caught=false avoids noisy pauses for NPEs that are caught and handled normally ŌĆö only uncaught ones (usually bugs) trigger the stop.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Skip the first N-1 hits and only pause on the Nth. Handy when a bug manifests after a loop runs many times.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Source file hint ŌĆö used as fallback if PSI cannot resolve the class (e.g. if the class is not indexed yet).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("The 'id: bp-N' value shown in list_breakpoints output. Use this to remove exception/method/field breakpoints that have no file:line anchor.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("The Docker container must expose port 5005 and the JVM must be started with -agentlib:jdwp=transport=dt_socket,server=y,suspend=n,address=*:5005")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("The JDWP port the target JVM is listening on. Standard port is 5005 for most Spring/JVM debug configs.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("The class containing the method. Must be fully qualified, including the package path.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("The exception to watch for. Must be fully qualified. The tool PSI-verifies the class on the project classpath and warns when it isn't found ŌĆö the breakpoint is still created.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("The field to watch. For Kotlin properties, use the property name ŌĆö the backing field name is usually the same.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("The hostname or IP of the machine running the target JVM. For local Docker containers 'localhost' is usually correct.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("The simple method name (no parentheses, no signature). For overloaded methods the breakpoint is set on the first PSI match.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Thread suspension policy: 'all' (pause all threads, default) or 'thread' (pause only the hitting thread ŌĆö use for concurrent debugging) ŌĆö for add_breakpoint")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Turns the breakpoint into a tracepoint: logs the expression value to the Debug console without suspending execution.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Used in JavaPsiFacade.findClass() scoped to projectScope(); then falls back to text-scan in the provided file if PSI fails.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Useful when the LLM knows the method but not the exact line. The PSI lookup and overload note reduce follow-up errors.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugBreakpointsTool.kt$DebugBreakpointsTool$("Which source file to add the breakpoint in. Relative paths are resolved against the project base directory.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Class '$className' is not loaded in the JVM. It may not have been instantiated yet, or the name may be incorrect.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Confirms the new value by reading it back. Returns: variable name, actual new value (post-write), type, and which method was used (XValueModifier direct or evaluate fallback).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Confirms: 'Dropped frame: rewound to beginning of ClassName.methodName', with frame index, thread name, session id, and the critical note that variable state and side effects are NOT undone.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Confirms: 'Forced early return from current method.' with the return type, value, and a note that remaining method execution is skipped.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Current method returns true immediately. LLM resumes, observes caller behaviour when the method returns true, and determines whether the bug is in the caller's handling.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Error getting stack frames: ${e.message}", "Error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Error getting thread dump: ${e.message}", "Error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Essential when 'how did I get here?' is the key question. Cannot be replaced by get_variables.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Explicit return type to use when constructing the JDI mirror value. 'auto' infers the type from the value string (nullŌåÆnull, true/falseŌåÆboolean, '42' in int rangeŌåÆint, etc.).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Expression cannot be blank.", "Blank expression", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Filter to a specific thread's stack. Most useful when multiple threads are suspended and you want one specifically.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Hot swap failed. Check for structural changes (new/removed methods, fields, or signature changes).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("How many frames deep to go. Default 20 covers most call chains; increase to 50 for deep recursive stacks.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("How many individual instance records to include (unique ID, reference type). 0 = only the total count is shown, no per-instance detail.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("How many levels of object nesting to expand. Depth 1 = immediate fields only; depth 4 = great-grandchild fields. Higher depths produce much larger output.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Invalid number format for return value: ${e.message}", "Number format error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Maximum stack frames to return (default 20, max 50) ŌĆö for get_stack_frames, thread_dump")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Name of a single variable to deep-inspect. When provided, max_depth is raised to at least 2 to give a meaningful expansion of that variable.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Narrow JVM capability requirement (canGetInstanceInfo=false on remote/non-HotSpot). Rarely used ŌĆö most debugging doesn't require heap counts.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Narrow use case: only useful when the LLM overstepped. The 'state not reset' caveat makes it confusing and a source of subtle mistakes when the LLM expects a clean re-run.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Only that variable is returned, expanded to the effective depth. Error if not found ŌĆö the error message lists all available variable names.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Only way to count live heap instances programmatically. No evaluate-based equivalent exists without iterating all objects via ObjectReference, which has no LLM-callable surface.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Python debug session", "Non-error message: 'Drop frame is not supported in Python debug sessions.'")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Return type: void, null, int, long, boolean, string, double, float, char, byte, short, auto (default) ŌĆö for force_return")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns 'hot swap result: success ŌĆ” Classes reloaded successfully. Execution continues with new code.' The LLM can then resume and verify the fix.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns all local variables in Frame #0 at depth 2. If an object has more fields than depth 2 reaches, shows 'ŌĆ”' placeholders.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns all non-daemon threads with their stack frames. Two threads BLOCKED on each other's monitor will show their stacks ending in synchronized blocks on each other's objects ŌĆö classic deadlock signature.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns only the `requestContext` variable, expanded 4 levels deep ŌĆö useful when that object is the suspect and you need to see all its nested state.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns stack trace: main thread, 7 frames. Frame #0 is the current pause line; frame #1 is the caller, etc.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns the total count of HikariCP pool entries. If the count grows across snapshots while connections should be evicted, it indicates a leak.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns: class name, total live instance count, optional breakdown by type (when multiple reference types match), optional per-instance details (first N instances with unique IDs).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns: expression text, result value string, runtime type. Output is always small ŌĆö no spill.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns: frame header (file:line), then formatted variable list with names, types, and values at the requested depth. Spills to disk if output exceeds 30K.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns: hot swap result (success/failure/cancelled/nothing_to_reload/timeout), session id, compile_first flag, and a human-readable explanation of the outcome.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns: thread name, frame count, and numbered list of frames each with index, method name, file name, and line number.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Returns: total thread count, suspended count, then per-thread block: name, id, status, daemon flag, and stack frames (if available and thread is suspended).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Rewound to beginning of current method. LLM can now step through the method again from the top, this time more carefully at the interesting lines.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Safe only if the method has no side effects ŌĆö evaluate can modify state if the expression calls a mutating method.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Sets featureEnabled to true and confirms via read-back. The LLM can then resume and observe which branch executes.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Stack frames are included per thread if that thread is suspended; otherwise shows '(frames unavailable ŌĆö thread not suspended)'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("Stack frames for that thread are returned if it exists in the session; otherwise falls back to the default thread.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("The active execution stack's thread name is used (from suspendContext.activeExecutionStack.displayName).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("The expression is evaluated in the context of the current stack frame and the result is returned.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugInspectTool.kt$DebugInspectTool$("The name of the local variable to change. Must exist in the current frame's variable list.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("'Press pause.' May not work instantly ŌĆö the JVM has to be in a state where it can be safely interrupted.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("'Skip ahead to step 7 and pause there.' Useful when there's a loop you want to iterate through, or when you want to jump past setup code.")
