ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("10 actions consolidated into one tool keeps the schema lean ŌĆö full debugging capability for the cost of a single tool definition. Action enum + state-tagged descriptions ([SUSPENDED]/[ANY]) gives the LLM enough context to choose correctly.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Advances one source line; any breakpoints inside called methods do NOT pause; tool reports the new line.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Advances one source-line, descending into the first method call on that line. Stops at the first executable line of the called method.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("After inspecting state at a breakpoint, when the LLM has gathered enough info and wants to let execution continue (often to hit a later breakpoint).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("All 10 actions accept session_id but the LLM almost always uses the active session. The optional default is the right design ŌĆö explicit session_id is essential when there are multiple sessions, but mandating it on every call would bloat every invocation.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Asks the debugger 'where are we?' ŌĆö like checking which step of a recipe you're on. Always call this first; the LLM can't act on the debugger without knowing whether it's paused.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("At the start of every debugging interaction, and after every step/run-to-cursor to learn where the program now is.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Best-effort semantics make it unreliable. The LLM has to call get_state in a poll-like pattern ŌĆö could be replaced by a 'pause and wait up to N seconds' helper.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Best-effort suspension of a running session. Calls XDebugSession.pause() which may take seconds and may fail if the JVM is in a non-suspendable state (native code, GC, etc.).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Calls `pause` and assumes it took effect immediately ŌĆö proceeds to `step_over` before pause has actually landed, gets NOT_PAUSED. Pause needs `get_state` polling because it's best-effort.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Calls `run_to_cursor` on a running session expecting it to pause+go-to-line; instead gets NOT_PAUSED. The action name is misleading (it requires a paused session).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Calls `step_out` from `main()` ŌĆö session terminates with SESSION_ENDED, then LLM tries to step_out again on a dead session and loops on NO_SESSION.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Cannot be omitted ŌĆö without it the agent can never let execution continue.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Confuses `force_step_into` (bypasses step filters) with `force_step_over` (ignores breakpoints in callees) ŌĆö picks the wrong one and gets unexpected pause locations.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Could realistically be expressed as `step_over(force=true)` ŌĆö would shrink the schema by one action. But that adds a param to the more-common step_over and changes its description, costing tokens on every iteration.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Descends into the next method even if it's library/framework code; tool reports the new file/line which may be inside the JDK or a 3rd-party JAR.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Pauses at file:line; tool returns the actual pause location (which may differ from the request if the line is blank or in a comment).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Resumes execution until reaching the specified file:line, then pauses. Roughly equivalent to setting a temporary breakpoint there and resuming.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Resumes execution until the current method returns, then pauses at the call site in the caller.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Returns a structured snapshot: state (paused|running), session id, thread, current frame's file + line + class + method, hit breakpoint (if any).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Returns immediately; the JVM will pause when it reaches a safe point. Always follow with get_state ŌĆö the pause may take 1-5 seconds, or may not happen at all.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Returns paused/running, current breakpoint id, thread name, file, line. Works on any session state.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Same as step_into but ignores the 'don't bother me with library code' setting. Use when you suspect the bug is in framework code.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Saves dozens of step calls when the LLM knows the target. Foundational for breakpoint-free targeted debugging.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Single-tool dispatcher for IntelliJ debug-session lifecycle: stepping (step_over/into/out + force variants), state inspection, run-to-cursor, resume, pause, and stop. Resolves the target XDebugSession through IdeStateProbe so agent-started and user-started sessions are both visible.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Skips `get_state` and steps blindly ŌĆö calls step_over on a running session, gets NOT_PAUSED, then has to call pause + get_state + step_over (3 tool calls instead of 1). Mitigation: 'CALL THIS FIRST' nudge in the description. Still the most common failure pattern.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Step over, and ignore any breakpoints inside the methods called on this line. 'Don't stop me even if there's a trap inside the sub-recipe.'")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("The agent's debugger remote control. Once a debug session is paused at a breakpoint, the agent uses this tool to step through code line-by-line, walk into method calls, jump to a specific line, or stop the session ŌĆö exactly like the buttons in IntelliJ's Debug toolbar, but driven by the LLM.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("The currently active session is used (the one the user last interacted with in the Debug tool window).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("The debugger advances; tool returns `Stepped over to MyClass.kt:43`. Follow up with get_state to see the new state if you need more detail.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("The debugger descends one frame; tool returns `Stepped into MyHelper.compute() at MyHelper.kt:18`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Tool returns `Paused at MyClass.kt:42 in thread main`. Now LLM knows it can step or inspect.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Tool returns immediately; the session continues. Subsequent get_state may show paused (next breakpoint hit) or running (no more breakpoints).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Useful enough for long-running ops. Required for any 'pause and inspect' workflow.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("When debugging is done, before starting a new debug session, or when the LLM needs to free up resources.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("When step_into appears to skip a method (because step filters hide it) and the LLM needs to see inside.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("When the LLM has read the code and knows the line where the bug surfaces ŌĆö saves stepping through 50 unrelated lines.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("When the LLM has stepped into a method whose body it now understands and wants to see how the caller uses the return value.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("When the LLM is trying to advance past a line that calls deeply-instrumented code (e.g. with breakpoints set elsewhere) and doesn't want to detour.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("When the LLM wants to inspect a long-running operation that has no breakpoints but whose state mid-flight is interesting.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("When the LLM wants to see what a helper method actually does, especially when its return value is the suspect.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("When tracing through a method to see what value each line produces, without descending into helper methods.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Which debug session to query if there are multiple. Most projects only ever have one running at a time.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("Without this, the LLM has to guess whether the session is paused before every step. That guess is wrong often enough to corrupt the debugging interaction.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("`force_step_into` and `force_step_over` could in theory be expressed as `step_into(force=true)` / `step_over(force=true)`. ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("`pause` is best-effort and can take 1-5 seconds or fail silently if the JVM is in native code. The LLM has to call get_state to confirm.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DebugStepTool.kt$DebugStepTool$("`step_out` from the top-level frame ends the session ŌĆö the LLM occasionally trips on this when debugging short scripts.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Channel handle returned by a prior delegation send ŌĆö required for action=close/answer/fetch_transcript/status/wait; optional for action=send (continuation: skips the picker and Accept dialog, sends a new user turn on the existing session ŌĆö works even after the remote session COMPLETED, within the ~30 min retention window: the remote IDE resurrects the persisted session and resumes it)")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Cheap channel hygiene; needed because the concurrent-channel count is capped. Idempotency makes it safe to call defensively.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Cheap, retained-aware liveness check that lets the LLM branch (wait vs fetch_transcript vs move on) without a transcript round-trip.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Closes the observability gap: an agent that lost a handle (or needs to correlate a busy-decline blocker to its own task) can recover it without a transcript round-trip.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Continuation branch: no picker, no Accept dialog ŌĆö a follow-up turn is written to the existing channel.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("DelegationDeclined: the target IDE's user declined the request to enable inbound delegation. Ask them to enable 'Accept incoming delegations' if they want to receive the task.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("DelegationLimitReached: max ${DelegationOutboundService.MAX_CHANNELS} concurrent delegations already open ŌĆö close one before sending another")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("DelegationOutboundService.close(handle). Idempotent ŌĆö closing an already-closed handle returns closed=false without error.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("DelegationRejected: the continuation was rejected ŌĆö reason: $reason. Your handle is still valid; retry shortly.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("DelegationRejected: the target IDE declined the request ŌĆö reason: ${e.rejectReason ?: "none"}")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("For a fresh send, the target IDE must be running with the project open AND have inbound delegation accepted.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("For action=wait only: integer seconds, allowed range 5ŌĆō1800 (default 300); values outside are clamped and the clamp is reported in the result. The result also arrives automatically when done, so a timeout is not a failure.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Forwarded verbatim as the delegated task (fresh send) or as a new user turn on the channel (continuation).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Full briefing for the remote agent: what to do, relevant context, expected deliverables ŌĆö required for action=send")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Lets the LLM synchronously attach to a delegation when the next step depends on its result, while preserving the no-failure-on-timeout + async-auto-delivery contract.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Maximum seconds to block before giving up the wait (the work keeps running regardless).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Picker opens pre-selected on 'backend'; on accept, returns a handle with status=running. Result arrives later as a nudge.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Resolved through the retained-aware handle lookup; returns active / closed / not-found consistently with answer and send-continuation.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Results are asynchronous: a send returns 'running' immediately and the outcome arrives as a nudge. An agent that expects an inline answer will misread the running header as the result.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Returns `transcript_path`, `size_bytes`, `token_estimate`, and the first 2 KiB under `head`. If the file exceeds 2 KiB, a truncation marker tells the LLM to read_file the path for the rest.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Returns `{\"closed\":true|false,\"handle\":...}` ŌĆö true if a live channel was closed, false if it was already closed (still a success).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Returns `{\"sent\":true,\"handle\":...,\"question_id\":...}`; the remote agent resumes with the answer.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Returns `{\"targets\":[{repoName, projectPath, status, lastOpened}]}` where status Ōłł running | available | closed | discovered | missing. " + "`available` means the IDE is open with inbound delegation off ŌĆö a send will ring its doorbell and prompt the user for consent.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Returns active+state while running, or closed+terminal last_state once done ŌĆö no transcript fetched.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Returns the JSON target list; no UI opens. Pick a repoName to pass as suggested_repo on a subsequent send.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Returns the compact JSON list of handles you hold in this session, each with repo/path/last_state; no UI opens.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Returns the transcript path on the other IDE plus a 2 KiB preview; follow with read_file for the full history.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Reuse a previous delegation's handle to continue that same remote session instead of starting a new one.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Reuse the handle from the original send rather than starting a new delegation for follow-ups; the remote agent resumes from where it left off.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Same-machine, same-user, local IPC only ŌĆö no cross-machine, cross-user, or multi-hop delegation (explicit v1 non-goals).")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Suspends up to 600s; returns the result inline on completion, the question on a clarifying-question, or 'still running' on timeout.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("That channel is torn down; the result is reported regardless of whether it was still open.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("The core of the feature ŌĆö the only way an agent can hand work to a repo it doesn't have open. Async + consent-gated by design.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("The id of the specific question being answered ŌĆö copy it from the Question nudge.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("The only window into what the remote agent did; the path+preview shape keeps token cost bounded while still allowing a full read on demand.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("The picker pre-selects the matching target if one is found; the user may override. The success result narrates whether the suggestion was honoured.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("The reply to send back. When auto-approve is off, the user may edit this in the confirm dialog.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("The task description that the other window's agent will receive ŌĆö write it as a self-contained brief; the remote agent has none of this conversation's context.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("The turn suspends until the remote session delivers a Result or Question, or the timeout elapses.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("There must be an active agent session ŌĆö the delegator session ID is captured at call time to route the result nudge.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("To check what's reachable before a send, or to answer 'which repos/agents are available?' without popping the picker.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Use this for a one-off liveness check, not as a polling loop; the result auto-delivers when done.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("Used to locate the open channel; if the channel is gone, returns DelegationHandleNotFound.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("ask_followup_question", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Feed a remote Question nudge to the user via ask_followup_question, then return their reply with action=answer.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("cannot connect to the target IDE", "DelegationTargetNotReachable ŌĆö most often the target has inbound delegation disabled (looks identical to 'not running'). Ask the user to enable 'Accept incoming delegations' on the target first.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("channel rejected the write", "DelegationWriteFailed ŌĆö the channel may be shutting down; try again or start a new send.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("continuation handle unknown / busy / gone / write failed", "DelegationHandleNotFound when the handle is unknown or retention-pruned (same type as status/answer/wait/fetch_transcript) ŌĆö start a fresh send. DelegationRejected (RETRYABLE, handle still valid) when the target is ide_b_busy (tab occupied) ŌĆö retry shortly with the same handle. DelegationExpired (handle GONE) carries a terminal-gone reason on a KNOWN handle ŌĆö session_closed / session_not_found (remote session gone or pruned), ide_b_not_running (target IDE down), resume_failed (session locked/missing on disk). DelegationWriteFailed ŌĆö IPC write failed. For ide_b_busy retry the same handle; for HandleNotFound/session_not_found/ide_b_not_running start a new send.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("handle is closed-but-retained (session already completed)", "DelegationHandleClosed ŌĆö you cannot answer a finished session; use action=send (same handle) to resume it with a new turn, or action=fetch_transcript to read what it did. Distinct from DelegationHandleNotFound so recovery differs.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("handle known but transcript unreachable", "DelegationExpired ŌĆö the remote session is known but its transcript is not on disk yet or an IO read failed; possibly transient, retry shortly.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("handle unknown or its retention window elapsed", "DelegationHandleNotFound ŌĆö start a fresh send; a pruned handle is consistently 'not found' across status/answer/send-continuation.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("handle unknown or pruned", "DelegationHandleNotFound ŌĆö the handle is gone (never existed or its retention window elapsed). Start a new send.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("handle unknown or retention-pruned", "DelegationHandleNotFound ŌĆö the handle never existed or its ~30 min retention window elapsed (consistent with status/wait/answer/send-continuation). Start a fresh send.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("integer seconds 5ŌĆō1800 (values outside are clamped; clamp is reported in the result)")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("read_file", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "After fetch_transcript, read_file the returned transcript_path for the full message history beyond the 2 KiB preview.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("spawn_agent", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "spawn_agent runs a sub-agent inside THIS IDE/repo; delegation hands work to a DIFFERENT IDE that holds another repo open.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("target user declines the consent prompt", "DelegationDeclined / DelegationRejected ŌĆö the target IDE's user said no. Surface to the user; don't retry blindly.")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("too many open channels", "DelegationLimitReached ŌĆö DelegationOutboundService.MAX_CHANNELS concurrent delegations already open; close one before sending another.")
ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("user declines the confirm dialog", "Returns an error that the user declined to send the answer (only when auto-approve is off).")

ArgumentListWrapping:DelegationTool.kt$DelegationTool$("user dismisses the picker", "DelegationUserCanceledPicker ŌĆö delegation not sent. Don't retry automatically; ask the user what they intended.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("All occurrences of `old_string` are replaced in a single write. The Document-API path replaces from end-to-start to preserve offsets. Result summary includes the count: `Replaced 47 chars with 52 chars in foo.kt (3 occurrences)`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Assumes a syntax warning rolled back the edit. It did not ŌĆö the file is already on disk with the broken content. The LLM must either follow up with a fixing edit or call `revert_file` to restore the previous version.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Character-strict matcher with no whitespace tolerance: a single different space, tab, or CRLF/LF mismatch makes the match fail with 0 occurrences. The error message says 'verify exact text', but the LLM cannot fix a content drift it doesn't know about.")
ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Defaults to false ŌĆö a non-unique `old_string` returns an error rather than guessing which occurrence to replace.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Each occurrence (one, or all if `replace_all=true`) of `old_string` is replaced by this content. The result is written back through Document API ŌåÆ VFS ŌåÆ file I/O fallback chain.")
ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Error: File not found: $resolvedPath", "Error: file not found", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Guesses at whitespace ŌĆö e.g. assumes 4-space indent when the file uses tabs, or omits trailing whitespace the file actually has. The matcher is character-for-character, so even a single different space returns 0 occurrences. Recovery: paste raw bytes from a fresh `read_file`, do not retype.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Includes the `42\\t` line-number prefix from `read_file` output in `old_string` ŌĆö the prefix is metadata, not file content, so the exact-text match returns 0 occurrences. The matcher is character-strict; it can't see through the prefix. Recovery: re-craft `old_string` with raw file bytes only.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Like a precise find-and-replace that refuses to guess. You give it the exact text you want to change (copied from what read_file showed) plus its replacement, and it makes the swap. If the old text appears in two places, it stops and asks you to be more specific instead of picking one at random.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Provides too little context in `old_string` (e.g. just `return null`) ŌĆö the same line appears 12 times in the file, so the unique-match check fails with `old_string found 12 times`. Recovery: add 3-5 surrounding lines for uniqueness, or set `replace_all=true` only when the goal is genuinely 'every occurrence'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Skips `read_file` and edits based on what it 'remembers' the file looks like from a few iterations ago. Another tool, another sub-agent, or the user may have changed the file since. The match still works if the bytes still match, but when they don't, the LLM has to debug a stale-content failure that wouldn't have happened with a fresh read.")
ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Surfaced in the approval dialog and the chat history's tool-call bubble. Stored on the ToolResult for trace replay.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Switches from 'unique match required' mode to 'replace every occurrence' mode. Useful for genuinely repetitive changes (e.g. updating every `TODO(2024)` to `TODO(2026)`). Brittle for symbol renames ŌĆö prefer `refactor_rename` for those.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Syntax validation is Java/Kotlin-only and best-effort ŌĆö Python, JS, JSON, YAML, Markdown all bypass the gate. Failures only surface when the LLM (or user) later runs `diagnostics` or build/test tools.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Syntax warnings do NOT roll back the edit ŌĆö the file is already on disk with the broken content. The LLM must follow up with a fix or `revert_file`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Targeted in-place edit via Claude Code-style exact-string replacement (old_string -> new_string, optional replace_all). Routed through Document API + WriteCommandAction for undo support, with VFS and java.io.File fallbacks. Strict character-for-character match ŌĆö no whitespace tolerance, no fuzzy matching. Kotlin/Java edits run a non-blocking syntax-validator gate that warns but does not roll back.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("The exact bytes you want replaced ŌĆö copied verbatim from what `read_file` showed (without the `N\\t` line-number prefix). The matcher uses `String.indexOf` ŌĆö there is no whitespace tolerance, no fuzzy matching, and no re-indentation. If a single character differs, the match fails with 0 occurrences.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("The exact text to find in the file. Must match character-for-character including whitespace, indentation, and line endings. Include just enough lines to uniquely match the section that needs to change. If the file content came from read_file with line numbers (e.g., '42\tconst x = 1'), do NOT include the line number prefix ŌĆö match only the raw file text.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("The file is searched for this exact byte sequence. Found exactly once: edit proceeds. Found zero times: error 'old_string not found'. Found 2+ times with `replace_all=false`: error 'old_string not unique'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("The one-liner the user sees in the approval dialog. Vague descriptions ('edit file') give the user nothing actionable; specific ones ('inline getFoo() into bar since it's only called once') let them decide quickly. The tool itself does not read this ŌĆö it's purely UX glue.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("The path is canonicalised, validated against the project root + `{agentDir}/memory/` allow-list via PathValidator, and the file is opened for editing.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("The replacement bytes. Indentation and line endings should match the surrounding file style ŌĆö the tool does not re-indent for you. Pass `\"\"` (empty string) to delete the matched region.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Uses `replace_all=true` to rename a symbol across a file ŌĆö works for the literal string but breaks when the same identifier appears as a substring of another (e.g. renaming `User` also rewrites `UserProfile`). Recovery: use `refactor_rename` (deferred tool) for symbol-level renames; reserve `edit_file replace_all` for genuinely string-level changes.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Where the file lives. Relative paths resolve against the project root; absolute paths must canonicalise to inside the project root or the agent's memory directory.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("Whole-file rewrites are not the use case ŌĆö for those, `create_file` (overwrite-with-confirmation) is the right tool. edit_file silently struggles when `old_string` is the entire file and `new_string` is also the entire file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("`description` is a required parameter even when the user has approved the tool for the whole session and won't see the dialog ŌĆö burns tokens on every call regardless.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("`old_string` must be unique in the file unless `replace_all=true` ŌĆö a too-small snippet errors out with 'old_string not unique'. Forces the LLM to add 3-5 lines of surrounding context, which slightly inflates token cost on every edit.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("create_file", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use instead when the file does not yet exist, or when the change is so sweeping that an in-place edit would be more disruptive than just rewriting.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("diagnostics", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Call after editing non-Java/Kotlin files to catch errors the syntax validator can't see (Python typos, broken JSON, malformed YAML).")
ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("must point inside the project root or `{agentDir}/memory/` ŌĆö `../etc/passwd`-style traversal is rejected before any file I/O")
ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("preserve the file's existing indentation style (tabs vs spaces, depth) ŌĆö the tool will not normalise it")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("read_file", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "ALWAYS read first ŌĆö edit_file's matcher is character-strict, so the `old_string` must come from a fresh read of the actual file bytes (without the `N\\t` line-number prefix).")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("refactor_rename", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use instead for symbol-level renames ŌĆö understands scope, references, and language semantics. `edit_file replace_all` is string-level and will rewrite substrings of unrelated identifiers.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EditFileTool.kt$EditFileTool$("revert_file", Relationship.FALLBACK, "Use to undo a bad edit (e.g. one that introduced a syntax error the LLM can't fix in another round). Single-file revert via VCS history.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("A short sentence explaining why the agent wants to plan first ŌĆö surfaced in the loop trace and in the tool result content so the user can see the motivation when reviewing the session.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("A switch the agent flips from \"do it\" to \"draft a proposal first.\" Like a contractor pausing demolition to walk the homeowner through the plan before swinging a hammer ŌĆö and only the homeowner can say \"ok, start the work.\"")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("Already-in-plan-mode re-call: calls `enable_plan_mode` when plan mode is already active. Schema filtering removes the tool when planMode is true, so this should not be presented to the LLM, but cached tool-call replay or stale schemas can still surface it. The execution guard treats it as a no-op-style toggle.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("Assumes the toggle is symmetric ŌĆö calls `enable_plan_mode` thinking it can call something equivalent to disable it later. There is no such tool; mode-out-of-plan is user-driven only. The execution guard in AgentLoop blocks any write tool the LLM hallucinates calling, but the LLM may waste turns trying to escape plan mode programmatically.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("Asymmetric on/off semantics: only the user can return to act mode (via UI Approve button). LLMs that try to programmatically toggle back hit the schema filter (no `disable_plan_mode` tool exists) and may waste turns flailing.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("Calls `enable_plan_mode` and then forgets to follow up with `plan_mode_respond`, instead trying read-only exploration tools or `think`. The user sees an empty plan-mode badge with no plan card, and has to wait or steer the agent into actually presenting something.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("Enables plan mode for trivial single-file edits where act mode would have been fine. Wastes a round-trip ŌĆö the LLM now has to call `plan_mode_respond` and wait for user approval before doing the one-line change it could have just made.")
ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("Multi-module refactor touching ~20 files; want to outline the dependency-update order before editing.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("Once enabled, every subsequent turn pays the schema-rebuild cost (write tools removed, `plan_mode_respond` added) until the user approves or revises. Cheap per-turn but compounds across long planning sessions.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("Programmatically flips `AgentService.planModeActive` (AtomicBoolean) to true; AgentLoop sees `enablePlanMode=true` on the ToolResult, the dynamic tool-definition provider rebuilds the schema (write tools + this tool removed, `plan_mode_respond` added), and the next LLM turn runs under plan-mode constraints. Asymmetric: only the user can flip the bit back via the UI Approve button ŌĆö there is no `disable_plan_mode` tool.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("Schema filtering removes `enable_plan_mode` itself from the tool set when plan mode is already active, so the LLM cannot \"refresh\" plan mode mid-session. This is correct, but can confuse models that try to re-enter plan mode after partial exploration.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("Skips reading/exploring before enabling plan mode and immediately calls `plan_mode_respond` with a plan based on assumptions. The system prompt explicitly says to explore first; rushing produces low-confidence plans the user has to rewrite.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("The reason is appended to the ToolResult content (`\"Switched to PLAN MODE. Reason: \${reason} ...\"`) and to the summary line. AgentLoop reads the result, flips `planModeActive`, rebuilds the tool schema, and the next LLM turn sees the plan-mode tool set.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("The reason string is required but never enforced for length or quality ŌĆö the LLM can pass a one-word `reason=\"complex\"` and still flip the mode. Audit traces depend on the LLM voluntarily writing useful reasons.")
ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("User asked to \"clean up the auth flow\" ŌĆö task is ambiguous enough that I should propose a concrete plan first.")

ArgumentListWrapping:EnablePlanModeTool.kt$EnablePlanModeTool$("must be a non-empty string ŌĆö a missing or null `reason` returns an `isError=true` result with summary `enable_plan_mode failed: missing reason` and the toggle does NOT happen")

ArgumentListWrapping:EndpointsTool.kt$EndpointsTool$( "fastapi", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use `fastapi(action=routes)` when the Microservices plugin is unavailable or when file-scan precision " + "(exact decorator-level source location) is needed. `fastapi` uses PythonFileScanner regex ŌĆö zero IDE plugin dependency; " + "`endpoints` uses the IDE's Python microservices provider (requires PyCharm Pro). Results may differ on dynamic routes." )

ArgumentListWrapping:EndpointsTool.kt$EndpointsTool$( "find_references", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use `find_references` for PSI-level usages of a Java/Kotlin symbol (class, method). " + "Use `endpoints(action=find_usages)` to resolve a URL string to every handler AND HTTP-client call site ŌĆö " + "including string literals in WebClient/RestTemplate calls that PSI find-references misses." )

ArgumentListWrapping:EndpointsTool.kt$EndpointsTool$( "flask", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use `flask(action=routes)` when the Microservices plugin is unavailable. Same trade-off as `fastapi` vs `endpoints`: " + "file-scan is always available, IDE provider requires Ultimate/PyCharm Pro. Blueprint prefix resolution is analogous." )

ArgumentListWrapping:EndpointsTool.kt$EndpointsTool$( "search_code", Relationship.FALLBACK, "Fall back to `search_code` when the Microservices plugin is unavailable, the index is stale, " + "or endpoint results are unexpectedly empty. Regex on @RequestMapping/@GetMapping/@PostMapping annotations covers most Spring endpoints; " + "regex on @app.get/@router.post covers FastAPI/Flask." )

ArgumentListWrapping:EndpointsTool.kt$EndpointsTool$( "spring", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use `spring(action=endpoints)` in Community or any IDE without the Microservices plugin. " + "In Ultimate, SpringTool.includeEndpointActions=false and its endpoint actions redirect to `endpoints`. " + "For non-endpoint Spring queries (beans, JPA, security), `spring` remains the correct tool." )

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Covers both SQLAlchemy and Tortoise in one call. The heuristic base-class detection (Base/Model) covers the vast majority of real projects.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Fragile to non-standard base class names ŌĆö projects that rename the declarative base produce silent empty results. Should document this more prominently.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Lifecycle event detection covers three distinct syntactic patterns ŌĆö search_code would require three separate queries to get the same coverage.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Low token cost, high utility for version-gated recommendations (pydantic v1 vs v2 is a critical break). Avoids parsing dependency files manually.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Niche but precise ŌĆö identifies the exact functions that defer work, which is invisible from route listings alone.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Only entries where the dependency name or the enclosing function name contains this substring are returned.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Pydantic v1 vs v2 is a critical compatibility break ŌĆö version_info is the fastest way to determine which is in use before recommending model validators or Field() arguments.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns BaseSettings subclasses with their field names and types. Sensitive field values shown as '[REDACTED]'. Truncated at 10 fields with remainder count.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns ORM models grouped by file: class name, ORM type (SQLAlchemy/Tortoise), and field definitions. Truncated at 10 fields with remainder count.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns Pydantic models grouped by file, each with field name and type annotations. Fields truncated at 10 with remainder count.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns a version list with fastapi first, then alphabetical. Summary includes FastAPI version string and total package count.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns all BaseSettings subclasses ŌĆö e.g. 'Settings: database_url: str, secret_key: [REDACTED], debug: bool = False'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns all Pydantic BaseModel subclasses across the project ŌĆö useful for a full API schema overview.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns all classes extending Base or Model with their Column/field definitions ŌĆö e.g. 'User [SQLAlchemy]: id: Integer, email: String, created_at: DateTime'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns all handler functions that Depend on auth-related callables (get_current_user, oauth2_scheme, etc.).")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns all route decorators found across all .py files, with composed prefixes: e.g. GET /api/v1/users, POST /api/v1/users/{user_id}.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns e.g. 'CORSMiddleware (allow_origins=[\"*\"], allow_methods=[\"*\"])' ŌĆö confirms CORS is configured and shows its parameters.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns e.g. '[startup] connect_db()' and '[lifespan] app_lifespan()' ŌĆö all startup/shutdown logic in one view.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns e.g. 'create_order()' and 'upload_file()' ŌĆö the handlers that queue deferred work.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns e.g. 'fastapi==0.110.0, pydantic==2.6.4, uvicorn==0.29.0' ŌĆö enough to determine pydantic v1 vs v2 compatibility.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns event entries as '[eventType] handler()' lines grouped by file. eventType is startup, shutdown, or lifespan.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns middleware registrations as 'MiddlewareClass (kwargs)' lines with file and line order.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns only config classes whose name contains 'Database' ŌĆö e.g. DatabaseSettings with host/port/name/user fields.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns routes grouped by file: HTTP method, full composed URL path, and handler function name. Count summary in header.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Returns security scheme declarations as 'variable = SchemeClass(args)' lines grouped by file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Security scheme inventory is hard to reconstruct from search_code alone ŌĆö this consolidates 10 pattern variants into a single structured call.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Structured extraction of typed settings with automatic sensitive-value redaction ŌĆö more useful than grepping for class definitions.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Structured field extraction from Pydantic models is more reliable than grepping for class body ŌĆö handles multi-line fields and skips class metadata.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Substring filter applied to both the dependency callable name and the declaring function name.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("The primary route discovery tool for FastAPI. Router prefix composition is the key differentiator over raw search_code ŌĆö LLM gets real URLs, not bare decorator strings.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("To find which routes use these schemes, follow up with dependencies(class_name=<variable_name>).")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Tracing FastAPI's Depends() chains across files is tedious with search_code ŌĆö this consolidates it into one structured view.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("When the user asks 'how does this API authenticate', 'is OAuth2 configured', 'what security schemes are used', or auditing authentication before adding a new protected endpoint.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("When the user asks 'is CORS configured', 'what middleware is registered', 'why are all responses getting a header added', or auditing request/response pipeline layers.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("When the user asks 'what FastAPI version is installed', 'is pydantic v1 or v2 in use', 'what SQLAlchemy version does this project use', or before recommending a version-gated API.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("When the user asks 'what database tables exist', 'what columns does the users table have', 'what ORM models are defined', or before writing a migration or query.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("When the user asks 'what does the UserResponse model look like', 'what fields does the CreateOrderRequest have', or 'list all Pydantic models'. Starting point for understanding API contract types.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("When the user asks 'what environment variables does this app use', 'what settings does the DatabaseConfig class expose', 'what's the config structure', or before writing a .env file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("When the user asks 'what initialization runs at startup', 'where is the database connection opened', 'does the app have a shutdown hook', or tracing resource lifecycle management.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("When the user asks 'what routes exist', 'what handles GET /api/users', 'show me all POST endpoints', or needs a route inventory before adding a new endpoint. Primary starting point for any FastAPI navigation task.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("When the user asks 'which endpoints kick off background jobs', 'where does the email-sending background task get registered', or auditing async side effects of HTTP handlers.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("When the user asks 'which endpoints require authentication', 'what does the get_current_user dependency do', 'where is DatabaseSession injected', or auditing the DI chain.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("Zero-param, fast, gives a clear middleware stack inventory that's otherwise scattered across multiple files.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("no BaseSettings classes found", "Project may use plain os.environ, python-dotenv without pydantic-settings, or Dynaconf ŌĆö none of these are detected.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("no Depends() usages found", "Either the project uses no FastAPI dependency injection or the filter is too narrow.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("no FastAPI event handlers found", "App may have no explicit startup/shutdown logic, or may use a different lifecycle mechanism (ASGI middleware, APM instrumentation).")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("no FastAPI security schemes found", "Project may use custom auth middleware or a third-party auth library not matching the 10 known FastAPI scheme class names.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("no Pydantic models found matching filter", "Model may not extend BaseModel directly ŌĆö generic classes, TypedDicts, or dataclasses are not detected.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("python_runtime_exec", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use `python_runtime_exec` to actually run FastAPI tests or validate that the routes work; `fastapi` only reads the source.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FastApiTool.kt$FastApiTool$("search_code", Relationship.FALLBACK, "Use `search_code` when dynamic registration, cross-file router chains, or unconventional base-class names defeat the file-scan patterns.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Bug fixed ŌĆö no-element-context fallback was `registry.forLanguageId(\"JAVA\") ?: registry.forLanguageId(\"kotlin\")`, which silently skipped `PythonProvider` in pure-Python projects. Switched to `registry.allProviders().firstNotNullOfOrNull { ... }`, matching the canonical pattern used by find_implementations, call_hierarchy, and type_hierarchy. Pure-Python projects now work correctly without a workaround. Surfaced by Phase 5 tool-docs swarm (Batch 2).")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Calls find_definition during indexing ŌĆö gets a dumb-mode error and immediately retries without backoff, burning iterations until the index finishes. No internal wait/retry; the LLM should switch to `search_code` or wait one tool-call cycle.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Calls find_definition in a pure-Python project without passing a file-scoped hint ŌĆö the no-element-context path now iterates all registered providers via `registry.allProviders()`, so `PythonProvider` is found correctly even when no Java plugin is loaded. If a lookup still returns 'No definition found', the symbol name itself is likely wrong (wrong case, snake_case vs camelCase, etc.).")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("If multiple methods/fields share a name across the codebase, name the class to scope the lookup. Internally builds `class_name#symbol` and tries that first before falling back to the unscoped lookup.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Kotlin top-level functions and extension functions are not resolved by bare name ŌĆö `findSymbol` for Java/Kotlin uses `PsiShortNamesCache.getMethodsByName`, which indexes class members only. Workaround: pass the FQN of the containing file class (`FooKt`) or use `search_code`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Output format differs by element kind ŌĆö classes get a skeleton block, methods get a signature line, fields get a type line. The LLM occasionally treats the field 'Type:' line as a method signature and fabricates a return type when summarising.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("PSI go-to-definition for a class (FQN or simple name), method (`Class#method` / bare name), or field ŌĆö delegates to the per-language `LanguageIntelligenceProvider` resolved from the file containing the matched symbol, returning file path, line, signature, and (for classes) a formatted skeleton.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Passes a snake_case identifier when the codebase uses camelCase (or vice versa) ŌĆö the underlying `findSymbol` lookup is case- and style-sensitive, so `find_user_by_id` returns 'No definition found' even when `findUserById` exists. The LLM then often retries `search_code` blindly instead of fixing the casing.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Python `findSymbol` parses a single `.` as `Class.member` (2-part split) ŌĆö multi-segment paths like `pkg.module.Class.method` won't resolve through the member path; the LLM has to use the bare-class lookup and rely on the FQN fallback through `PsiShortNamesCache`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Requires indexing to be complete ŌĆö `inSmartMode(project)` defers the read action until smart mode, but the `PsiToolUtils.isDumb(project)` guard at entry returns immediately with a dumb-mode error if indexing is in progress, so the LLM gets a hard fail (not a wait).")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Returns the FIRST match for ambiguous bare-name lookups ŌĆö `findSymbol` walks class ŌåÆ `Class#member` ŌåÆ short-names cache and stops on the first hit. For overloaded methods (Java method overloading, Kotlin extension functions on different receivers), the disambiguation note fires only for `PsiMethod` and only via `PsiShortNamesCache.getMethodsByName`. Fields and Python functions get no warning.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Searches for a Kotlin top-level function or extension function by bare name ŌĆö `findSymbol` only walks `PsiShortNamesCache.getMethodsByName` (members of classes) and `getFieldsByName`, missing top-level `fun` declarations. The LLM gets 'No definition found' and falls back to `search_code`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Skips the `class_name` hint on overloaded methods ŌĆö gets the first match plus a '(N other method(s) with same name)' note, then proceeds as if the first hit is canonical. The disambiguation hint fires only for `PsiMethod`, not for fields or top-level Python functions, so the LLM can silently land on the wrong overload.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("The agent's Cmd-click. Give it a symbol name and it jumps to where the class, function, or field is declared ŌĆö same machinery the IDE uses when you Cmd/Ctrl-click an identifier, just driven by the LLM instead of a mouse.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("The provider's `findSymbol` runs through (1) class FQN/simple-name lookup, (2) `Class#method` or `Class.method` parsing for member resolution, (3) bare-name fallback via `PsiShortNamesCache` (Java/Kotlin) or top-level walk (Python). First match wins; for methods, a disambiguation note is appended if the short-names cache reports multiple methods with the same name.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("Tool runs the bare-symbol path directly. For methods, the result includes a disambiguation note like `(3 other method(s) with same name ŌĆö provide class_name to disambiguate)` when `PsiShortNamesCache` reports siblings.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("What you're looking for. Can be a fully-qualified class name (`com.foo.Bar`), a simple class name (`Bar`), a method/field name, or `Class#method` / `Class.method` syntax to scope a member to its class.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("`findSymbol` is called with the composed `<class_name>#<symbol>` string first; on a hit, that scoped result is returned. On a miss, the tool falls through to the bare-symbol path.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("call_hierarchy", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "After find_definition locates a method, call_hierarchy traces who calls it (callers) and what it calls (callees). find_definition ŌåÆ call_hierarchy is the standard 'how is this method wired into the codebase' pipeline.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("file_structure", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use instead when you have a file path and want every declaration in it ŌĆö no symbol name needed.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("find_implementations", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "After find_definition locates an interface or abstract method, find_implementations enumerates concrete subtypes/overrides. Together they answer 'what's the contract and who satisfies it'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("find_references", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Definition vs. usages ŌĆö find_definition jumps to where a symbol is declared, find_references lists every place it's used. Typical workflow: find_definition to locate the source, then find_references to see who depends on it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("must be a single identifier or `Class#member` / `Class.member` / fully-qualified path ŌĆö arbitrary text fails silently with 'No definition found'")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindDefinitionTool.kt$FindDefinitionTool$("search_code", Relationship.FALLBACK, "Use when the language has no LanguageIntelligenceProvider registered (e.g. JS/TS, Go, Rust today), when the symbol is dynamic (Python attribute lookups, runtime injection), or when find_definition returns 'No definition found'. Loses precision but always works.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Bare method name is fed directly to `findSymbol`. For ambiguous names like `execute` or `build`, `PsiShortNamesCache.getMethodsByName` returns the first match in iteration order; the tool does NOT emit a disambiguation note (unlike `find_definition`), so a wrong-overload pick is silent.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Calls `find_implementations` on a non-abstract method of a concrete class ŌĆö `OverridingMethodsSearch` returns subclass overrides if any exist, otherwise an empty list. The LLM treats the empty result as 'this method has no implementations' rather than 'this method has no overrides because nothing extends the class'. Correct interpretation: empty list = no overrides exist in project scope, NOT 'this method is unimplemented'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Calls find_implementations during indexing ŌĆö `PsiToolUtils.isDumb(project)` guard at entry returns immediately with a dumb-mode error. The `inSmartMode(project)` deferral on the read action only applies AFTER entry; the LLM gets a hard fail, not a wait, and often retries without backoff.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Composed into `class_name#method` if both params are set, else passed bare. Routed to `registry.allProviders().firstNotNullOfOrNull { findSymbol }`, which iterates ALL registered language providers (Java/Kotlin and Python) until one resolves. The matching provider then drives `findImplementations`: `OverridingMethodsSearch` for `PsiMethod`, `ClassInheritorsSearch` for `PsiClass`, empty list for any other element kind.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Composes `<class_name>#<method>` and feeds it to `findSymbol` across all providers. First provider that resolves it wins, and its `findImplementations` runs against the resolved element.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Confuses 'implementations' with 'subclasses' and runs `find_implementations` on a CONCRETE class that nothing extends ŌĆö gets 'No implementations found' and concludes the class has no users, when the correct question was `find_references` (call sites) or `type_hierarchy` (subtypes that may not exist yet). Implementations is for INTERFACES / ABSTRACT classes / abstract methods specifically; for concrete-class-extension questions, use type_hierarchy.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Either a method name (interface or abstract method) OR a class name (interface, abstract class). The parameter is named `method` for historical reasons but the tool resolves the symbol generically ŌĆö passing a class name returns its concrete subclasses; passing a method name returns its overrides.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Element-kind filter is silent ŌĆö non-method, non-class elements (fields, top-level Kotlin functions, Python module-level functions, lambdas, type aliases) hit the `else -> emptyList()` branch in both providers and return 'No implementations found' indistinguishable from 'this is an interface with zero implementors'. The LLM has no signal that the symbol kind was wrong.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Expects Python's `typing.Protocol` (duck-typed structural subtyping) to surface ŌĆö `PythonProvider.findImplementations` walks `helper.findInheritors`, which traces nominal class inheritance only. A class that satisfies a Protocol structurally without `class Foo(MyProtocol):` declaration WILL NOT appear, so the LLM gets a falsely empty list for protocol-style interfaces.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Forgets the `class_name` hint on a common method name like `execute`, `build`, `run` ŌĆö `findSymbol` walks `PsiShortNamesCache.getMethodsByName` and picks the first match, which may be a different class entirely. Implementations come back for the WRONG interface, and the LLM presents them as canonical with no warning.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Hard 40-result cap with no pagination, sort key, or filter ŌĆö `implementations.take(40)` truncates without sorting by file path or relevance. For ubiquitous interfaces (Spring's `Repository`, JDK's `Comparable`, `Runnable`), the displayed implementations are an arbitrary first-40 subset of the searcher's iteration order. The trailing `... and N more` line distinguishes 'all of them' from 'a tiny biased sample', but the LLM occasionally drops the footer when summarising.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Library/JDK implementations are NOT enumerated by default ŌĆö `findImplementations` runs against `GlobalSearchScope.projectScope(project)` (line 49 of FindImplementationsTool.kt), which excludes external libraries and the JDK. For 'who implements `java.lang.Comparable`' across the JDK + libraries, the project scope returns only project-owned implementations. Workaround: there is no parameter to widen scope; the LLM must use `search_code` with a regex against library JARs, or fall back to `find_references` which has the same scope limitation.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("No disambiguation note on overloaded method names ŌĆö unlike `find_definition`, this tool does NOT append a 'N other method(s) with same name' warning when `PsiShortNamesCache` reports multiple matches. A bare method name like `execute` silently picks one overload and returns implementations of THAT one only, with no hint that `class_name` would help.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Optional: fully qualified or simple class/interface name containing the method. Required if method name is ambiguous.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("PSI 'go-to-implementation' for an interface/abstract method or class ŌĆö resolves the symbol via `LanguageIntelligenceProvider.findSymbol` (composing `class_name#method` when both are passed), then dispatches by element kind: `PsiMethod` runs `OverridingMethodsSearch.search(scope, deep=true)` for every overriding method (Java/Kotlin) or walks Python subclass MRO for matching method names; `PsiClass` runs `ClassInheritorsSearch.search(scope, deep=true)` for every concrete subclass. Returns `name: signature` plus `filePath:line` rows, capped at 40 hits with a `... and N more` footer.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Python Protocol / structural typing is invisible ŌĆö `PythonProvider.findImplementations` traces nominal class inheritance via `helper.findInheritors`. A class that satisfies a `typing.Protocol` structurally without an explicit `class Foo(MyProtocol):` parent declaration is not returned. For Python projects that lean on Protocol, this tool dramatically under-reports.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Requires full project indexing to be complete ŌĆö `PsiToolUtils.isDumb(project)` guard at entry returns a hard dumb-mode error if the index is still building. The `inSmartMode(project)` deferral applies only AFTER entry, so it doesn't help here. LLM has to retry, not wait.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Scope hint for the method lookup when multiple interfaces/classes share a method name. Built into the `class_name#method` symbol string and tried first; on a miss the bare-method path runs.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("Searches for a Kotlin top-level function or extension function ŌĆö these aren't `PsiMethod` members, so `findSymbol` either misses them or returns a non-method element, and `findImplementations` falls through to its `else -> emptyList()` branch silently. The LLM sees 'No implementations found' with no signal that the symbol kind was wrong.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("The agent's 'Go to Implementation' (Cmd-Alt-B in IntelliJ). Given an interface or an abstract method, it answers 'who actually does this thing?' ŌĆö listing every concrete class that implements the interface or every method that overrides the abstract declaration. Same machinery the IDE uses when you Cmd-click a method name on an interface; just driven by the LLM.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("VERIFIED: `find_implementations` does NOT have the hardcoded `JAVA`/`kotlin` provider-fallback bug that affects `find_definition` (line 113) and `find_references` (line 227). Lines 38-59 of FindImplementationsTool.kt use the correct pattern: enumerate `registry.allProviders()`, then `firstNotNullOfOrNull { p -> p.findSymbol(project, symbolName)?.let { p to it } }`. This means find_implementations works correctly in pure-Python projects where `PythonProvider` is registered but no Java plugin is loaded ŌĆö the same correct pattern used by `call_hierarchy` and `type_hierarchy`. When fixing the find_definition / find_references bugs, this file is the reference template.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("call_hierarchy", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "After find_implementations enumerates concrete overrides of an abstract method, call_hierarchy on each implementation traces who calls THAT specific override. Useful when an interface has many implementors and you need to understand which subtype is wired into which call path.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("find_definition", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Canonical workflow: find_definition first to anchor the interface/abstract method (confirm the symbol exists and see its signature), then find_implementations to enumerate the concrete satisfiers. find_definition ŌåÆ find_implementations is the standard 'what's the contract and who fulfills it' pipeline.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("find_references", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "find_references on an interface method tends to return both call sites AND override declarations mixed together; find_implementations returns ONLY the override declarations. Use find_implementations when you need a clean 'who satisfies the contract' answer; use find_references when you also want the call sites.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("must resolve to a `PsiMethod` or `PsiClass` ŌĆö fields, top-level Kotlin functions, and Python module-level functions return an empty list with no warning")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("search_code", Relationship.FALLBACK, "Use when no `LanguageIntelligenceProvider` exists for the language (JS/TS, Go, Rust today), when the symbol is duck-typed (Python `typing.Protocol`, JS structural typing), or when find_implementations returns 'No implementations found' on a symbol that you know is an interface ŌĆö the result may be missing transitive overrides through reflection, dynamic dispatch, or generated code that the index can't see.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindImplementationsTool.kt$FindImplementationsTool$("type_hierarchy", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Class-relation pair. type_hierarchy walks BOTH directions (supertypes + subtypes) and returns the full inheritance tree. find_implementations returns ONLY the concrete leaves ŌĆö the actual implementors. Use type_hierarchy when you want the structural picture of who-extends-what; use find_implementations when you specifically want 'who has to satisfy this contract'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Bug fixed ŌĆö no-file-context global path was `registry.forLanguageId(\"JAVA\") ?: registry.forLanguageId(\"kotlin\")`, which silently skipped `PythonProvider` in pure-Python projects. Switched to `registry.allProviders().firstNotNullOfOrNull { ... }`, matching the canonical pattern used by find_implementations, call_hierarchy, and type_hierarchy. Pure-Python projects now work correctly without needing to pass `file`. Surfaced by Phase 5 tool-docs swarm (Batch 4).")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Calls find_references during indexing ŌĆö gets a dumb-mode error from `PsiToolUtils.isDumb(project)` and immediately retries without backoff. No internal wait/retry; the LLM should pause one tool-call cycle or fall back to `search_code`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Calls find_references in a pure-Python project without passing `file` ŌĆö the no-file-context path now iterates all registered providers via `registry.allProviders()`, so `PythonProvider` is found correctly even when no Java plugin is loaded. Passing `file` still provides the most reliable file-scoped disambiguation, but is no longer required for basic symbol resolution in Python projects.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Confuses the result with `find_definition` output ŌĆö find_references returns USAGES (excluding the declaration itself, which lives elsewhere in the index), so the LLM sometimes claims 'this method has no callers' when the result is empty, when really the method IS the only thing named `foo` and just isn't called yet. Use find_definition first to confirm the target exists before drawing 'unused' conclusions.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Does NOT separate import-statement references from real call/use references ŌĆö `ReferencesSearch.search` returns import references alongside actual usages. For Java/Kotlin classes with many imports and few real call sites, the result is dominated by import lines. The LLM has to filter visually. Workaround: ask the LLM to grep for the symbol name in the result lines, ignoring lines starting with `import`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Each reference is rendered as `path:line\\n` followed by `(start..end).joinToString` over the surrounding lines, with `>>>` prefixing the hit line and ` ` (3 spaces) prefixing context lines. Computed via `document.getLineStartOffset` / `getLineEndOffset` on the containing file's PSI document.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("File-scoped resolution runs first: the resolver looks up the provider for that file's language, calls `findSymbol`, and accepts the result only if its containing file matches. Falls through to `(PsiJavaFile).classes.flatMap { methods }.firstOrNull { name == symbol }` for method-by-name within the file. If file-scoped resolution misses, the global path runs anyway ŌĆö so `file` is a hint, not a hard filter.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("File-scoped resolution's class-walk fallback (in `resolveSearchTarget`) only runs for `PsiJavaFile` ŌĆö Kotlin files, Python files, and any other language with `file` set fall through to the global path even when file-scoped resolution should narrow them. Disambiguation via `file` is therefore most reliable for Java sources.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Hard 50-result cap with no pagination, sort key, or filter ŌĆö `references.take(50)` truncates without sorting by file path, line number, or relevance. For a method called 200+ times across the codebase, the LLM sees an arbitrary slice of the first 50 in iteration order and can't widen the search. Workaround: pass a more specific symbol or use `file` to scope, then re-query for the remainder.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Hardcoded-fallback bug is now fixed in both find_references and find_definition ŌĆö both now use `registry.allProviders().firstNotNullOfOrNull` for the no-file-context path. Fixed together in the same commit; consistent with find_implementations, call_hierarchy, and type_hierarchy.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("How many lines of code to print above and below each reference, with `>>>` marking the actual hit line. 0 is a single-line `path:line <code>` row; 1-3 is a small surrounding block.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("PSI find-usages for a class, method, or field ŌĆö resolves the symbol to a `PsiElement` (provider `findSymbol` first, `PsiShortNamesCache` fallback), then runs `ReferencesSearch.search(target, projectScope).findAll()` and emits `path:line <line text>` rows (or `>>>`-marked context blocks when `context_lines > 0`); capped at the first 50 hits with a count footer.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Path to the file that DECLARES the symbol ŌĆö used to pick the right one when multiple classes/methods share a name across the project. Validated against the project root by `PathValidator.resolveAndValidate` to prevent traversal.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Requires indexing to be complete ŌĆö `PsiToolUtils.isDumb(project)` guard at entry returns immediately with a dumb-mode error if indexing is in progress; the `inSmartMode(project)` on the read-action only defers WITHIN the action, not the entry check. LLM gets a hard fail and has to retry.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Routed to `resolveSearchTarget`: if `file` is set, file-scoped resolution runs first (provider for that file's language ŌåÆ file-scoped class+method walk for Java files); otherwise the resolver iterates `registry.allProviders().firstNotNullOfOrNull` (post-commit 3918e3d7b ŌĆö no hardcoded JAVA/kotlin fallback), with `PsiShortNamesCache.getMethodsByName` / `getFieldsByName` as the last fallback. The first match is fed to `ReferencesSearch.search(target, projectScope)`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Searches an overloaded method by bare name without the `file` hint ŌĆö the resolver hits `PsiShortNamesCache.getMethodsByName(symbol).firstOrNull()`, which picks WHICHEVER overload the cache returns first. References for the wrong overload come back, and the LLM treats them as canonical. Pass `file` to scope the lookup to the declaring file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Sets `context_lines` higher than 3 expecting more context ŌĆö the value is silently coerced to `0..3` by `coerceIn(0, 3)`. Asking for `context_lines=10` returns the same output as `context_lines=3`, which can mislead the LLM into thinking it's seeing the full surrounding block.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Single-line rendering: `path:line <trimmed line text>`. Compact and cheap ŌĆö preferred for high-fanout symbols where context lines would blow out the 50-result cap into a wall of code.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Slow on large codebases ŌĆö `ReferencesSearch.findAll()` is index-backed but still walks every file in `GlobalSearchScope.projectScope`. For symbols in large monorepos, expect multi-second latencies; the 120s default tool timeout can be hit on pathological symbols (every PSI element named `equals` / `hashCode` / `toString`).")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("The agent's 'Find Usages' (Cmd-Click ŌåÆ Find Usages in IntelliJ). Hand it a symbol name and it returns every place that symbol is read, called, or referenced ŌĆö same index-backed search the IDE shows in the Usages tool window, just textual output instead of a tree view.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Tool runs the global path: iterates `registry.allProviders()` until one resolves the symbol via `findSymbol`, then falls back to `PsiShortNamesCache` short-name lookup. First hit wins regardless of which file declares it. All registered providers (Java/Kotlin and Python) are tried, so pure-Python projects work correctly.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Top-level Kotlin functions / extension functions are not findable by bare name in the no-file-context path ŌĆö `findSymbol` walks `PsiShortNamesCache.getMethodsByName`, which indexes class members only. Workaround: pass `file` pointing at the containing `.kt` file, which uses provider resolution.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("Treats the 50-result cap as the true count ŌĆö output truncation appends `... (showing first 50 of N)` only when `references.size > 50`, but the LLM sometimes drops the footer when summarising and reports '50 references' as the total. The header line `References to '<symbol>' (N total)` is the authoritative count.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("What you're looking for ŌĆö typically a class name, method name, or field name. Bare names work; `Class#method` / FQN is NOT parsed by this tool's resolver (unlike `find_definition`), so for member disambiguation use the `file` hint instead.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("`file` parameter does double duty as a path-validation entry point AND a disambiguation hint, but the file-scoped fallback only handles `PsiJavaFile.classes.methods` ŌĆö non-Java files get path validation but no actual file-scoping benefit. ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("find_definition", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Inverse-direction pair: find_definition jumps to where a symbol is DECLARED, find_references lists where it is USED. ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("find_implementations", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "For interfaces and abstract methods, `find_references` tends to return the implementations and call-through sites; `find_implementations` returns ONLY the concrete subtype/override declarations. ArgumentListWrapping:FindReferencesTool.kt$FindReferencesTool$("search_code", Relationship.FALLBACK, "Use when no LanguageIntelligenceProvider exists for the language (JS/TS, Go, Rust today), when the symbol is dynamic (Python `getattr`, runtime-injected names), when find_references returns 'No symbol found', or when you specifically WANT to find string-literal / comment mentions that PSI references-search excludes by design.")
