ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$( "IDE is still indexing. format_code cannot run safely during indexing " + "because it may operate on incomplete PSI trees. Try again in a moment.", "Indexing", 5, isError = true )

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Assumes `.editorconfig` is honoured. The description mentions it but EditorConfig is only respected when IntelliJ's EditorConfig plugin is loaded AND the file is under a directory containing `.editorconfig`. In stripped-down IDE installs the file's settings are silently ignored and the IDE's global code style wins.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Calls format_code during indexing and gets the DUMB_MODE error, then immediately retries instead of waiting ŌĆö typically loops 3-5 times before indexing finishes.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Calls format_code immediately after edit_file without checking whether the edit changed anything that matters for formatting ŌĆö produces a no-op result and a wasted iteration. Worth flagging because the LLM tends to chain edit_file ŌåÆ format_code ŌåÆ optimize_imports unconditionally.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Calls format_code on non-source files (`format_code('README.md')`, `format_code('package.json')`) ŌĆö IntelliJ has formatters for Markdown/JSON/YAML so the call may succeed and silently rewrite the file in IDE-style (e.g., reflowing prose, alphabetising JSON keys). Surprising and rarely what the LLM wanted.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Code style comes from the IDE's loaded settings, not the project. `.editorconfig` is honoured only if the EditorConfig plugin is loaded; project-level scheme files (`.idea/codeStyles/`) are honoured only if IntelliJ has imported them. A formatter that 'just works' on the user's machine may produce different output in CI or for a teammate.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("CodeStyleManager.reformat operates on the PsiFile, not the on-disk bytes ŌĆö runs through the Document API. So if the file has unsaved editor changes, those get reformatted too (which is usually what the user wants, but worth knowing).")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("DumbService blocks during indexing ŌĆö first-time project open, after a Gradle/Maven sync, or after switching branches with dependency changes can mean format_code is unavailable for 30s-2min. The error message tells the LLM to retry but doesn't say when.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Even when before == after (no changes needed), the call still grabs the EDT, opens a write-command-action, and runs the reformatter ŌĆö costs ~50-200ms of EDT time. Cheap, but not free, and the LLM tends to call format_code reflexively.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Like clicking IntelliJ's 'Reformat Code' menu item, but driven by the agent. Tidies indentation, spacing, and line breaks in one file using whatever code style the IDE has loaded for that language ŌĆö the same style your hand-written edits would conform to if you used the IDE shortcut.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("No range/scope parameter ŌĆö always whole-file. Can't reformat 'just this method' or 'just lines 100-150'. If the user wants surgical reformatting they have to use edit_file by hand.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Path is canonicalised, validated against project boundaries via PathValidator, the corresponding VirtualFile is refreshed and resolved, and a write-command-action runs CodeStyleManager.reformat on the PsiFile.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Reformat a file via IntelliJ's CodeStyleManager.reformat on the resolved PsiFile, executed inside a WriteCommandAction on the EDT. Refuses to run while DumbService.isDumb is true (PSI may be incomplete during indexing). Compares document text before/after and emits a 'no changes needed' result when nothing changed; otherwise reports the count of changed lines.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Tries to format an entire directory by passing a folder path ŌĆö gets a parse error (`Cannot parse file:`) because PathValidator + PsiManager.findFile both reject directories. Should iterate file-by-file via glob_files first.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("Where the file lives ŌĆö relative to the project (`src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt`) or absolute (`/Users/me/proj/src/Main.kt`).")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("edit_file", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use instead when you only need to reflow a small region or fix a specific indent. format_code is whole-file reformat ŌĆö too blunt for surgical whitespace fixes.")
ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("must point inside the project root (or another allow-listed root) ŌĆö traversal is rejected before any I/O")
ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("must point to a parseable file ŌĆö directories and unknown file types fail with a 'Cannot parse file' error")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("optimize_imports", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Same shape (single `path` param, dumb-mode check, FILE_WRITE, write-command-action) but a different concern ŌĆö imports vs whitespace/indentation. Typical pairing after edit_file: optimize_imports first, then format_code.")

ArgumentListWrapping:FormatCodeTool.kt$FormatCodeTool$("run_inspections", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Inspections (e.g., 'Reformat code') flag style issues; format_code is one of the canonical fixes. ArgumentListWrapping:GetAnnotationsTool.kt$GetAnnotationsTool$("find_definition", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Canonical pair: use `find_definition` to locate the annotation class itself (see its declaration and " + "attributes), then `get_annotations` to see where it is applied and with what parameters. Especially " + "useful when the LLM is debugging 'why is this @Transactional not working' ŌĆö find_definition shows the " + "annotation definition; get_annotations shows the exact parameter values on the target element." )

ArgumentListWrapping:GetAnnotationsTool.kt$GetAnnotationsTool$("must resolve to a `PsiClass` ŌĆö top-level Kotlin functions, Kotlin objects, and Python module-level symbols may or may not resolve depending on the provider")

ArgumentListWrapping:GetAnnotationsTool.kt$GetAnnotationsTool$("search_code", Relationship.FALLBACK, "Use `search_code` when: (1) no `LanguageIntelligenceProvider` is registered for the language, " + "(2) the class name is unknown and you need to find all classes annotated with `@SomeAnnotation` across the " + "project (get_annotations requires knowing the class name upfront), or (3) you need to count how many " + "classes in a package carry a given annotation. search_code answers 'where is this annotation used?'; " + "get_annotations answers 'what annotations does this specific element have?'" )

ArgumentListWrapping:GetAnnotationsTool.kt$GetAnnotationsTool$("type_inference", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "After `type_inference` tells the LLM what type a variable or return value is, `get_annotations` tells " + "it what validation, serialization, or persistence rules apply to that type. Common workflow: infer the " + "type of a parameter, then get_annotations on the parameter's declared class to check `@Valid`, `@NotNull`, " + "and `@JsonProperty` mappings." )

ArgumentListWrapping:GetMethodBodyTool.kt$GetMethodBodyTool$("file_structure", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use file_structure to enumerate all methods in a class when you don't know the method name, " + "then call get_method_body once you've identified the target. file_structure gives the outline; " + "get_method_body fills in one method's body at a time." )

ArgumentListWrapping:GetMethodBodyTool.kt$GetMethodBodyTool$("find_definition", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Canonical upstream step: use find_definition first to confirm the method exists, see its " + "declared signature, and discover its file location. Then use get_method_body to retrieve the " + "full implementation. find_definition ŌåÆ get_method_body is the standard 'what's the contract, " + "what's the implementation' pipeline." )

ArgumentListWrapping:GetMethodBodyTool.kt$GetMethodBodyTool$("read_file", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use read_file when: (1) the method is a top-level Kotlin function or extension function " + "(get_method_body won't find it); (2) provider.getBody returns null and the stub comment appears; " + "(3) you want a large contiguous slice of a file including multiple adjacent methods; " + "(4) the class is in a library JAR without sources. read_file is the correct fallback whenever " + "get_method_body emits '// Source unavailable'." )

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("A wildcard expression that says which file names to keep. `**` means 'any depth of folders', `*` means 'any chars but /', `?` means 'one char'. Same syntax as `.gitignore` patterns.")
ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("After mtime-DESC sort, the first N matches are returned; result includes a `... (limited to N of M results)` footer when the cap clips.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Calls glob_files on a single file path expecting it to confirm existence ŌĆö returns 'directory not found'. Should use read_file (existence check is implicit) or trust the prior tool's success message, which the description explicitly warns about.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Caps the result count ŌĆö like `head -50` on a long listing. The walk over-collects (2x cap) to ensure mtime-DESC sort returns the truly newest files, then trims.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Compiled to a `java.nio.file.PathMatcher` (`glob:` syntax) and tested against each visited path three ways: relative-to-search-root, relative-to-project-root, and bare filename ŌĆö so `*.kt` at any depth still matches even when search_root is the project root.")
ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Error: directory not found: $path", "Error: directory not found", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Error: invalid glob pattern '$pattern': ${e.message}", "Error: invalid pattern", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Forgets `**/` for recursive descent ŌĆö `*.kt` from project root only matches top-level files (zero in most projects). The pattern needs `**/*.kt` to recurse.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Glob matching is case-sensitive on Linux/macOS and case-insensitive on Windows (NIO PathMatcher inherits filesystem semantics) ŌĆö patterns that work on the developer's macOS may behave differently in CI on Linux when filenames differ in case.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Glob pattern to match files against (e.g., '**/*.kt' for all Kotlin files, 'src/**/*.java' for Java files under src, '*.xml' for top-level XML files, 'build.gradle*' for Gradle build files).")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Like `find -name` in a terminal, but it knows to skip junk folders (.git, node_modules, build) and shows you the most recently changed files first. Good for 'where do my Kotlin test files live?' kinds of questions.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Resolved + canonicalized via `PathValidator.resolveAndValidateForRead`. Search walks from this directory; emitted match paths are relative-to-project when the file is inside the project, absolute otherwise.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Returns mtime-DESC order rather than relevance/lexicographic ŌĆö recent test churn can push a freshly-touched throwaway file to the top of an `**/*.kt` listing, hiding the canonical match.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Skip-list is hard-coded ŌĆö there is no way to opt-in to scanning `.gradle/` or `node_modules/` even when the user explicitly wants to (e.g. inspecting a published artifact's pom). The agent must fall back to `run_command find` for those cases.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Symlinks are followed by `Files.walkFileTree` with default options, so a symlink loop inside the project can stall the walk. No cycle protection beyond the OS-level `FileSystemLoopException` (which is silently swallowed by `visitFileFailed`).")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("The path of the directory to list contents for (absolute or relative to the project root). May also point under ~/.workflow-orchestrator/ to find spilled tool output. Defaults to project root.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Treats the response order as alphabetical and writes follow-up code that assumes sort stability ŌĆö results are sorted by mtime DESC, so the same pattern returns a different order between runs as files are touched.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Uses glob_files to find content (e.g. `pattern='**/*TODO*.kt'` to find TODO comments) ŌĆö glob matches on file names only, not file contents. Should use search_code with a regex.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Walks the filesystem from a directory root using `java.nio.file.PathMatcher` (`glob:` syntax), prunes a hard-coded set of build/VCS dirs (.git, node_modules, build, target, .gradle, etc.), and returns matches sorted by mtime newest-first. PathValidator-restricted to the project root or `~/.workflow-orchestrator/`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("Where to start the search. Relative paths anchor on the project root; absolute paths must still resolve under the project (or under the agent's `~/.workflow-orchestrator/` data dir for read tools).")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("`.gitignore` is not honoured ŌĆö the skip-list is a fixed set of common build/VCS dirs, so generated/vendored files outside that list (e.g. `vendor/`, `target/generated-sources/` once the path crosses outside `target/`) will appear in matches.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("`max_results * 2` early-termination means very-broad patterns (`**/*`) on large repos can return a non-deterministic 'first 100 we hit during walk' set rather than the globally-newest files. The mtime sort applies only to the over-collected candidates.")
ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("file_structure", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Glob to find a candidate file, then file_structure to see its top-level shape before reading.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("find_definition", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use instead when looking for a symbol's source file ŌĆö PSI knows where `class Foo` lives without a filename guess.")

ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("must be valid `glob:` syntax ŌĆö `**`, `*`, `?`, character classes `[abc]`, and brace alternation `{a,b}` are supported; an unbalanced `[` or `{` returns 'invalid glob pattern' before any I/O")
ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("must resolve under the project root or `~/.workflow-orchestrator/` ŌĆö paths outside both are rejected by PathValidator before any walk")
ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("read_file", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Glob first to discover candidate files, then read_file the most-recently-modified match.")
ArgumentListWrapping:GlobFilesTool.kt$GlobFilesTool$("search_code", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use instead when matching on file CONTENTS (regex on what's inside) rather than file NAMES.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("After editing source in a module to verify it still compiles, or after an upstream module change to catch downstream ABI breakage in dependents. Also after `run_command mvn install` ŌĆö set refresh_maven_first=true so the compile sees the freshly-published artifacts.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("After run_tests reports failures, to iterate quickly on just the failing subset without re-running the whole class.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("All three methods run in one JUnit 5 PATTERNS launch; aggregated output returned as a single ToolResult.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Brief description of what this action does and why (shown to user in approval dialog) ŌĆö for run_tests, compile_module, rerun_failed_tests")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Compiles a module via CompilerManager.make on either a module compile scope (with optional includeDependents) or the whole project compile scope when module is omitted. Wraps in a 120s timeout; errors return per-file messages via formatCompileErrors. Optional refresh_maven_first triggers MavenProjectsManager reimport (forceUpdateAllProjects, or addManagedFilesOrUnignore for unimported pom.xml) and awaits up to 30s before compile.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Compiles myapp.api plus every module that depends on it ŌĆö surfaces any downstream ABI breakage.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Defaults to false ŌĆö compiles against IntelliJ's current in-memory model, which can be stale after a CLI Maven build.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Error running tests: ${e.message}", "Test execution error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Executes Maven goals through IntelliJ's Maven plugin runner (MavenRunConfiguration). The run appears in the Run tool window and honors the IDE-configured Maven home / JRE / settings.xml / VM options. Builds an ephemeral, transient run configuration, launches it via the IDE Run executor, captures process output through the descriptor's ProcessHandler, and disposes everything through a RunInvocation. 20-minute timeout.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("ExecutionEnvironmentBuilder.createOrNull returned null (no runner registered for this configuration)")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Free-form Maven flags appended after the goals ŌĆö for run_maven_goal (e.g. \"-DskipTests -Pdev -T 4 -U\"). Tokenized respecting quotes.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Fully qualified test class name (required for run_tests ŌĆö use test_finder to discover classes)")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("How the agent runs Java/Kotlin tests and compiles modules without shelling out. It clicks the green Run arrow on JUnit/TestNG tests, compiles a module the way the Build menu would, and reruns only the tests that just failed ŌĆö using IntelliJ's own machinery so results show up in the Test Results tool window the user is used to seeing.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Java/Kotlin runtime execution meta-tool wrapping IntelliJ's JUnit/TestNG native runner, CompilerManager-driven module compilation, and a failed-tests rerun path. Registered only when the Java plugin is present (ToolRegistrationFilter.shouldRegisterJavaBuildTools). run_tests routes through a native-runner-first / Maven-Gradle-shell-fallback dispatcher with a BuildSystemValidator pre-flight and a per-launch RunInvocation for deterministic disposal.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("MavenProjectsManager.forceUpdateAllProjectsOrFindAllAvailablePomFiles fires (or addManagedFilesOrUnignore for fresh-clone projects); compile waits up to 30s for the reimport to finish.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Name or partial name of the prior test session to rerun failures from ŌĆö for rerun_failed_tests. Defaults to most-recent test session when omitted.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Only the named methods run; multi-method uses JUnit 5 PATTERNS on the native runner, or shell fallback for TestNG.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Optional Maven module list ŌĆö for run_maven_goal. Prepends -pl <csv> -am to scope a multi-module build to these modules and their dependencies.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Optional method (or comma-separated methods) to scope the run to. Omit to run the whole class.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Re-runs only the failed/errored tests from a prior test session. Resolves the most-recent test descriptor via RunContentManager.allDescriptors (or one matching session_id), extracts FAILED/ERROR tests via collectTestResults, then builds a filtered JUnit/TestNG run configuration via reflection (pattern mode for multi-class, class mode for single-class) and launches it through the same RunInvocation path as run_tests.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Reimport Maven before compiling so the build picks up newly-installed JARs from ~/.m2.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Reruns only the FAILED/ERROR tests from the last test session via a filtered [Rerun] configuration.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Returns 'Compilation of <target> successful' (with warning count when non-zero), or per-file compile errors via formatCompileErrors when errors > 0.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Returns a header (goals, modules, working dir, Maven home, exit code, duration) followed by the Maven output; large output spills to disk with a preview.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Returns aggregated rerun output for the filtered configuration covering only the FAILED/ERROR tests; returns an informational (non-error) result when the prior session had 0 failures.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Returns aggregated test output prefixed with a breadcrumb ('Running tests in module: X (Build path: Y, N test methods detected in Z)'); shell fallback parses Surefire/Gradle reports; empty suites return NO_TESTS_FOUND.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Runs JUnit/TestNG tests for a specific class. Native runner path uses IntelliJ's JUnit configuration + ProgramRunnerUtil; falls back to Maven/Gradle shell when native setup fails or use_native_runner=false. Multi-method via JUnit 5 PATTERNS field; TestNG with 2+ methods auto-routes to shell. Pre-flight BuildSystemValidator catches main-source classes, zero @Test methods, missing settings.gradle entries. RunInvocation guarantees process+listener disposal on every exit path.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Runs `mvn <goals>` the way the IDE's Maven tool window would ŌĆö using your configured Maven home, JDK, and settings.xml ŌĆö instead of shelling out to the `mvn` on PATH.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Runs `mvn clean install -DskipTests` via the IDE Maven runner; result appears in the Run tool window.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Runs the specified test class (and optionally specific methods inside it). Uses the IDE's real test runner so results appear in the Test Results panel; falls back to `mvn test` / `./gradlew test` if the native runner can't be set up.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Seconds before the test run or module compile is killed (default: 300, max: 900) ŌĆö for run_tests and compile_module")
ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Selects the test descriptor whose displayName contains this string (case-insensitive).")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Space-separated Maven goals (required for run_maven_goal), e.g. \"clean install\" or \"dependency:tree\". " + "Maven flags like -DskipTests may be appended here or passed via extra_args.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Test method name(s) ŌĆö for run_tests. Single: 'testFoo'. " + "Multiple methods from the same class in one launch: 'testFoo,testBar,testBaz' " + "(comma-separated, whitespace around commas is trimmed). JUnit 5 and " + "Maven/Gradle shell support multi-method natively; TestNG auto-falls back to shell.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("The 'Rerun Failed Tests' button ŌĆö re-runs just the tests that just failed instead of the whole class. Returns a friendly informational message when there's nothing to rerun.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("To execute a specific JUnit/TestNG test class after writing or fixing tests, to verify a change, or to drive a TDD red-green loop. Use test_finder first if the class name isn't known.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("To run Maven goals (clean, install, dependency:tree, etc.) so they use the IDE's Maven config and show up in the Run tool window. Prefer over run_command(\"mvn ...\") for goal execution.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Triggers IntelliJ's Build for a module ŌĆö same as Build > Build Module ŌĆö and reports compile errors per file. ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("Use IntelliJ native test runner (true) or Maven/Gradle shell (false). Default: true ŌĆö for run_tests")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("When true (and `module` is set), also recompile modules that depend on the target ŌĆö catches downstream ABI breakage after editing an upstream module. Default: false ŌĆö for compile_module")

ArgumentListWrapping:JavaRuntimeExecTool.kt$JavaRuntimeExecTool$("When true, trigger a Maven reimport (MavenProjectsManager.forceUpdateAllProjects or addManagedFilesOrUnignore for unimported pom.xml) and wait up to 30s for it to finish before compiling ŌĆö picks up classpath changes from a prior `run_command mvn install`. ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("'Start work' on a ticket ŌĆö creates a branch named after it AND moves the ticket to In Progress in one step.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("'true' adds a server-side assignee=currentUser() filter; 'false' returns every issue in the sprint.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("17 actions in one tool is at the top end of what's reasonable. By rough usage intuition, ~5 actions (`get_ticket`, `search_tickets`, `search_issues`, `transition`, `comment`) carry 80%+ of the value. The remaining 12 are tail-frequency.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("401 Unauthorized", "Token expired or revoked. Stop and ask the user to re-enter the token in Settings.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("A second async call runs `service.getFullDevStatus(key)` in parallel; result is appended.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Adds a comment to the ticket. Pre-flight: verifies ticket exists via getTicket before posting.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Adds remote-link data (Confluence pages, Figma mocks, external URLs) to the response.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Adds the Dev Status panel data (branches, PRs, commits, builds, deployments, reviews) to the response.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Adds the current user's permission flags on this project ŌĆö useful before attempting a write action that might 403.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Application Links not configured", "Empty list ŌĆö Dev Status panel needs BitbucketŌåöJira app link configured server-side.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Asks Jira 'from this state, where can this ticket go?' Always call this before `transition` because transition IDs are workflow-specific.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Before any `transition` call when the LLM doesn't already know the right transition_id (which is essentially always).")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Brief description of this action shown to user in approval dialog ŌĆö recommended for transition, comment, start_work")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Coerced to typed FieldValues via parseFieldsJson; passed to TicketTransitionService.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Composite action: creates a Git branch off source_branch and (per JiraService semantics) typically transitions the ticket to In Progress.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Could be folded into get_ticket as `include_comments=true` ŌĆö saves one action slot. Tradeoff: get_ticket already has 4 fan-outs; adding a 5th lengthens the description.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Depends on Jira being available and the user's token being valid. Network outages and 401s are common failure modes that the LLM has to handle gracefully (don't retry 401).")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Downloads a Jira attachment to disk. If it's an image and image-autoload is on, the agent can also see it directly. If it's a PDF/Word/Excel/etc., the response includes a hint pointing at `read_document` to extract text.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Downloads an attachment to {sessionDir}/downloads/jira-{attachmentId}/{filename}. For image MIMEs (when PluginSettings.enableToolImageAutoload + whitelist match), bytes also load into the per-session AttachmentStore as imageRefs so BrainRouter routes the next call through /.api/completions/stream. For document classes (PDF/DOC/DOCX/etc.), appends a `read_document` hint paragraph.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Drop candidate. Overlaps semantically with search_tickets, which is strictly more powerful (JQL > board filter). Removing this action saves a description line and removes a 'which to use?' decision for the LLM.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Each Jira instance has different custom fields. Custom-field IDs (`customfield_10412`) are project-specific and only discoverable via the MissingFields payload ŌĆö the LLM cannot guess them from the field's display name.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Field values to set as part of the transition (e.g. resolution = Fixed). Format varies by field type ŌĆö see the constraints.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("File saved + imageRefs populated; next LLM call sees the image via BrainRouter's stream route.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("File saved at {sessionDir}/downloads/jira-10042/design.pdf; response includes a `read_document` hint paragraph for PDF extraction.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Filter results to entries logged by this specific user. Accepts Jira username (DC) or accountId (Cloud).")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Filter to the current user's tickets only ('true'/'false'). search_issues defaults 'true'; get_sprint_issues defaults 'false' (whole sprint) and applies it server-side as assignee=currentUser().")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("First attempt without `fields` returned MissingFields; LLM fetched the field id from the payload and retried.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Fold `get_dev_branches` and `get_linked_prs` into `get_ticket(include_dev_status=true)` with optional sub-flags (`dev_status_only=branches|prs`). Saves 2 action slots; tradeoff is the include-flag signature growing.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Forbidden", "Returns `payload_type: forbidden`. User lacks the Transition Issues permission.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Free-text issue search. Defaults: max_results=20, current_user_only=true. Server translates to a `text ~ '...'` JQL roughly.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Frequent enough ŌĆö every multi-step task tends to want a status comment at the end.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("High-value composition. Replaces 3 calls with 1 and matches the daily 'start work' workflow.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Highest drop-candidate among the 17 actions. Users who want time-log readback typically open Jira directly. Removing this action would save its description line (~20 tokens) and one schema slot. Keep only if action-level usage shows non-trivial calls.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("How long was spent ŌĆö Jira's compact format. Hours and minutes; days/weeks if your project allows them.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("If true, also fetch remote links (Confluence pages, external URLs linked from this ticket). Default false.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("If true, also fetch the ticket's status/assignee/priority change history. Default false.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("If true, also fetch the user's permissions on this ticket's project (transition, comment, log work, watch). Default false. Useful for the LLM to check before attempting an action that may 403.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("InvalidTransition", "Returns `payload_type: invalid_transition` with reason. Usually the transition_id doesn't apply to the current status ŌĆö call get_transitions and pick a valid one.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("JQL is the most powerful Jira primitive; LLMs are surprisingly good at writing it.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Jira Cloud and Jira DC have subtly different APIs (notably user identification: Cloud uses accountId, DC uses name). The current implementation targets DC; Cloud projects may surface format mismatches in field values.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Jira issue key (e.g. PROJ-123) ŌĆö used across all actions that operate on a single issue")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Lists worklog entries for a ticket. Accepts `key`, `issue_key`, or `issue_id` for compat.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Lists worklogs authored by the current user across all tickets. Supports optional date-range filtering via `since`/`until`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Logs time against a ticket. Pre-flight: verifies ticket exists. Time format must be Jira-compatible (`2h`, `30m`, `1h 30m`).")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Low frequency ŌĆö sprint info changes rarely and tends to be a UI lookup. Drop candidate if usage tracking confirms.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("MissingFields", "Returns isError=true with `payload_type: missing_required_fields`, the list of (id, name, required, schema) entries, and guidance text. LLM should call ask_followup_question per field, then retry with `fields={...}`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Moves a ticket to a new status. Delegates to TicketTransitionService.executeTransition which validates required fields. On MissingFields error, returns a typed payload with field metadata so the LLM can collect values via ask_followup_question and retry.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Network", "Returns `payload_type: network_error` with cause. LLM should pause briefly before retry; persistent network failure ŌåÆ ask user to check VPN.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Note: ${bundle.fetchErrors} of 6 feeds errored ŌĆö result may be incomplete.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Note: could not resolve current user identity ŌĆö results may include " + "worklogs by other users on these tickets.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Observationally low-frequency: most users log time via the IDE's time-tracking UI or via Tempo ŌĆö not via the agent. Could be a drop candidate if action-level usage tracking confirmed.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Optional ISO 8601 datetime when the work was performed (e.g. '2026-05-10T09:00:00+00:00' or '2026-05-10') ŌĆö for log_work. Backdating worklogs requires the underlying user to have permission; otherwise Jira returns 403. Defaults to 'now' when omitted.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Optional ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD or full ISO 8601) lower bound ŌĆö for get_worklogs, my_worklogs. Inclusive.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Optional ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD or full ISO 8601) upper bound ŌĆö for get_worklogs, my_worklogs. Inclusive.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Optional Jira username/accountId filter ŌĆö for get_worklogs (returns only worklogs by this user)")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Optional field values for transition screens ŌĆö for transition. Keys are Jira field IDs (e.g. \"assignee\", \"labels\", \"priority\"). Values follow the Jira field-value format: user={\"name\":\"username\"}, labels=[\"l1\",\"l2\"], option/priority={\"id\":\"id\"}, multi-select=[{\"id\":\"a\"},{\"id\":\"b\"}], cascading={\"value\":\"parent\",\"child\":{\"value\":\"child\"}}.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Overlaps with `get_ticket(include_dev_status=true)`. Could be replaced by guidance to use the include flag ŌĆö saves one action slot.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Overlaps with `get_ticket(include_dev_status=true)`. Could fold into that.", VerdictSeverity.WEAK)
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Parallel async call to `service.getMyPermissions(projectKey)`; result appended as `Permissions` block.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Parallel async call to `service.getRemoteLinks(key)`; result appended as `Remote Links` block.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Press a button in Jira's workflow ŌĆö 'Resolve Issue', 'Start Progress', etc. If the workflow asks for extra fields (like a Resolution dropdown), the tool tells you exactly which fields and how to format them, then you call again with the values.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Rare ŌĆö only when the LLM needs to discover board IDs to chain into get_sprints / get_board_issues.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Rarely. Most LLM queries that look like 'show me all open tickets' translate to JQL via search_tickets, which is more flexible.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Rate limiting: Jira Cloud throttles aggressively on 429; DC rarely does. The LLM has no rate-limit awareness and may spam retries on 429.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Raw JQL pass-through. Default max_results=8 (smaller than search_issues to keep token cost down on broad queries).")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Reads everything about a ticket. Like opening the ticket in Jira and clicking through every tab in one go ŌĆö basics, dev status (PRs, builds, deploys), linked design docs, change history, your permissions on it.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Replace `get_board_issues` with guidance to use `search_tickets` + JQL `sprint in openSprints()` or similar.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Required precondition for `transition`. Cannot be skipped because Jira's transition IDs are workflow-specific.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Required precondition for setting user-picker fields in `transition`. Without it the LLM guesses usernames and gets 400 errors.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("RequiresInteraction", "Returns `payload_type: requires_interaction` ŌĆö the transition has a screen with conditional/scripted fields the agent can't satisfy. Surface to user.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Returns Dev Status branch list for a ticket. Subset of `get_ticket(include_dev_status=true)`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Returns PRs linked from the ticket via Dev Status / Application Links. Subset of `get_ticket(include_dev_status=true)`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Returns a list of `(id, name, toStatus)` triples ŌĆö e.g. `ID 31: Start Work ŌåÆ In Progress`, `ID 41: Resolve Issue ŌåÆ Resolved`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Returns a single text block: ticket header (key, summary, status, assignee, reporter, priority, fix versions, etc.) followed by 0-4 fan-out sections in order ŌĆö Dev Status, Remote Links, History, Permissions. Token estimate sums all blocks.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Returns available next-status transitions for the ticket ŌĆö each with id, name, toStatus.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Returns profile(s) matching 'Jane Smith'; LLM picks the right username for the transition `fields` param.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Returns the current user's worklog entries (ticket key, time spent, started date, comment) across all projects.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Returns the ticket fields plus 0-4 optional fan-out blocks (dev status, remote links, history, permissions). Each include_* flag spawns a parallel async call; results merge into one text block.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Returns ticket plus a Permissions block listing granted/denied flags so LLM can decide whether to even attempt a transition.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Returns: filepath on disk, filename, mime, size. For images that pass autoload: response includes imageRefs so the next LLM turn sees the image. For documents: appends a `read_document` hint paragraph with the absolute path.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Saves 4-5 follow-up tool calls (get_dev_branches, get_linked_prs, etc.) by fanning out in parallel.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Search query for user lookup ŌĆö for user_search. Matches displayName, username, and email.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Searches Jira users by display name, username, or email. Returns a list of matching user profiles.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Shows all time entries logged by you ŌĆö across all tickets ŌĆö optionally scoped to a date window.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("State machine moves are the highest-value Jira write op ŌĆö they're what wraps a coding task. The MissingFields contract is what makes this safe vs guessing.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("The most-used Jira action. Token cost of the fan-out flags pays for itself by avoiding 3-4 follow-up calls.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("The numeric ID of the workflow transition to invoke. Comes from `get_transitions`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("The numeric attachment id from the ticket. Get it from `get_ticket` (which lists attachments).")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Transition `comment` parameter and `comment` action's `body` parameter are different things ŌĆö easy to confuse.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Useful for quick 'what did I log this week' queries without needing a ticket key.", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Validated by ToolValidation.validateJiraKey (uppercase letters, dash, digits) before the request.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When narrowly asked 'what PRs are linked to this ticket' and the LLM doesn't need the rest of the dev panel.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When narrowly asked 'what branches are on this ticket' ŌĆö e.g. before deciding which branch to check out.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When the LLM has a JQL string in mind ŌĆö typically because the prompt mentions specific JQL operators, dates, or status names.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When the LLM needs to resolve a human name ('John Doe') to a Jira username/accountId before setting an assignee or reviewer field.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When the user asks 'leave a comment saying X' or wants to record context on a ticket without changing its status.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When the user asks 'log 2 hours on PROJ-123' or after completing a multi-hour task they want tracked.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When the user asks 'what did I log this week' or wants a summary of their own time entries.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When the user asks 'what was discussed on this ticket' or the LLM needs to understand prior decisions before acting.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When the user has natural-language intent ('find my open auth tickets') rather than a JQL string.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When the user references a screenshot, design doc, or report attached to a ticket and asks the agent to use it.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When the user says 'start work on PROJ-123' or 'I'm picking up X' ŌĆö saves three sequential calls (create branch, transition, comment).")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When the work was performed. Omit to use the current time; provide a past datetime to backdate the entry (requires permission).")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When true, get_ticket also embeds the full dev panel (branches, PRs, commits, builds, deployments, reviews) in the response. Default false. Use for broad questions like 'has this been deployed' or 'what's the status of this ticket'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("When true, only your assigned issues are returned, resolved server-side the same way the Sprint tab does.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Whenever the user mentions a ticket key (`PROJ-1234`) or asks 'what's the status of X'. The default call is just (key); add include_* flags when the user asks broader questions like 'has this been deployed' (ŌåÆ include_dev_status) or 'who changed this' (ŌåÆ include_history).")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Whenever the user says 'resolve this', 'start work on X', 'close PROJ-123', etc. Always preceded by `get_transitions` to find the right id.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Which ticket's worklogs to fetch. `issue_key` and `issue_id` are also accepted as aliases.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Without `jira` the LLM has to shell out via curl with the user's bearer token in the command line ŌĆö a security regression that bypasses PasswordSafe. The 17 actions consolidated into one tool (with action-enum dispatch) cost ~1 schema entry instead of ~17, saving ~3-5K tokens per iteration once activated. Conditional registration means zero cost when Jira isn't configured. The MissingFields typed contract on transitions is a meaningfully better experience than parsing raw 400 bodies.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Without this, screenshots/design-docs attached to tickets are invisible to the agent. The image-autoload integration is what makes 'look at this screenshot on the ticket' a one-call workflow.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("Workflows differ per project ŌĆö what's transition_id=31 for one project may not exist for another. `get_transitions` is mandatory before `transition`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("`description` is recommended-but-not-required across write actions (transition, comment, start_work). Could either be required (forcing better approval-dialog UX) or removed (relying on action+key fallback).")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("`description` parameter is marked recommended for write actions but isn't enforced ŌĆö the approval dialog shows action+key when it's missing, which is less informative than a per-call description.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("`get_board_issues` overlaps semantically with `search_tickets`. JQL is strictly more powerful; the board-issue listing is mostly a UI affordance.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("`get_dev_branches` and `get_linked_prs` are narrower slices of `get_ticket(include_dev_status=true)`. Could be removed and replaced by guidance to use the include flag ŌĆö saves ~2 action description lines.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("`get_worklogs` is plausibly the lowest-frequency action. Time-log readback is rarely the LLM's job.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("`key`, `issue_key`, and `issue_id` are all accepted as aliases for the same parameter on several actions (get_worklogs, get_linked_prs, get_dev_branches, start_work). Defensive but undocumented; LLMs may use any of the three based on prior prompts.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("`search_issues` and `search_tickets` could be merged via a `query_kind: text|jql` discriminator. Tradeoff: small token savings; clarity slightly worse.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("ask_followup_question", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "On `transition` MissingFields, the LLM uses ask_followup_question for each missing field, then retries `transition`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("attachment not found", "404 surfaced; LLM should re-fetch get_ticket to get a fresh attachment list.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("autoload off / mime not whitelisted / no session store", "Falls back to text-only download confirmation; no imageRefs added. The file is still on disk; LLM can `read_file` it directly for text formats or `read_document` for documents.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("bamboo_builds", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Build status feeds into the ticket's Dev Status panel ŌĆö visible via `get_ticket(include_dev_status=true)`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("bitbucket_pr", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Tickets often embed PR links; use `jira(get_linked_prs)` then `bitbucket_pr(get_pr_detail)` to bridge the two systems.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("bitbucket_repo", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "After `jira(start_work)` creates a branch, use `bitbucket_repo` to push and `bitbucket_pr(create_pr)` to open a PR.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("for image autoload: PluginSettings.enableToolImageAutoload is true AND mime is in toolImageAutoloadMimeWhitelist AND the agent loop is wrapped by AgentLoopAttachmentScope (provides SessionAttachmentAccess.current())")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("invalid JQL syntax", "Jira returns a 400 with the syntax error message; surface verbatim to LLM so it can fix the query.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("issue not found", "Returns a 404-shaped error with the issue key. LLM should re-confirm with the user (often a typo).")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("kanban board (no sprints)", "Empty list ŌĆö LLM should recognize as 'this isn't a scrum board'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("one fan-out errors but ticket succeeds", "Failed fan-out is silently dropped (`takeIf { !it.isError }`). The successful ticket block plus any other successful fan-outs are returned. Partial result is the design.")

ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("read_document", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "After `download_attachment` of a PDF/DOC/etc., the LLM uses read_document on the returned filepath to extract text.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("sonar", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "When a quality gate fails on a PR linked to a ticket, the agent reads sonar issues + jira ticket to compose the fix.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("source_branch not found", "Git error surfaced; LLM should re-confirm with user (often a typo or default branch name mismatch).")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("ticket not found", "Returns isError=true with explicit `Cannot add comment: ticket PROJ-1234 not found.` LLM should re-confirm key with user.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("transition forbidden", "Branch may still be created; surface the partial-success state to user.")
ArgumentListWrapping:JiraTool.kt$JiraTool$("user lacks transition permission", "Empty list returned by Jira (not an error). LLM has to recognize the empty case as 'you can't transition this'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$( "IDE is still indexing. optimize_imports cannot run safely during indexing " + "because it may incorrectly identify used imports as unused. Try again in a moment.", "Indexing", 5, isError = true )

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Assumes the call wrote bytes and tries to read the file to verify, but optimize_imports returns 'already optimal' without writing ŌĆö the no-op short-circuit means no actual disk change. The summary string is the source of truth for whether anything happened.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Both `optimize_imports` and `format_code` are thin wrappers over IntelliJ bulk-transform actions on a single file. Same DumbService gate, same WriteCommandAction wrapper, same no-op detection. See auditNotes for a possible merge.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("CRITICAL: depends on `DumbService.isDumb(project)` being false. Without the guard, running during indexing could remove imports the IDE just hasn't finished resolving yet, silently corrupting the file. The guard is load-bearing ŌĆö do not remove it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Calls it BEFORE writing the code that uses the new symbols ŌĆö the optimizer can't add an import for a reference the file doesn't have yet, and may strip imports it thinks are unused. Optimize AFTER edit_file, not before.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Calls it during indexing, gets the 'still indexing' error, and immediately retries instead of waiting ŌĆö burns iterations on the same DumbService rejection.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Drops 'unused' imports that have side effects at load time (Scala `language.implicitConversions`, JS/TS reflect-metadata, Kotlin `@file:` companion markers). The optimizer has no concept of side-effect imports ŌĆö if you have any, you're better off using edit_file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Like clicking IntelliJ's 'Code ŌåÆ Optimize Imports' menu item on a single file. The agent uses this after editing code to clean up the import block ŌĆö pulling in references it just used, dropping ones the edit made stale, and sorting them the way the project prefers.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("No-op detection compares full text, not just imports ŌĆö if the optimizer reorders comments adjacent to the import block, that counts as a change. Usually fine, occasionally surprising.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Operates on PSI, so the file must be parseable. A syntax error elsewhere in the file blocks optimization ŌĆö the tool returns a parse error rather than partial work.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("PathValidator canonicalises the path, refuses traversal outside the project, then the file is loaded into PSI and the language-appropriate ImportOptimizer runs on it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Per-language behaviour varies ŌĆö Java optimizer aggressively merges to star-imports past a threshold; Kotlin's never does; Python's puts each import on its own line. The LLM gets whichever the project's IDE settings prefer, and can't override per-call.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Runs IntelliJ's per-language ImportOptimizer on a single file inside a WriteCommandAction on the EDT ŌĆö adds missing imports the IDE can resolve, removes unused ones, and reorders/groups them per project code style. Gated by DumbService.isDumb so it never executes during indexing. Compares text before/after and short-circuits to 'already optimal' when nothing changed.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Runs optimize_imports on a file with an intentional side-effect-only import (e.g. `import scala.language.implicitConversions`, `import 'reflect-metadata'` in TS, or a Kotlin file-level `@file:JvmName` companion that touches a marker class) ŌĆö the optimizer drops it as 'unused', breaking the build at runtime. The LLM has no way to know the import was load-bearing.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Skips it after a refactor that removed code ŌĆö the unused imports linger and confuse the next edit_file round (the LLM sees stale `import X` and assumes X is still in scope).")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Strong candidate to merge with `format_code` into a single `transform(kind=imports|format|both)` tool ŌĆö both wrap an IDE bulk-transform action on a single file, share the DumbService gate + WriteCommandAction frame + no-op detection, and are typically chained after edit_file. Merging would halve the schema cost and let the LLM combine 'optimize then format' into one tool call (today it takes two). The trade-off: separate tools make the LLM's intent self-documenting in traces.")
ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("Which file to clean up. Path can be relative to the project root or absolute, just like read_file / edit_file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("diagnostics", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Run after optimize_imports to confirm no 'unresolved reference' errors remain ŌĆö the optimizer can occasionally fail to add an import if the symbol is ambiguous between two libraries.")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("edit_file", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use edit_file when you need fine-grained control over a specific import (e.g. preserving a side-effect-only import the optimizer would drop, or adding a single import without touching anything else).")

ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("format_code", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Sibling tool. Same shape (single file, EDT WriteCommandAction, DumbService-gated, no-op detection). Pair them after edit_file: optimize_imports first, then format_code ŌĆö the optimizer can change line counts that formatting then re-aligns.")
ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("must have an ImportOptimizer registered for its language ŌĆö exotic file types return 'No import optimizer available'")
ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("must point inside the project root ŌĆö `../etc/passwd`-style paths are rejected before any IDE work")
ArgumentListWrapping:OptimizeImportsTool.kt$OptimizeImportsTool$("must point to a file the IDE recognises (PSI parseable) ŌĆö unknown extensions return 'Cannot parse'")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("AgentController prepends accumulatedPlanText to the new planText before writing to disk and rendering the plan card ŌĆö the result is the full combined plan, not just the continuation.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Allowed only for the orchestrator (`allowedWorkers = {ORCHESTRATOR}`). Sub-agents cannot present plans ŌĆö they must use task_report instead. Worth knowing before composing plans inside a delegated worker.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Available only in plan mode (schema filter strips it from tool definitions in act mode). The LLM occasionally tries to call it after the user approves and act mode resumes ŌĆö the call fails the schema check and the iteration is wasted.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Boolean-coercion fallback for `needs_more_exploration` is intentional but masks a class of LLM bugs: passing `\"True\"` (capital T from some models) is silently coerced; passing `\"yes\"` is silently treated as false. No warning surfaced.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Calls plan_mode_respond for conversational replies (answering a question, acknowledging feedback) ŌĆö the tool is for materially new or revised plans only; ordinary chat should be plain text. The description hardens this but the LLM still over-reaches, especially after compaction.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Captured as the PlanResponse tool result content; persisted as a PLAN_UPDATE UI message so it survives session resume; rendered in the plan document viewer for inline commenting.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Defaults to false ŌĆö AgentLoop calls userInputChannel.receive() and suspends until the user sends a message (typed feedback, inline step comments, or Approve click). This is the load-bearing default; flipping it accidentally turns plan mode into an unbounded auto-loop.")
ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Defaults to false ŌĆö the new response replaces the current plan entirely. This is correct for fresh plans and revised plans.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Escape hatch: 'I started writing the plan but realized I need to read more files first.' When true, the loop keeps running instead of pausing for your review.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("If true: AgentLoop emits the plan to the UI but does NOT suspend ŌĆö the loop continues immediately so the LLM can read more files, search, etc. The plan rendered is treated as a draft.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Includes nested tool invocations or tool-use XML inside the `response` markdown ŌĆö the parameter is a markdown document only, no tools execute from inside it.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Like a draft proposal a manager submits before doing the work ŌĆö the agent writes up what it intends to do, hands it to you, and waits at the door until you say go (or scribble in the margins). The agent cannot resume on its own.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Loop suspension is one-way ŌĆö only the user can transition out (typed message into userInputChannel, inline comment, or Approve click). If the user goes idle, the loop sits forever; there is no programmatic timeout or self-rescue path for the LLM.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Only set to true in response to an output-length truncation nudge or when following up on a needs_more_exploration=true call; never for first-call or wholesale revision")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Plan-mode communication primitive: emits a markdown plan as a PlanResponse tool result; when needs_more_exploration=false, AgentLoop suspends on userInputChannel.receive() until the user types feedback or clicks Approve, at which point planModeActive flips off and tool definitions are rebuilt for act mode.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Presents the plan as plain assistant text outside this tool ŌĆö gets nudged by the act-mode/plan-mode separation but the bare text is not rendered as a plan card, has no inline-comment surface, and the loop does not suspend. Should always wrap plan content in this tool when in plan mode.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Set to true if while formulating your response you found you need to do more exploration with tools, for example reading files. (Remember, you can explore the project with tools like read_file in PLAN MODE without the user having to toggle to ACT MODE.) Defaults to false if not specified.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Set to true when continuing after a truncation nudge OR after a successful call with needs_more_exploration=true. The new response is appended to the existing plan content in the editor ŌĆö start exactly where the previous content was cut off and do not repeat earlier sections. Defaults to false (replaces the current plan).")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Sets needs_more_exploration=true when the plan is actually complete ŌĆö the loop continues iterating instead of pausing for user review, burning tokens on redundant exploration before eventually re-emitting the same plan. Defaults to false for a reason.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Sprinkles `> [!REVIEW REQUIRED]` callouts throughout individual tasks instead of consolidating into a single top-of-plan zone ŌĆö visual noise, defeats the scan-once UX. ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Stitch mode: the new content is glued onto whatever plan text was already saved. Use only in response to a system truncation nudge, or when deliberately adding sections after a needs_more_exploration=true draft.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("The actual proposal ŌĆö a markdown document the user reads in the plan viewer. Headings, code blocks, file paths, callouts at the top for things that need user input. The whole plan goes in this one field.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("The full implementation plan as a markdown document. ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("The plan is a single markdown blob in `response`; structured fields (phases, tasks, file lists) are convention-only ŌĆö the LLM has to manually hold the `## Phase N` / `### Task N` shape, and a malformed plan still renders but loses the inline-comment alignment.")

ArgumentListWrapping:PlanModeRespondTool.kt$PlanModeRespondTool$("Tries to use edit_file or create_file to write the plan to disk while in plan mode ŌĆö blocked twice (schema filter strips write tools; AgentLoop's WRITE_TOOLS guard rejects cached calls). ArgumentListWrapping:ProjectStructureTool.kt$ProjectStructureTool$("Adds or updates a module-to-module dependency in IntelliJ's project model via ModuleRootModificationUtil.updateModel + WriteCommandAction. Requires requestApproval before writing.")

All paths reflective so :agent compiles against IDEs without the Maven plugin. For Gradle roots: ExternalSystemUtil.refreshProject (reload mode only). ArgumentListWrapping:ReadFileTool.kt$ReadFileTool$("No directory listing ŌĆö explicitly errors if `path` is a directory. Surprising to new users, but glob_files covers that case.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("A short reason ŌĆö appears in the chat result and the audit log so the user (and you, on review) know why the agent threw away its own edit.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Bug fixed: PathValidator.resolveAndValidateForWrite added (was naive startsWith('/') check); VFS refresh + FileDocumentManager.reloadFromDisk added after successful checkout. Surfaced by Phase 5 tool-docs swarm Batch 3.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Doesn't preserve unsaved buffer changes ŌĆö if the user has the file open with unsaved edits, `git checkout` restores the committed content on disk and `FileDocumentManager.reloadFromDisk` discards those unsaved edits from the editor buffer too.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Embedded into the success summary (`Reverted file <path>: <description>`) and the long-form result content.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Expects revert to undo IDE-side state ŌĆö open editor tabs, breakpoints, run-config edits, or LocalHistory entries. It only touches the on-disk file via git; nothing else is rolled back.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Forgets the required `description` parameter and gets a missing-param error before any git work runs.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("If a future destructive-git-policy meta-tool (CLAUDE.md ŌåÆ 'Revert Architecture') lands, revert_file is a natural action of that tool rather than a standalone ŌĆö same pattern as `runtime_exec`/`debug_step` consolidating related verbs behind one schema.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Like `git checkout` for one file, but called from inside the IDE ŌĆö wipes whatever the agent just did to a file and snaps it back to whatever git last remembered. Other files are left alone. If the file was never committed, there's nothing to go back to.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Passes a path outside the project root expecting it to succeed ŌĆö PathValidator rejects paths outside the project root and `{agentDir}/memory/` before git is ever invoked.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Path is canonicalised and validated against the project root via PathValidator.resolveAndValidateForWrite, then passed to `git checkout -- <canonicalPath>` running with the project root as cwd.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Path to the file to revert (absolute or relative to the project root). The file must be tracked by git.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Revert source is git HEAD only ŌĆö no LocalHistory, no per-session backup, no in-memory undo stack. If a file was never committed, the revert is a no-op or an error.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Single file at a time ŌĆö no glob, no list, no bulk revert. Reverting 12 bad edits costs 12 tool calls.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Single-file revert via raw `git checkout -- <path>` shelled out from `ProcessBuilder` against the project root ŌĆö restores the file to its committed HEAD state. Path validated by PathValidator.resolveAndValidateForWrite before git is invoked. After a successful checkout, refreshes VFS (LocalFileSystem.refreshAndFindFileByPath + vf.refresh) and reloads any cached Document (FileDocumentManager.reloadFromDisk) so open editor tabs and subsequent read_file calls see the reverted content immediately. Does NOT use IDE LocalHistory or file backups; uncommitted-but-not-yet-modified files (no diff vs HEAD) are no-ops.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("The `description` parameter is required and embedded into the audit trail / user-visible summary ŌĆö unusual for write tools (edit_file/create_file have no analogous required justification). Worth keeping as a model for future destructive tools.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Tries to revert a freshly-created file that was never committed ŌĆö `git checkout` either no-ops (file is untracked) or errors, and the agent loops trying again instead of using `run_command rm <path>`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("Which file to undo edits on ŌĆö same path you'd type at the terminal. Can be relative (`src/Foo.kt`) or absolute (`/Users/me/proj/src/Foo.kt`).")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("changelist_shelve", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Also a 'save state' tool ŌĆö shelves edits onto an IntelliJ changelist instead of discarding them. Use shelve when you might want the changes back; revert_file is for throwing them away.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("edit_file", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "The inverse ŌĆö edit_file is what produces the change that revert_file undoes.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("file must be tracked by git ŌĆö untracked files produce a non-zero exit and a 'Revert failed' error")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("path must be inside the project root or `{agentDir}/memory/` ŌĆö traversal outside the project is rejected by PathValidator before git is invoked")
ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("read_file", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Verify the revert worked by reading the file again ŌĆö confirms the on-disk content matches HEAD.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RevertFileTool.kt$RevertFileTool$("run_command", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "The fallback `git checkout -- <path>` does the same thing, but is approval-gated per-invocation rather than session-approvable, and routes through the broader command-safety analyzer.")
