ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Calls run_inspections immediately after edit_file and interprets problems that existed before the edit as regressions. The tool always reflects the current on-disk state; it does not diff against a baseline. The LLM should compare the problem list to what it knows was there before the edit, not assume all output is new.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Calls run_inspections on a project with a large inspection profile and expects instantaneous results. The tool iterates EVERY enabled LocalInspectionToolWrapper in the profile and runs a PSI visitor for each ŌĆö on large files with many enabled inspections this can take several seconds. If latency is a concern, use `severity=ERROR` to limit the walk to error-level inspections only, or use `diagnostics` for a faster single-pass compilation check.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Column numbers are -1 in DiagnosticEntry ŌĆö ProblemDescriptor does not expose column through the current walk (Phase 7 follow-up). Line numbers are 1-based and correct.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Depends on indexing being complete ŌĆö returns 'IDE is still indexing. Try again shortly.' (isError=true) during DumbService periods. Retry after a delay or do other non-PSI work first.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Does NOT cover compilation errors (unresolved references, type mismatches) ŌĆö those are the compiler front-end's domain and belong to `diagnostics`. run_inspections and diagnostics are complementary, not equivalent.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Expects run_inspections to catch all compilation errors. It does not ŌĆö the tool runs LocalInspectionTools (style, lint, quality inspections) not the compiler front-end. Compilation errors (unresolved references, type mismatches) belong to `diagnostics`. run_inspections is for quality-of-code issues beyond what the compiler checks.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("File-scoped IntelliJ inspection sweep via the active project inspection profile ŌĆö iterates LocalInspectionToolWrapper entries gated by profile.isToolEnabled(), runs each tool's buildVisitor+PsiRecursiveElementWalkingVisitor walk under ReadAction.nonBlocking().inSmartMode(), collects ProblemDescriptors, applies an optional minimum-severity filter, builds structured DiagnosticEntry list (head-20 prose preview inline, full JSON spills to disk above 30K via ToolOutputSpiller), and returns isError=false even when problems are found.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Inspection profile gating via profile.isToolEnabled() means results vary across projects depending on the user's active profile. A problem visible in one project may be absent in another if the inspection is disabled in that profile.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Like running the IntelliJ 'Inspect Code' command on a single file and getting back a list of every warning and error the IDE can see ŌĆö unused variables, null-safety issues, Spring misconfigurations, performance hints, deprecations ŌĆö all scoped to the file you're working on, without launching a full build.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Only problems whose normalised severity ordinal is <= the requested level are included in the result list. The PSI walk still runs for all enabled inspections; filtering is applied to the collected ProblemDescriptors.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Passes `severity=INFO` expecting only informational hints ŌĆö INFO also includes WARNING and ERROR results because the filter is a minimum threshold (ordinal-based). 'ERROR' returns only errors; 'WARNING' returns warnings and errors; 'INFO' returns everything. The parameter name is misleading when read as a level filter rather than a floor.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Path is canonicalised and validated by PathValidator, the VFS entry is resolved, PSI is built, and the full LocalInspectionToolWrapper iteration runs under inSmartMode.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Reads `isError=false` as 'no problems found'. WRONG ŌĆö `isError=false` means the tool executed successfully; the problem list IS the payload. A file with 50 inspection warnings and a clean file both return `isError=false`. Only path validation failures, DumbService-blocked indexing, file-not-found, PSI parse failure, PSI invalidation mid-walk, and uncaught exceptions return `isError=true`. The LLM must read the content ŌĆö 'No inspection problems found in X' vs 'N problem(s) in X:' ŌĆö to determine whether a fix is needed. This is the shared F6 invariant across the diagnostics tool family (diagnostics, run_inspections, list_quickfixes, problem_view).")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Runs every enabled LocalInspectionToolWrapper in the profile sequentially ŌĆö on files with many enabled inspections (300+) and a complex PSI tree the walk can take several seconds. The COMMAND output cap (100K) mitigates token cost, but latency is real for large files.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Some inspections silently throw exceptions on certain file types and are skipped ŌĆö the tool catches and suppresses per-inspection exceptions. This means the result list may omit problems from inspections that crash on the specific file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("The floor for which severity levels are included in the output. Think of it as a radio dial: ERROR returns only the most critical issues; WARNING returns warnings and errors; INFO returns everything the IDE can see.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("Which file to inspect ŌĆö relative to the project root or absolute. Same path semantics as read_file and diagnostics.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("\n[Read with read_file or search_code for inspection matching, filtering by severity/file/inspection]")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("diagnostics", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use diagnostics for fast per-file compilation checks (type errors, unresolved references) ŌĆö it is faster and language-provider-based. Use run_inspections when you need the full inspection profile sweep (unused code, style, performance, Spring misconfig) that goes beyond what the compiler checks.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("file must already exist in the VFS ŌĆö unsaved scratch paths are not visible to findFileByIoFile")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("isError=false on problems-found is shared across the diagnostics tool family (diagnostics T5, run_inspections T2, list_quickfixes T3, problem_view T4). This invariant is documented in agent/CLAUDE.md ('ToolResult.isError semantics') and each tool's execute() KDoc. A dedicated llmMistake entry above and the F6 KDoc block on execute() reinforce it. Any future diagnostics-family tool must follow the same contract.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("list_quickfixes", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "run_inspections surfaces problems with the inspection short name and available fix names already included in the output. For a specific line, list_quickfixes gives the full Alt+Enter menu ŌĆö the natural next step when the LLM wants to apply a suggested fix.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("must point inside the project root or another allow-listed path root ŌĆö traversal (../../etc/passwd) is rejected before any IDE call")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("problem_view", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "problem_view reads the IDE's existing Problems tool-window state without re-running inspections ŌĆö faster but reflects what the IDE has already computed. Use run_inspections to force a fresh sweep; use problem_view to read cached results.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RunInspectionsTool.kt$RunInspectionsTool$("profile.isToolEnabled() is intentionally used instead of isEnabledByDefault ŌĆö this ensures the tool sweep matches what the user sees in the IDE editor. ListQuickFixesTool uses the same gating for the same reason.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("'What run configurations exist in this project?' ŌĆö same content as the run-config dropdown next to the green Run arrow.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("After finishing with an agent-created config (e.g. a one-shot JUnit single-method config used during a debug session) or to clean up leftover configs from a previous session.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Before create/modify/delete to discover existing config names; to find the currently selected configuration; to filter to a specific type (e.g. all Spring Boot configs).")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Brief description of what this action does and why (shown to user in approval dialog) ŌĆö for create_run_config, modify_run_config, delete_run_config")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Builds a brand-new Run/Debug configuration in the IDE. The LLM picks the type (application, Spring Boot, JUnit, Gradle, remote debug) and supplies the relevant fields; the tool wires them in. The new config shows up in the Run dropdown with an [Agent] prefix so it's clearly distinguishable.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("CRUD over IntelliJ's RunManager: list/create/modify/delete RunConfiguration entries across application, spring_boot, junit, gradle, and remote_debug factories. Create/modify use a mix of typed setters (ApplicationConfiguration) and reflection-based setters (Spring Boot, JUnit persistent data fields, Gradle, remote debug PORT/HOST/SERVER_MODE) so the tool can drive config types whose APIs aren't on the compile-time classpath. Per-property failures are collected and reported alongside the success result; delete is gated to [Agent]-prefixed configs only.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Configuration name ŌĆö for create_run_config (auto-prefixed with [Agent]), modify_run_config, delete_run_config")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("ConfigurationFactory not resolvable (plugin missing)", "Returns 'Could not resolve configuration factory for type '<type>'. The required plugin may not be installed.' with isError=true.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Creates a new RunConfiguration via the type's ConfigurationFactory (resolved by reflection for spring_boot, junit, gradle, remote_debug ŌĆö only application is direct). The created config name is auto-prefixed with '[Agent] '. Per-type setters apply: ApplicationConfiguration uses typed setters; Spring Boot/Gradle/JUnit use reflection (setMainClassName/setVMParameters/setProgramParameters/setActiveProfiles/JUnit persistent data TEST_OBJECT+MAIN_CLASS_NAME+METHOD_NAME); remote_debug sets PORT/HOST/SERVER_MODE fields. Module is resolved via ModuleManager.findModuleByName. Failures per property are collected and surfaced as warnings in the result rather than aborting the create.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Deletes a run configuration the agent created. Refuses to delete anything that doesn't have an [Agent] prefix so user-owned configs are safe.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Environment variables as key-value pairs ŌĆö for create_run_config, modify_run_config. In modify_run_config, these are MERGED with existing env vars by default (use replace_env_vars=true for full replacement)")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Environment variables to set on the launched process, as a JSON object of string keys to string values.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Error deleting run configuration: ${e.message}", "Error deleting config", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Error listing run configurations: ${e.message}", "Error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Error modifying run configuration: ${e.message}", "Error modifying config", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Fully qualified main class (required for application/spring_boot) ŌĆö for create_run_config")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("If true, replace ALL existing env vars instead of merging ŌĆö for modify_run_config only (default: false)")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Lets the LLM manage the IDE's saved Run/Debug configurations the way a user does via Edit Configurations. Creates a new entry, tweaks env vars or VM options on an existing one, or removes a config the agent created earlier. Agent-created configs are prefixed [Agent] so the tool can never delete a user-owned configuration.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Lists RunManager.allSettings, optionally filtered by type id/displayName substring. Marks the currently selected configuration with [SELECTED] and extracts main class, module, VM options, and redacted env-var keys via reflection.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Mutates an existing RunConfiguration's env_vars / vm_options / program_args / working_dir / active_profiles in place. env_vars merge with existing by default (read via getEnvs reflection or typed accessor, then putAll new); replace_env_vars=true replaces entirely. Per-property failures are collected and surfaced. Requires at least one of the modifiable fields. Existing config may be user-owned (not [Agent]-prefixed) ŌĆö the tool does not gate modify by prefix.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Only configs whose type id or display name contains the filter token are returned.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Parameter 'main_class' is required for type '$configType'", "Error: missing main_class", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Parameter 'test_class' is required for type 'junit'", "Error: missing test_class", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Removes a configuration from RunManager via removeConfiguration. Gated to names starting with '[Agent]' ŌĆö this is a hard safety constraint to protect user-created configurations from accidental deletion.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Required when type is application or spring_boot ŌĆö create_run_config returns an error in those cases.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Resolved via ModuleManager.findModuleByName; failure lists available module names.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Returns 'Created run configuration '[Agent] <name>'' with Type, Main class / Test class / Module / VM options / Program args / Env var keys / Active profiles / Debug port as applicable. If some properties failed to apply, a WARNING block lists them but the config is still created.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Returns 'Modified configuration '<name>'' with Changes applied summary and the new field values. Warnings listed if any per-property reflection failed.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Returns 'Run Configurations (N total):' followed by per-config blocks (name + [SELECTED] marker, Type, optional Main class, Module, VM options, Env vars with redacted values).")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Set via setVMParameters / setVmParameters / setVmOptions (whichever the config type exposes).")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Spring Boot active profiles, comma-separated ŌĆö for create_run_config, modify_run_config")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Surfaced in the approval gate so the user knows why the agent is creating this config.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("To adjust an existing config ŌĆö turn on a profile, add an env var, raise the heap ŌĆö without losing the rest of the config's state.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("To set up a launch configuration the project doesn't have yet ŌĆö e.g. a Spring Boot run with custom profiles, a JUnit single-test config, a remote-debug attach config ŌĆö before launching it via runtime_exec.run_config.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("Tweaks an existing run configuration without recreating it. Add an env var, change VM options, switch profiles. Env vars default to merging with what's there (so the LLM doesn't accidentally erase the user's settings); pass replace_env_vars=true to overwrite the whole set.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeConfigTool.kt$RuntimeConfigTool$("at least one of env_vars, vm_options, program_args, working_dir, active_profiles must be provided")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$( name = processName, type = "Running", status = "Active", pid = extractPid(handler) )

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("'How did the tests do?' ŌĆö same data as the test results pane in IntelliJ, structured for the LLM. The LLM picks this over parsing console output because the tree gives it pass/fail/error/skipped counts plus per-test detail.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("'Stop the running app cleanly, force-kill if it doesn't respond.' Lets the LLM tear down what it (or the user) launched, without the user having to click the IDE Stop button.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("'What's alive in the IDE right now?' ŌĆö like glancing at the Run tool window to see which tabs have green play dots.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("5 actions consolidated into one tool keeps the schema lean while exposing the full IntelliJ run/debug surface area. run_config's readiness pipeline and idempotent stop-then-launch are irreplaceable; the observation actions are the only path to descriptor-anchored data without re-implementing IntelliJ's RunContentManager. Action enum + targeted state machine make this the highest-leverage tool in the runtime category.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("After done with a server it launched via run_config; before relaunching when the LLM wants explicit ordering rather than the idempotent path; when get_running_processes shows a stale process the LLM doesn't need anymore.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("After java_runtime_exec.run_tests / python_runtime_exec.run_tests / coverage.run_with_coverage finish, when the LLM needs structured pass/fail counts or wants to filter to FAILED only.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("After run_config to confirm the app actually started cleanly; after a test run to read uncategorized stdout/stderr; to follow up when readiness detection times out and the LLM needs to see what happened.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("After service-readiness, the tool subscribes to XDebugSessionListener.sessionPaused and waits up to ready_timeout_seconds for the first pause.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("As a cheap state-check before deciding whether to launch a new instance, before calling stop_run_config, or to discover sessions the user started outside the agent (gutter Run button, run-config dropdown).")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Attempt port discovery via log patterns and lsof/ss/netstat (default true) ŌĆö for run_config")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Calls `get_test_results` on a non-test config ŌĆö gets `No test data` because `findTestRoot` returns null. The action description doesn't gate on config type because the tool can't tell from the descriptor alone whether it's a test run.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Calls `run_config` twice without realizing the first call is idempotent ŌĆö the first instance is stopped and replaced. Mostly harmless but the LLM is sometimes surprised when the second `READY` payload reports a different PID.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Caps the readiness wait at this many seconds; on timeout, returns TIMEOUT_WAITING_FOR_READY with the last lines of output.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Cheapest action in the tool. Often the difference between one tool call and three (LLM blindly calls run_config which then has to stop-and-relaunch).", VerdictSeverity.NORMAL)

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Confuses `run_config` with `run_command` ŌĆö uses run_command to launch a server (`gradle bootRun &`), gets no readiness detection, no port info, and no IDE integration. The right move is run_config with the existing run configuration name.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Custom regex to match readiness in stdout (required when readiness_strategy=explicit_pattern) ŌĆö for run_config")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Defaults to true; set false if you don't care or the OS commands cost more than they're worth.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Descriptor with that name (exact or substring) is selected; live descriptors preferred over terminated ones.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Detach-on-ready is what makes 'launch a server, then test it' workflows actually work. Without it, every server dies on session reset.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Detach-on-ready transfers ownership to the IDE, but the IDE doesn't surface 'this was started by the agent' ŌĆö the user can't tell which Run tabs are agent-started without context. Mostly cosmetic.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Empty-suite handling and structured tree access aren't recoverable via run_command parsing. The interpretTestRoot canonicalization is shared with java_runtime_exec and coverage, so the bugfix benefits all test paths.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Enumerates active run+debug sessions via ExecutionManager.getRunningProcesses() and XDebuggerManager.debugSessions, returning name, type (Running|Debug), status, and PID where extractable.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Error getting run output: ${e.message}", "Error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Error getting test results: ${e.message}", "Error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Error: invalid regex pattern '${it}': ${e.message}", "Error", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Expects `readiness_strategy=auto` to work for non-Spring/non-HTTP apps ŌĆö gets the idle_stdout heuristic, which fires after 2000ms of stdout silence and frequently fires too early on apps that pause output during startup. Fix: pass `readiness_strategy=explicit_pattern` with a known log line.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("First descriptor with test results is used; if none, the first running descriptor is tried.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Full URL to GET for readiness. The user owns correctness ŌĆö no path-construction or port-discovery wrapping.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Full URL to probe for readiness (e.g. http://localhost:8080/health). Overrides auto-detection when readiness_strategy=http_probe ŌĆö for run_config")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("If http_probe is in effect, URL is composed from OS-discovered port + Spring actuator paths.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("If true, the tool waits for the readiness pipeline before returning READY/DEBUG; on success, refs are nulled to detach the process.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("In debug mode only: should the tool also wait for the first breakpoint hit before returning?")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Irreplaceable. Every alternative loses readiness, ports, idempotency, or all three. The 5-stage state machine and 17-category error taxonomy together represent the most useful concentrated capability in the agent.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("LLM learns whether 'my-spring-boot-app' is already running before deciding between run_config and get_run_output.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Launches an existing run configuration with idempotent stop-then-launch (graceful ŌåÆ force kill of any existing instance), full readiness detection (http_probe / log_pattern / idle_stdout / explicit_pattern / process_started, auto-selected by config type), OS-only port discovery (lsof / ss / netstat), and detach-on-ready so the process survives across agent sessions. Drives a 5-stage state machine and emits results from a 17-category error taxonomy.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("No run session found matching '$configName'.$availableMsg", "Not found", 30, isError = true)

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Note: ${match.others.size + 1} sessions matched '$configName'. " + "Selected the ${if (match.pickedLive) "live" else "most recent"} one. Other matches: $othersDesc")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Number of lines to return from the end (default: 200, max: 1000) ŌĆö for get_run_output")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Only matching lines are returned, then last_n_lines is applied to the filtered set.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Only path to console buffers for sessions the user started outside the agent. The tail+filter combination keeps token cost manageable.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Only return lines matching this regex ŌĆö useful for fishing out errors or specific log lines.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Overall process timeout in seconds (default 600, only when wait_for_finish=true) ŌĆö for run_config")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Pick how the tool decides 'ready'. `auto` does the right thing for Spring Boot and most JVM apps; the others are escape hatches.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Port discovery is the most consequential design decision. Static port parsing was deliberately removed in favor of OS-only discovery ŌĆö the tool reports nothing rather than reporting a wrong port. The Spring log banners are kept ONLY as readiness signals; the matched port number is intentionally discarded.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Port discovery requires lsof / ss / netstat to be available. In stripped containers (Alpine without lsof and ss) port info is silently absent ŌĆö result has no Listening ports field.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Press the green Run arrow on a saved run configuration, but with a brain on top: it stops any duplicate instance first, waits until the app is actually serving traffic (not just 'JVM started'), tells you which port it bound, and lets the app keep running after the conversation ends. The single most useful action in the tool.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Process runs to completion; returns 'Process RunMigration finished. Exit code: 0' with output tail.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Readiness detection has language and framework limits. http_probe wants a Spring config or explicit ready_url; log_pattern wants Spring banners or explicit_pattern; idle_stdout fires on 2000ms silence and can fire too early on apps that pause output during startup. Non-Spring HTTP services need explicit ready_url.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Reads a port number out of `Tomcat started on port(s): 8080` in `get_run_output` and assumes it's authoritative ŌĆö it isn't. The `Listening ports:` field on the run_config result is the only authoritative port source. Run-config overrides via VM options / env vars / profiles can shift the bound port away from the static log-banner number.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Reads structured test results from a recently-completed test run via TestConsoleUtils.findTestRoot + interpretTestRoot. Empty suites return NO_TESTS_FOUND (not PASSED). Failed runs propagate isError=true. Waits up to MAX_PROCESS_WAIT_SECONDS (600s) for process termination, TEST_TREE_FINALIZE_TIMEOUT_MS (10s) for the test tree to finalize.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Reads the Console tab of a Run/Debug session ŌĆö what the user would see if they clicked on that tab. Useful for checking startup logs, error messages, or fishing a port number out of a startup banner.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Reads the console buffer of a RunContentDescriptor matched by name (exact, then case-insensitive substring). Prefers live descriptors over terminated ones via selectDescriptorByName. Returns last N lines (default 200, max 1000), optionally regex-filtered.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Regex matched against the stdout buffer. The first match flips readiness to true.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Resolved against RunManager ŌĆö exact name first, then case-sensitive unique substring.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Returns 'Stopped <N> process(es) matching <name>.' Process handler refs and descriptor are cleaned up via the per-launch RunInvocation.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Returns DEBUG with session name, PID, ports, ready signal, and paused_at: <file>:<line>. LLM can now drive the session via debug_step / debug_inspect.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Returns READY with PID, Listening ports (e.g. {8080, 9090}), and 'HTTP probe 200 OK: http://localhost:8080/actuator/health'. App keeps running after this tool returns; LLM can now curl endpoints.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Returns a header (Console Output, Launch Mode, Status, optional Note about other matched sessions, optional Filter) followed by '---' and numbered lines. Output >30K is auto-spilled to disk via ToolOutputSpiller and a preview is returned.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Returns a multi-line block beginning with READY (run mode) or DEBUG (debug mode), with Config name, Type, Mode, PID, optional Listening ports, optional Ready signal, optional Status, and optional pre-launch note when an existing instance was stopped before relaunch. The process is detached from the agent session ŌĆö it survives session resets.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Returns a multi-line list ŌĆö one block per session ŌĆö with name, Type (Running|Debug), Status (Active|Paused at breakpoint), and optional PID. Empty list returns 'No active run/debug sessions.'")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Returns counts (passed/failed/errored/skipped) plus per-test detail when status_filter narrows the set. Empty suites return NO_TESTS_FOUND with isError=true (not PASSED ŌĆö historical bugfix). Output >30K is auto-spilled to disk.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Returns the last 100 lines of the console ŌĆö enough to see startup banner + recent activity.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Run normally, or under the debugger? `coverage` and `profile` are reserved but currently rejected (use coverage.run_with_coverage instead).")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Run session '${descriptor.displayName}' found but console output is empty.", "Empty output", 10)
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Runs lsof / ss / netstat by PID after readiness; `Listening TCP port(s):` is added to the result on success.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Seconds to wait for graceful shutdown before force-killing (default 10) ŌĆö for stop_run_config")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Sends SIGTERM, waits 10s, force-kills if needed; returns 'Stopped 1 process(es) matching MySpringApp.'")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Set true for short-lived processes (CLI tools, batch jobs) that should run to completion. Mutually exclusive in spirit with wait_for_ready, though both can be set (wait_for_ready is checked first).")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Sets `readiness_strategy=http_probe` on a non-Spring app without providing `ready_url` ŌĆö gets `READINESS_DETECTION_FAILED` because http_probe needs either a Spring config (for actuator paths) or an explicit URL.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Should the tool block until the app is actually serving traffic, or return as soon as it's launched?")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Single-tool dispatcher for IntelliJ run/debug surfaces: 3 observation actions (get_running_processes, get_run_output, get_test_results) anchored on RunContentManager + ExecutionConsole, and 2 launch actions (run_config, stop_run_config) anchored on ProgramRunnerUtil + ExecutionEnvironment. run_config drives a 5-stage state machine ŌĆö pre-launch validation, idempotent stop-then-launch, ProgramRunner.Callback launch, readiness pipeline (http_probe / log_pattern / idle_stdout / explicit_pattern / process_started), and detach-on-ready ŌĆö with 17 distinct error categories and OS-only port discovery (lsof / ss / netstat).")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Stopping a launched server cleanly is the natural pair to run_config. The shared StopOutcome state machine eliminates duplication and keeps idempotency contract honest.", VerdictSeverity.STRONG)

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Stops processes whose RunContentDescriptor.displayName matches config_name (case-insensitive substring or handler.toString contains). Graceful destroyProcess ŌåÆ poll ŌåÆ force destroyProcess ŌåÆ poll. Reuses the same StopOutcome state machine that run_config uses for its idempotent pre-launch stop.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("That descriptor is selected (with the same exact-then-substring matching as get_run_output).")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("The 17 error categories are documented but not enumerated in the schema. The LLM has to read the message prefix and remember to branch on it ŌĆö easy to miss DUMB_MODE vs PROCESS_START_FAILED.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("The agent's run/debug toolbar buttons, but scriptable. The LLM uses this to launch a Spring Boot app, wait until it's actually serving traffic, learn which port it bound, then keep working ŌĆö and to read the console of any process the IDE knows about, including ones the user started by clicking the green Run arrow.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("The launch / observe split mirrors IntelliJ's underlying API split (RunContentManager + ExecutionConsole vs ProgramRunnerUtil + ExecutionEnvironment). The actions can't be split into separate tools without duplicating the descriptor-resolution code in five places.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("To launch a Spring Boot app or other long-running service the user has a saved run configuration for, then immediately query its endpoints. Also for `mode=debug` to drive a debug session via debug_step / debug_inspect.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Treats `STOP_FAILED` from the idempotent pre-launch stop as a retry-able error ŌĆö it isn't. A force-kill that didn't kill is almost always a kernel- or OS-level problem; escalate to the user.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Tries `mode=debug` on a config that wasn't designed as a debug config (e.g. a Maven goal) ŌĆö this works but gives unexpected results because `wait_for_pause` only fires when an existing breakpoint is hit. Set breakpoints via `debug_breakpoints` first, or just use `mode=run`.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Try to learn which TCP ports the process bound. OS-only ŌĆö never derived from static config.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Used verbatim for the HTTP probe, bypassing OS port discovery and Spring actuator-path resolution.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("Which session to stop ŌĆö matched by descriptor display name (case-insensitive substring).")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("`get_run_output` and `get_test_results` both pivot on selectDescriptorByName. They could plausibly be merged into a single `get_session_output(format=console|test_tree)` ŌĆö net effect: -1 action, +1 enum param. Marginal at best; current form is clearer.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("`get_test_results` waits up to 600s for a still-running process before giving up. For long-running test suites that's correct, but for the LLM iterating quickly it can stall an iteration boundary.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("`mode=coverage` and `mode=profile` exist in the schema enum but currently return INVALID_CONFIGURATION pointing the LLM elsewhere. Acceptable surface area for the schema; could be removed once a profiler tool exists.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("`run` uses DefaultRunExecutor; `debug` uses DefaultDebugExecutor and observes XDebuggerManager.TOPIC for session attach.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("`stop_run_config` could in principle be merged into `run_config` as a separate `mode=stop` ŌĆö but the parameter shapes diverge sharply (graceful_timeout_seconds, force_on_timeout vs the readiness/launch cluster), so the merge would clutter run_config's parameter list. Keeping them separate preserves param locality.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("a ProgramRunner must be registered for executor + config-type ŌĆö else NO_RUNNER_REGISTERED")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("a test run must exist (typically launched via java_runtime_exec.run_tests, python_runtime_exec.run_tests, or coverage.run_with_coverage)")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("before-run Build task fails", "Returns BEFORE_RUN_FAILED with per-file compile errors (path:line:col ŌĆö message), via formatCompileErrors.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("config must pass checkConfiguration() ŌĆö RuntimeConfigurationError ŌåÆ INVALID_CONFIGURATION; warnings pass")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("coverage", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Coverage uses the same runner machinery. mode=coverage on run_config currently redirects to coverage.run_with_coverage.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("debug_breakpoints", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Set breakpoints first via debug_breakpoints, then run_config(mode=debug, wait_for_pause=true) to launch and stop at the first hit.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("debug_inspect", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "After mode=debug returns DEBUG and the session is paused, debug_inspect reads variables, evaluates expressions, lists frames.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("debug_step", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "After mode=debug returns DEBUG, debug_step navigates the session (step_over/into/out, run_to_cursor, resume, pause, stop).")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("descriptor found but console empty", "Returns '<name> found but console output is empty' (informational).")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("descriptor found but no test data", "Returns '<name> found but no test results available. It may not be a test run.'")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("graceful + force kill both fail", "Returns STOP_FAILED with the count of processes still running. Kernel-level problem; escalate.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("http_probe selected without Spring config or ready_url", "Returns READINESS_DETECTION_FAILED ŌĆö provide ready_url or pick another strategy.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("idempotent stop fails", "Returns STOP_FAILED with the PIDs that didn't die. Do NOT retry ŌĆö escalate.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("internal exception", "Returns 'Error listing processes: <msg>' with isError=true. Rare ŌĆö usually means the IDE itself is in a bad state.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("java_runtime_exec", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Java/Kotlin test running and module compilation. Use java_runtime_exec.run_tests instead of trying to run a JUnit/TestNG config via run_config (which is rejected with INVALID_CONFIGURATION).")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("mode=coverage and mode=profile are enum values that don't actually work ŌĆö they redirect via INVALID_CONFIGURATION. The LLM occasionally picks them and then has to re-pick.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("must match exactly one config (substring matches that match >1 return AMBIGUOUS_MATCH)")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("no ProgramRunner registered", "Returns NO_RUNNER_REGISTERED ŌĆö usually means the required plugin isn't installed.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("no descriptor matches name", "Returns 'No run session found matching <name>' with the available session names listed.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("no descriptor matches", "Returns 'No test run found matching <name>' or 'No test run results available.'")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("process exits before readiness", "Returns EXITED_BEFORE_READY with exit code. Inspect tail before retrying.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("process still running after 600s", "Returns 'Process for <name> is still running after 600s (may still be building/compiling). Try again later.' with isError=true.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("python_runtime_exec", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Python pytest running. Same rationale as java_runtime_exec ŌĆö pytest configs go through python_runtime_exec.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("readiness signal never arrives", "Returns TIMEOUT_WAITING_FOR_READY with the last tail_lines of output.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("run_command", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use run_command for ad-hoc shell invocations or commands not bound to a saved IntelliJ run configuration. Loses readiness, ports, idempotency, IDE integration.")

ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$("runtime_config", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use runtime_config to create/modify run configurations the LLM doesn't have yet, then run them via run_config.")
ArgumentListWrapping:RuntimeExecTool.kt$RuntimeExecTool$(msg, "Process failed before ready", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("After search_code formats its result, lines not matching `grep_pattern` are dropped before the result reaches the LLM. Filtering is applied pre-spill, so it also reduces the size of any spilled file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Auto-injected by AgentLoop because search_code is in `OUTPUT_FILTERABLE_TOOLS`. A second-stage filter applied to the formatted result lines AFTER the main search runs ŌĆö like piping `search_code | grep <grep_pattern>`. Useful for narrowing 'content' mode output to just lines containing a secondary keyword without a follow-up call.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Auto-injected by AgentLoop. When `true`, the full search result is spilled to `{sessionDir}/tool-output/search_code-<epoch>-output.txt` and the LLM gets a head-20 + tail-10 preview plus the file path. Lets the LLM run a broad search once, then re-grep the saved file with read_file/search_code instead of re-running the search.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Backward-compat aliases `query`ŌåÆ`pattern` and `scope`ŌåÆ`path` are accepted but undocumented in the description ŌĆö the LLM never learns they exist, so they only help on replayed sessions or hand-edited tool calls.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Caps the result count ŌĆö like `head -50` on a long match list. The walk early-terminates as soon as this many matches are collected (alphabetical order per directory).")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Compiled to a `kotlin.text.Regex` (with `IGNORE_CASE` if `case_insensitive=true`). Tested against each line via `containsMatchIn` ŌĆö matching is per-line, not multi-line ŌĆö so `\\n` in the pattern will never match. On a `PatternSyntaxException` the pattern is silently re-compiled as `Regex.escape(pattern)` so literals always work.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Error: Search path does not exist: $searchRoot", "Error: path does not exist", ToolResult.ERROR_TOKEN_ESTIMATE, isError = true)
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("File extension filter (e.g., 'kt' for Kotlin files, 'java' for Java files). If not provided, it will search all files.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Forgets that `context_lines` only applies in `output_mode='content'` ŌĆö setting it under `output_mode='files'` or `'count'` is silently dropped (no warning, no error). Wastes a parameter slot in the tool call.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Hard-coded skip-list. There is no way to opt-in to scanning `.gradle/` or `node_modules/` even when the user explicitly wants to (e.g. greppinng a vendored dep for a known string). The LLM has to fall back to `run_command grep` for those cases.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("If true, `User` matches `user` and `USER`. Equivalent to inlining `(?i)` at the start of the pattern.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("If true, save full output to a file and return a preview with the file path. Use for large outputs you may need to search later. Read the file with read_file or search_code.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Invalid regex silently auto-escapes via `Regex.escape(pattern)` ŌĆö no error surfaces to the LLM. A pattern with a typo'd backslash falls back to literal-string match, which can silently return the wrong results.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Like `grep -C N` ŌĆö emit N lines before and N lines after each match line. Helps the LLM see surrounding code without a follow-up read_file. Only meaningful with `output_mode='content'`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Like `grep -rE` in a terminal, but it knows to skip junk folders (.git, build, node_modules), caps each file at 1MB, and gives you a choice of three output styles ŌĆö just paths (cheap discovery), full matching lines (with context, like `grep -C 2`), or per-file counts. Good for 'where in the codebase do we call this method?' or 'which files mention this error string?'.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Lower-cased and compared with `file.extension.lowercase()` ŌĆö only files whose extension matches exactly are searched.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Output is returned inline; if it exceeds 30K chars, ToolOutputSpiller spills it automatically anyway.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Output mode: 'files' (file paths only, default ŌĆö lightweight for discovery), 'content' (matching lines with surrounding context), 'count' (match counts per file).")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Passes a literal string with regex metacharacters un-escaped ŌĆö e.g. `pattern='List<String>'` is interpreted as a regex with a character class. The tool auto-falls back to `Regex.escape()` when the pattern is invalid, but a *valid-but-wrong* regex (e.g. `'a.b.c'` matching `axbyc`) silently does the wrong thing. The LLM should escape literal dots/parens/brackets explicitly.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Per-file 1MB cap means generated SQL dumps, large JSON fixtures, and minified bundles are silently skipped ŌĆö no warning surfaces to the LLM that the file was excluded. A search that 'finds nothing' may actually be missing the file entirely.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Per-line matching only ŌĆö patterns spanning newlines (`function foo\\(\\) {\\n return`) will never match. The regex sees one line at a time. For multi-line patterns, use structural_search or read_file + manual inspection.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Picks `output_mode='content'` for a sweeping pattern with no `max_results` cap, then has the result truncated mid-match by ToolOutputSpiller. Should start with `output_mode='files'` (default ŌĆö lightweight) to bound the search, then `output_mode='content'` with a tighter `path=`/`file_type=` once the candidate set is small.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Recursive regex search over files under a directory using Kotlin's `Regex` engine (java.util.regex). Returns one of three output modes ŌĆö 'files' (default ŌĆö distinct matching paths), 'content' (line-numbered matches with optional before/after context), or 'count' (per-file match tallies). Streams files line-by-line for the no-context path; reads whole files into memory only when context is requested. Hard-coded skip-list (.git, build, node_modules, target, .gradle, .idea, etc.), per-file 1MB cap, binary-extension filter. PathValidator-restricted to the project root or `~/.workflow-orchestrator/`. Auto-falls back to `Regex.escape()` when the pattern is invalid regex, so literal strings 'just work'.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Regex pattern to filter output lines. Only lines matching this pattern are returned. Use when you only need specific information from a potentially large output.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Regex-only ŌĆö no PSI semantics. Hits inside comments, string literals, and unrelated identifiers are all returned equally. For a precise symbol-shaped search prefer find_definition / find_references / structural_search.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Resolved + canonicalized via `PathValidator.resolveAndValidateForRead`. If it's a directory, the walk descends from it (honoring the skip-list and `file_type` filter). If it's a file, only that file is grepped (still subject to the 1MB cap and binary-extension filter). Emitted match paths are relative-to-project for files inside the project, absolute canonical otherwise.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Restrict to one extension ŌĆö like `find . -name '*.kt' -exec grep` but baked in. Massively cuts noise on polyglot projects (e.g. limit to `kt` to skip `.md`/`.html` matches).")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Result is spilled to disk and the LLM receives only a preview (head 20 lines + tail 10 lines + file reference). Subsequent calls can `read_file` the spill or `search_code` it with a tighter pattern.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Search aborts as soon as `matches.size >= maxResults`; result includes a `... (results limited to N)` footer in 'content' mode when the cap clips.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Switches the formatter: 'files' ŌåÆ distinct relative paths joined by newline; 'content' ŌåÆ `path:line: text` per match (or `path:line:> text` for the match line and `path:line: text` for context lines, with `---` separators between match groups); 'count' ŌåÆ `path: N matches` per file.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("The path of a directory or single file to search in (absolute or relative to the project root). Directories are walked recursively; a file path greps the lines of that one file. May also point under ~/.workflow-orchestrator/ (e.g. agent session tool-output dir) to search spilled output. Defaults to project root.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("The regex (or literal string) to search for. Same syntax as `java.util.regex` ŌĆö `\\b` for word boundaries, `(?i)` for inline case-insensitive, `(?m)` for multi-line anchors. If the pattern fails to compile, the tool falls back to a `Regex.escape()`'d literal match instead of erroring ŌĆö so plain strings like `List<String>` work without manual escaping.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("The regular expression pattern to search for. Uses standard regex syntax. Literal strings are also accepted and will be auto-escaped if they contain invalid regex.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Three styles ŌĆö pick by how much detail you actually need. 'files' is just a deduped list of matching paths (cheapest, like `grep -l`). 'content' shows each matching line with optional before/after context (like `grep -nC 2`). 'count' returns one `path: N matches` per file (like `grep -c`).")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Triggers the slower whole-file-into-memory read path (instead of the streaming line-by-line path), so each match is bracketed by N lines of context with `---` separators between match groups.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Uses an unanchored pattern that matches too broadly ŌĆö e.g. `pattern='User'` returns thousands of hits across `UserService`, `userId`, `user.name`, comments, etc. Should add word boundaries (`\\bUser\\b`), file_type filter, or scope via `path=` to the relevant module.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Uses search_code on a directory that requires globally-deterministic results (e.g. enforcing CI-vs-local parity) ŌĆö the walk visits files in alphabetical order per directory but `max_results` early-terminates, so a broad pattern returns a different first-50 across machines if `mtime` or filesystem layout differs.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Uses search_code when find_definition / find_references / find_implementations would be more precise ŌĆö regex sees `class Foo`, `// Foo is`, and `String foo = \"Foo\"` as equivalent hits. PSI-aware tools resolve symbols and skip comments/strings.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Walk order is alphabetical per directory and `max_results` early-terminates, so a broad pattern returns 'first 50 we hit', NOT the most-relevant 50 or the most-recently-modified 50. Differs from glob_files which sorts by mtime DESC.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("Where to grep. A directory walks all matching files recursively; a single file path narrows the grep to that one file (handy for spilled tool-output dumps). Relative paths anchor on the project root; absolute paths must still resolve under the project or under the agent's `~/.workflow-orchestrator/` data dir.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("`.gitignore` is not honored ŌĆö generated files outside the fixed skip-list (e.g. `vendor/`, custom output dirs, `target/generated-sources/` once the path crosses outside `target/`) appear in matches.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("any value other than the three enums silently falls through to 'files' (default branch in `when`) ŌĆö no error, but unexpected if the LLM typos `'contents'` or `'list'`")
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("canonicalPath failed for $basePath; falling back to non-canonical (inside-project relative paths may degrade)", e)
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("compared exact-match against `file.extension` ŌĆö `'kotlin'` will match nothing because the extension is `'kt'`")
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("does NOT replace `pattern` ŌĆö the main search still runs in full; this is post-formatting line filtering only")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("each context line counts toward the default 50K output cap, so wide context (e.g. `context_lines=20`) on a broad pattern can blow the budget fast")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("early-termination is in walk order (alphabetical per directory), NOT relevance order ŌĆö a broad pattern returns 'first 50 we hit' which may not be the most useful 50")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("find_definition", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use instead when you want a symbol's source ŌĆö PSI resolves `class Foo` precisely whereas search_code returns every textual `Foo` including comments, strings, and unrelated identifiers.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("find_references", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use instead when you want callers/usages of a known symbol ŌĆö PSI follows imports, generics, and overloads; search_code can't distinguish `foo.bar()` (method call) from `// foo.bar` (comment).")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("glob_files", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Use when matching on file NAMES rather than file CONTENTS ŌĆö `**/*Test.kt` is a glob_files job, `class.*Test` inside files is a search_code job. Often paired: glob to narrow candidate files, then search_code with `path=` on the result.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("if pointing to a file, the file's extension still has to clear the binary-extension filter (`jar`, `png`, `zip`, etc. are rejected) and the 1MB size cap; otherwise it returns zero matches with no warning")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("invalid regex auto-falls-back to escaped-literal match ŌĆö no error surfaced to the LLM, so a typo'd pattern can silently match the wrong thing")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("matched per-line ŌĆö anchors `^` and `\$` bind to line start/end, not file start/end; `.` does NOT match newlines unless you use `(?s)` (which is moot here since each line is matched separately)")
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("must resolve under the project root or `~/.workflow-orchestrator/` ŌĆö paths outside both are rejected by PathValidator before any read")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("read_file", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "Use after search_code locates the right file ŌĆö `output_mode='files'` to find candidates, then read_file to inspect the full surrounding code.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("single extension only ŌĆö no comma-separated list, no glob (use multiple search_code calls or `path=` narrowing instead)")
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("standard `java.util.regex` syntax ŌĆö `\\b`, `\\s`, `\\d`, lookahead, named groups all supported")

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$("structural_search", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Use for AST-shape queries (e.g. 'all `try { } catch (Exception) { }` blocks') ŌĆö search_code's regex can't see code structure, structural_search can.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$(file, canonicalProjectRoot, regex, fileType, collectContext, contextLines, matches, maxResults)
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeTool.kt$SearchCodeTool$(searchRoot, canonicalProjectRoot, regex, fileType, collectContext, contextLines, matches, maxResults)

ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeToolTest.kt$SearchCodeToolTest$( buildJsonObject { put("pattern", "NullPointerException") put("path", toolOutputDir.absolutePath) put("output_mode", "content") }, project )
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeToolTest.kt$SearchCodeToolTest$( buildJsonObject { put("pattern", "ROUND_TRIP_TOKEN") put("path", toolOutputDir.absolutePath) put("output_mode", "files") }, project )
ArgumentListWrapping:SearchCodeToolTest.kt$SearchCodeToolTest$(readResult.isError, "ReadFileTool should accept the path search_code emitted (got: ${readResult.content})")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Calls diagnostics immediately after edit_file without realising the SyntaxValidator that edit_file ran already covered the same file at write time ŌĆö duplicate work. edit_file's post-write validation is local and fast; diagnostics is the broader sweep. Use diagnostics for the *next* iteration after the structural edit has settled, not as a redundant check on the line you just wrote.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Calls diagnostics on JSON / YAML / Go / TypeScript and gets `Code intelligence not available for {language}` (`isError=false`, token estimate 5). The LLM occasionally retries with the same path expecting a different result. Move on ŌĆö there is no provider; use search_code or run_inspections instead.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Calls diagnostics on a file the IDE hasn't finished indexing yet ŌĆö receives `IDE is still indexing. Try again shortly.` (`isError=true`) and immediately re-fires instead of waiting or doing other work first. The DumbService guard is intentional; retry-with-backoff or pivoting to non-PSI work is the right move.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Column numbers are pinned to -1 in the structured DiagnosticEntry output ŌĆö providers don't expose column through the DiagnosticInfo contract today (Phase 7 follow-up).")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Combined with end_line to filter the diagnostic list. The full file is still parsed (PSI doesn't do partial parses); only the result list is filtered and a `(N issue(s) outside the specified range were excluded)` suffix is added.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Combined with start_line to filter the diagnostic list to lines within the inclusive range.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Depends on indexing being complete ŌĆö returns `IDE is still indexing` (isError=true) during DumbService periods (project import, plugin reload, VCS branch switch). The agent has to back off and retry, which adds latency at session start.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Like the squiggly red and yellow underlines in your IDE ŌĆö but as a structured list the agent can read. Tells the LLM exactly which lines have errors, what's broken, and at what severity, without it having to spawn a Maven or Gradle build.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Lower bound of the line range to scope diagnostics to ŌĆö useful right after an edit when you only care about issues near your change.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Mid-refactor false positives: when the user (or the agent itself) is mid-edit and references are temporarily unresolved, diagnostics will report them ŌĆö matches IDE behaviour but means the LLM should not panic on transient errors right after a large edit.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Not all languages have a provider ŌĆö JSON, YAML, Go, TypeScript, etc. return `Code intelligence not available for {language}`. Coverage is currently Java/Kotlin (JavaKotlinProvider) and Python (PythonProvider via reflection on PythonCore).")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Path is canonicalised, validated against project boundaries via PathValidator, the VFS entry is resolved, PSI is built, and the language provider runs its diagnostics walk inside `inSmartMode`.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Provides only `start_line` (or only `end_line`) expecting partial scoping ŌĆö both are required to filter, and a missing pair causes the tool to fall through to whole-file analysis silently. Easy to miss because there is no explicit error.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Reads `isError=false` as 'no problems found'. WRONG ŌĆö `isError=false` means 'the tool ran successfully'; the problem list IS the payload. Problems-found and clean-file results both return `isError=false`. Only path validation, indexing, missing file, parse failure, PSI invalidation, and uncaught exceptions return `isError=true`. The LLM must inspect the content (`No errors in X.` vs `N issue(s) in X: ...`) to decide success-vs-fix-needed.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Severity classification reflects the inspection profile / language provider's own thresholds, not a stable cross-language taxonomy. An ERROR in Kotlin and an ERROR in Python may have very different blast radii.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Single-file semantic diagnostics via the registered LanguageIntelligenceProvider ŌĆö runs PSI traversal under ReadAction.nonBlocking().inSmartMode() to surface compile errors, unresolved references, type mismatches, and PsiErrorElements; optional start_line/end_line scopes the result to an edit range; full structured DiagnosticEntry list spills to disk above 30K, prose preview (head 20) stays inline.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Upper bound of the line range ŌĆö pair with start_line to scope to a specific edit window.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("Where the file lives ŌĆö relative to the project root or absolute. Same path semantics as read_file.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("\n[Read with read_file or search_code for inspection matching, filtering by severity/file/inspection]")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("`hasQuickFix` is pinned to false in DiagnosticEntry ŌĆö providers don't propagate quick-fix availability (same limitation as ProblemViewTool).")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("edit_file", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "edit_file runs SyntaxValidator on every write ŌĆö diagnostics is the broader follow-up sweep. Typical loop: edit_file ŌåÆ diagnostics (verify nothing else broke) ŌåÆ list_quickfixes if issues found.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("file must already exist in the VFS ŌĆö pass-through `findFileByIoFile` returns null for unsaved/scratch paths")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("find_references", Relationship.COMPOSE_WITH, "When diagnostics reports an unresolved reference, find_references / find_definition help the LLM locate the missing symbol's actual definition.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("isError=false on problems-found is unusual enough that it warrants a llmMistake entry above and a dedicated KDoc block on `execute`. Same contract as RunInspectionsTool, ListQuickFixesTool, ProblemViewTool ŌĆö the problem list IS the payload.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("list_quickfixes", Relationship.COMPLEMENT, "Once diagnostics surfaces a problem, list_quickfixes returns the IDE-suggested fixes for it ŌĆö the natural next step after the LLM reads the diagnostic line.")
ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("must be paired with end_line ŌĆö providing only one is silently ignored and falls back to whole-file analysis")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("must point inside the project root or another allow-listed root ŌĆö traversal (`../etc/passwd`) is rejected before any IDE call")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("problem_view", Relationship.SEE_ALSO, "Reads the IDE's existing Problems tool window state instead of running a fresh analysis ŌĆö overlapping payload but reflects what the IDE has already computed (Wolf flag, persisted issues) rather than re-walking PSI.")

ArgumentListWrapping:SemanticDiagnosticsTool.kt$SemanticDiagnosticsTool$("run_inspections", Relationship.ALTERNATIVE, "Project-wide or scope-wide inspection sweep ŌĆö heavier, slower, but covers IntelliJ inspection profiles diagnostics doesn't see (style, performance, deprecation). Use diagnostics for fast per-file checks, run_inspections for broader audits.")
